  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement account record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the settlement account.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the settlement account verification process.']

  Note: 'Stores the merchant\'s designated bank account(s) for settlement fund disbursement. Captures bank name, account holder name, account type (checking, savings), IBAN, account number (tokenized/masked), routing number, BIC/SWIFT code, currency, settlement frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), settlement delay days, and account verification status. Supports multi-currency and multi-bank settlement configurations per merchant.'
}

Table merchant.processing_limit {
  processing_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the processing limit record.']
  control_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific compliance rule governing this limit.']
  fraud_velocity_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_velocity_control. Business justification: Processing limits are frequently set or tightened in direct response to fraud velocity control breaches. Risk operations teams need to reference the specific velocity control that mandated a limit cha']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_account. Business justification: Processing limits (velocity controls, daily/monthly caps, CNP limits) are configured at the merchant_account level — the operational processing account. Currently processing_limit only FKs to merchant']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant to which this processing limit applies.']
  payment_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.payment_corridor. Business justification: Cross-border processing limits are corridor-specific — a merchant may have different volume caps for USD→EUR vs USD→MXN corridors. This link enables corridor-specific limit enforcement, regulatory com']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Processing limits are set per payment product — a merchant has different transaction and volume limits for card-present vs. BNPL vs. cross-border products. Risk and regulatory decisions require produc']
  underwriting_decision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underwriting decision record that approved the limit.']
  card_not_present_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for card‑not‑present (CNP) transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the processing limit record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value for cross‑border transactions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of monetary limits.']
  daily_transaction_count_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of individual transactions permitted per day.']
  daily_volume_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary volume limit for transactions within a calendar day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the limit becomes enforceable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the limit expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended limits.']
  is_compliant boolean [note: 'True when the limit configuration satisfies all applicable regulatory and internal compliance rules.']
  is_manual_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if transactions breaching the limit trigger a manual review workflow.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether authorized personnel may temporarily override the limit.']
  limit_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description explaining why the limit was created or modified.']
  limit_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the type of limit configuration.']
  limit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for periodic review and possible adjustment of the limit.']
  limit_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the limit configuration – whether set by underwriting, risk model, or manual policy.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the limit, indicating which transaction dimension it governs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_transaction|daily_volume|monthly_tpv|transaction_count|cnp|cross_border|refund — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  monthly_tpv_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative transaction value allowed in a month.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form notes or comments about the limit.']
  processing_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the limit configuration.']
  refund_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total amount that can be refunded within the limit period.']
  risk_score_at_set decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk model score associated with the merchant at the time the limit was established.']
  single_transaction_max_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value allowed for any single transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the processing limit record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking revisions to the limit configuration.']

  Note: 'Defines the transaction processing limits and velocity controls configured for a merchant account. Stores single transaction maximum amount, daily transaction volume cap, monthly TPV (Total Payment Volume) limit, maximum transaction count per day, card-not-present limit, cross-border transaction limit, refund limit, and limit review date. Limits are set during underwriting and adjusted based on risk profile changes.'
}

Table merchant.hierarchy {
  hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each merchant hierarchy relationship record.']
  child_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant that is the subordinate entity in the hierarchy.']
  parent_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant that acts as the parent in the relationship (e.g., PayFac, ISO, corporate parent).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the relationship meets applicable AML, KYC, and PCI‑DSS requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the hierarchy record was initially inserted into the lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date on which the parent‑child relationship starts to apply.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date on which the relationship terminates; null for open‑ended relationships.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child merchant relative to the root of the hierarchy (root = 0).']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the relationship (e.g., PayFac to sub‑merchant, ISO portfolio, corporate parent‑subsidiary, aggregator‑merchant).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the child merchant may belong to only this parent; false if multi‑parenting is allowed.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'True when the child merchant is designated as the primary account within the parent grouping.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the hierarchy relationship was last examined for regulatory or risk compliance.']
  merchant_hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Operational state of the relationship (e.g., active, pending approval, suspended).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional textual comments or business rationale for the hierarchy entry.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑99.99) reflecting the aggregate risk of the parent‑child grouping.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that created or last updated the hierarchy entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gateway|transaction_platform|merchant_mgmt|fraud_platform|digital_wallet|risk_compliance|crm|erp|analytics — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to any field in the hierarchy record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing integer used to manage concurrent updates.']

  Note: 'Models the organizational hierarchy relationships between merchant entities — including PayFac (Payment Facilitator) to sub-merchant relationships, ISO (Independent Sales Organization) portfolios, corporate parent-to-subsidiary chains, and aggregator-to-merchant structures. Captures parent merchant ID, child merchant ID, hierarchy type (PayFac, ISO, corporate, aggregator), effective date, and hierarchy level. Enables consolidated reporting and risk exposure aggregation across merchant groups.'
}

  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Represents a sub-merchant entity operating under a PayFac or aggregator umbrella. Stores sub-merchant name, sub-MID, parent PayFac or aggregator reference, business type, onboarding date, processing status, assigned MCC, transaction volume tier, and sub-merchant-level risk classification. Sub-merchants are distinct from direct merchants in that they are sponsored and managed by a PayFac rather than directly contracted with the acquirer.'
}

Distinct from pricing plan — this is the legal contract record, not the fee configuration.'
}

  compliance_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the compliance fee.']

  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the non‑compliance fee waiver was issued.']

  Note: 'Tracks PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) compliance status for each merchant. Stores compliance level (SAQ A, SAQ B, SAQ D, ROC), current compliance status, last assessment date, next assessment due date, AOC (Attestation of Compliance) reference, ASV scan status, penetration test date, non-compliance fee waiver status, and assigned QSA (Qualified Security Assessor). Mandatory for all card-accepting merchants per card scheme rules.'
}

  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_account. Linking merchant_risk_profile to merchant_']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant to which this risk profile belongs.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_policy. Business justification: Compliance and risk teams must trace which risk_policy version governed reserve requirements, risk tier classification, and monitoring thresholds at the time a merchant_risk_profile was created or upd']
  processing_limit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant\'s processing limit configuration.']
  reserve_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account.   underwriting_decision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underwriting decision record that created the profile.']

  average_ticket_size decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mean value per individual transaction.']
  business_model_risk_classification varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on the merchant\'s business model and industry exposure.']
  chargeback_rate_3m_rolling decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average chargeback rate over the trailing three‑month window.']
  chargeback_rate_current decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total transactions that have resulted in chargebacks in the most recent reporting period.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the merchant complies with applicable regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk profile record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the risk profile becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the risk profile expires or is superseded (nullable).']

  is_manual_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manual risk review is required.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether risk parameters can be overridden by authorized personnel.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know‑Your‑Customer verification status for the merchant.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to any field in the record.']
  last_risk_review_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent risk assessment was performed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form information about the risk profile.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for any manual override of risk settings.']
  refund_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction value refunded to cardholders within the reporting period.']
  reserve_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current monetary amount held in reserve for the merchant.']
  reserve_balance_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the reserve balance amount.']
  reserve_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the merchant\'s transaction volume that must be held as reserve.']
  reserve_requirement_type varchar [note: 'Method used to hold a financial reserve for the merchant (none, rolling, or capped).']
  risk_review_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during the risk review.']
  risk_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the latest risk review was completed.']
  risk_review_trigger varchar [note: 'Event or condition that initiated the latest risk review.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk score derived from underwriting models (0‑100).']
  risk_score_explanation varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of factors influencing the risk score.']
  risk_score_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the risk score (e.g., model, manual).']
  risk_score_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the scoring model used.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk profile.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Overall risk tier assigned to the merchant after underwriting and ongoing assessment.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the merchant.']
  transaction_volume_tier varchar [note: 'Categorization of merchant based on average transaction volume.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk profile.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for the risk profile record.']

  Note: 'Stores the underwriting and ongoing risk assessment profile for a merchant. Captures risk tier (low, medium, high, prohibited), business model risk classification, chargeback rate (current and rolling 3-month), refund rate, fraud rate, reserve requirement type (none, rolling, capped), reserve percentage, reserve balance, last risk review date, and risk review trigger. Owned by the merchant domain as the operational risk configuration record; distinct from the enterprise risk domain\'s scoring models.'
}

Table cardholder.account_holder {
  account_holder_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the account holder (golden record).']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Corporate account holders in B2B payments fintech are registered legal entities. Linking enables KYC/AML regulatory reporting, tax identification reconciliation, and sanctions screening against the le']
  account_holder_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account holder.']
  account_holder_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the holder is an individual consumer (B2C) or a corporate entity (B2B).']
  avs_verified boolean [note: 'Result of Address Verification System check during onboarding.']
  business_category varchar [note: 'Industry classification of business account holder (e.g., retail, services).']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the mailing address.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account holder was closed or deactivated, if applicable.']
  corporate_name varchar [note: 'Legal corporate name for business account holders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account holder record was first created in the system.']
  cvv_last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent CVV verification event.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the individual account holder.']
  dpan varchar [note: 'Device Primary Account Number used for tokenized transactions.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the account holder.']
  failed_login_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of consecutive failed login attempts.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the account holder.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the account holder as provided during onboarding.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date when the business entity was incorporated.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current KYC verification status of the account holder.']
  kyc_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when KYC verification was completed.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last successful login to the platform.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the account holder.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the mailing address.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the mailing address.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Merchant Category Code associated with the account holder (if applicable).']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Country of citizenship or nationality of the account holder.']
  onboarding_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the account holder was onboarded.']
  pan varchar [note: 'Primary Account Number linked to the account holder (masked for security).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the account holder.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the mailing address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications with the account holder.']
  sca_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account holder has completed Strong Customer Authentication compliance.']
  source_of_funds varchar [note: 'Declared source of funds for the account holder.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the mailing address.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the current status (e.g., fraud_suspected, compliance_hold).']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account holder is tax-exempt.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (e.g., SSN, EIN, TIN) for tax reporting.']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PAN has been tokenized.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the account holder record.']

  Note: 'SSOT master record for every consumer and business cardholder identity served by the platform. Captures full legal identity (name, DOB, nationality, tax ID), account holder type (individual B2C vs. corporate B2B), onboarding channel, lifecycle status (active, suspended, closed, dormant), preferred language, and platform-assigned cardholder reference number. This is the root anchor entity for all cardholder sub-entities and the authoritative golden record for cardholder identity across all payment channels.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_profile {
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cardholder profile record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.account_holder. Business justification: cardholder_profile extends the master account_holder; linking provides a single source of truth and allows removal of duplicated personal and KYC fields from the profile.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Links employee cardholder profiles to cost centers for expense allocation and internal reporting.']
  dcc_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.dcc_config. Business justification: Cardholders can be pre-enrolled in DCC programs; the applicable dcc_config governs offer presentation method, margin percentage, and opt-in defaults for that profile. DCC enrollment management and com']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Needed for loyalty and offer management: the primary partner offering programs to the cardholder is identified for personalized marketing and compliance.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: FX fee schedule applied per cardholder for fee calculation reports; treasury uses this to charge correct FX fees based on customer segment.']
  issuing_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.participant. Business justification: Regulatory AML/KYC reporting requires linking each cardholder to their issuing bank’s settlement participant to trace funds and satisfy cross‑border settlement netting.']
  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_profile. Business justification: Cardholder profiles are subject to jurisdiction-specific compliance rules (GDPR in EU, different KYC thresholds by country, cross-border restrictions). Linking cardholder_profile to jurisdiction_profi']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Global card programs issue cardholder profiles under specific legal entities per jurisdiction. Required for GDPR data residency compliance, local regulatory reporting, and multi-entity financial conso']
  preferred_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Needed for offering cardholder‑specific scheme preferences during new card issuance and for compliance reporting on scheme‑wise cardholder demographics.']
  rate_margin_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate_margin_config. Business justification: Custom margin config per cardholder determines FX rate margins; used in pricing engine and compliance reporting.']
  account_lock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder\'s account is currently locked due to security policies.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the cardholder\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  address_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder\'s address has been validated through KYC processes.']
  cardholder_type varchar [note: 'Segment indicating whether the cardholder is an individual consumer, business entity, merchant, or partner.']
  completeness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage (0‑100) reflecting how many optional profile fields are populated.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the most recent consent (e.g., marketing) was recorded.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system or service that initially created the profile record.']
  data_retention_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates if the cardholder has opted out of data retention beyond statutory periods.']
  dpan_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the cardholder\'s device PAN used for tokenization services.']
  email_verified boolean [note: 'True when the primary email address has been confirmed via verification workflow.']
  external_customer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partner systems to reference this cardholder.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score generated by the fraud detection platform for this cardholder.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑declared gender of the cardholder for demographic reporting.']
  is_test_account boolean [note: 'True if the profile is used for internal testing or sandbox environments.']
  kyc_verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the Know‑Your‑Customer verification process for the cardholder.']
  last_modified_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that performed the most recent update.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the cardholder profile.']
  marketing_consent_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder has opted in to receive marketing emails.']
  marketing_consent_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder has opted in to receive marketing SMS messages.']
  pan_linked boolean [note: 'True if a primary account number (PAN) is linked to this profile.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Cardholder\'s chosen channel for receiving marketing and transactional messages.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Language preference for communications and UI.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Main email address used for communication and account notifications.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for SMS alerts and voice contact.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profile record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the profile record.']
  right_to_erasure_requested boolean [note: 'True when the cardholder has submitted a request for data deletion.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from fraud and compliance signals.']
  sca_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current SCA compliance state of the cardholder as required by PSD2.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created or last modified the profile.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone identifier associated with the cardholder.']
  total_failed_auth_attempts varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of consecutive failed login or authentication attempts.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking changes to the profile over time.']

  Note: 'Extended personal and contact profile for an account holder. Stores PII attributes including full legal name, date of birth, gender, primary email, primary phone, preferred communication channel, marketing consent flags, and profile completeness score. Distinct from account_holder (which owns identity and lifecycle) — this entity owns the mutable contact and preference layer that changes independently of identity status. Supports GDPR/CCPA data subject rights management including right-to-erasure flags.'
}

Table cardholder.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the cardholder address record.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Address records belong to a cardholder profile; linking enables address lookup and removes the need for profile‑level address fields.']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the address record.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose.']
  avs_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the address passed Address Verification System (AVS) check.']
  avs_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AVS verification was performed.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or locality of the address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the address became effective for the cardholder.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the address ceased to be effective (null if still active).']
  geocode_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate from geocoding the address.']
  geocode_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate from geocoding the address.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the primary address for the cardholder.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the address (e.g., Home, Office).']
  line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address.']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  line3 varchar [note: 'Third line of the street address (optional).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the address record.']
  verification_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of verification attempts performed for this address.']
  verification_source varchar [note: 'Source of address verification.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification status of the address.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing address records associated with a cardholder. Stores structured address components (street, city, state/province, postal code, country ISO code), address type (billing, shipping, residential, registered business), AVS-verified flag, AVS verification timestamp, geocoding coordinates, and address lifecycle (primary, secondary, historical). Supports AVS (Address Verification System) checks during card-not-present authorization and KYC address validation workflows.'
}

Regulatory compliance and partner-level KYC audit reporting require knowing which ecosystem partner\'s onboarding f']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_model. Business justification: KYC verification scores and risk_tier assignments are computed by specific risk models. Model governance and AML regulatory audits require traceability from each KYC verification record to the scoring']
  primary_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to whom this verification applies.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: KYC verifications are performed to satisfy specific regulatory obligations (CDD under AMLD5, KYC under BSA, EDD under FATF Recommendation 10). Linking each KYC verification to the regulatory_obligatio']
  aml_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of the Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening performed during verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the KYC verification record was first created in the system.']
  ctf_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of the Counter‑Terrorism Financing screening performed during verification.']
  document_hash varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the submitted document to ensure integrity.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of identity document submitted for verification.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the verification is considered expired and must be renewed.']
  is_sca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the verification satisfies Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  issued_at timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the KYC verification was performed.']

  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'True if this verification must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., FinCEN, FCA).']
  reverification_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the cardholder has been re‑verified after the initial verification.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned to the cardholder after KYC evaluation.']
  sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder passed sanctions list screening.']
  score_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the verification score was calculated.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the verification request originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the KYC verification record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the KYC verification.']

  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the verification record.']

  Note: 'KYC (Know Your Customer) verification record capturing the full identity verification lifecycle for a cardholder. Stores verification method (document scan, biometric, database check, eIDV), verification provider, verification status (pending, passed, failed, expired, manual_review), risk tier assigned post-KYC, document types submitted, verification score, expiry date of verification, and regulatory jurisdiction. Supports AML/CTF compliance obligations and onboarding gating logic. Each re-verification creates a new record preserving the full audit trail.'
}

  tamper_detected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document image shows signs of tampering.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the document record for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Individual identity document submitted as part of a KYC verification. Captures document type (passport, national ID, driver\'s license, utility bill, bank statement), issuing country, document number (tokenized/masked), issue date, expiry date, document image reference (vault URI), OCR extraction status, tamper detection flag, and manual review outcome. Linked to a parent kyc_verification record. Supports regulatory document retention requirements under BSA, PSD2, and FATF standards.'
}

  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: A PAN is issued in a specific currency; the currency_pair governs DCC eligibility and cross-border fee application at the card level.   ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: PANs are issued under a specific partner\'s program.   irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. A payments-fintech domain expert expects this']
  issuing_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.participant. Business justification: Each PAN is issued under a specific settlement participant (issuing bank/BIN sponsor). Interchange reporting, scheme compliance, and chargeback processing require knowing which participant issued the ']
  primary_pan_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key linking the PAN to its owning cardholder profile.']

  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening for the PAN.']
  bin varchar [note: 'First six digits of the PAN identifying the issuing bank and card brand.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PAN record meets current PCI‑DSS requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for transactions using this PAN. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|AUD|CAD|CHF|CNY|INR|BRL|... — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the PAN expires and can no longer be used for new authorizations.']

  iin varchar [note: 'First eight digits of the PAN used for routing and issuer identification.']

  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know‑Your‑Customer verification state of the cardholder linked to this PAN.']

  masked_pan varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the PAN (e.g., XXXX‑XXXX‑XXXX‑1234) for human‑readable displays.']
  pan_format varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PAN consists of only numeric characters or includes alphanumeric characters.']
  pan_length varchar [note: 'Number of digits in the PAN (typically 16, 15, or 19).']
  pan_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the PAN within its lifecycle.']
  pan_token varchar [note: 'PCI‑DSS compliant token that represents the PAN; never stores the clear PAN.']
  pan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PAN is a physical PAN (FPAN) or a device‑generated token (DPAN).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the PAN record was initially persisted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the PAN record.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑99.99) indicating fraud likelihood.']
  token_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the PAN token becomes invalid.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for generating and managing the PAN token.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to derive the token from the clear PAN.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of successful transactions processed for this PAN.']

  Note: 'Authoritative PAN (Primary Account Number) registry for cardholders. Stores the tokenized PAN reference (never the raw PAN in clear text per PCI DSS), BIN (Bank Identification Number), IIN (Issuer Identification Number), card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover), PAN type (FPAN physical, DPAN device token), PAN status (active, blocked, expired, replaced), expiry date, and the issuing bank reference. This is the SSOT for PAN-to-cardholder linkage and supports BIN-level routing and interchange qualification.'
}

  payment_credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the payment credential record.']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.card_program. Credential issuance and lif']
  pan_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.pan_record. Business justification: A payment_credential authenticates a specific PAN/DPAN. The credential stores dpan_reference and token_reference as raw string fields, but pan_record is the authoritative PAN registry (storing pan_tok']
  primary_payment_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder who owns this credential.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Payment credentials (EMV chip, tokenized cards, 3DS enrollment) are governed by scheme-specific standards. Scheme determines 3DS version support, tokenization method, and CVV validation rules. Issuers']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to authenticate the credential during a transaction.']
  biometric_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether biometric authentication is enabled for this credential.']
  biometric_type varchar [note: 'Specific biometric modality used (if enabled).']
  contactless_nfc_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'True when NFC/contactless payments are permitted for this credential.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credential record was first created in the system.']
  credential_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label assigned to the credential (e.g., "Primary Card Credential").']
  credential_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the credential record (e.g., Digital Wallet Platform).']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the credential.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Category of the credential indicating the underlying payment instrument.']
  credential_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version number for the credential record.']
  cvv2_validation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether CVV2 validation is required for this credential.']
  cvv_hash_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the hashed CVV/CVC stored securely in the HSM.']
  emv_chip_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip authentication is supported.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the credential is no longer valid.']
  external_credential_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., token service provider) to reference this credential.']
  hsm_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HSM key used to encrypt credential secrets.']
  issuance_date date [note: 'Date when the credential was originally issued.']
  last_rotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the credential secrets were last rotated.']
  otp_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one‑time‑password (OTP) authentication is active.']
  otp_last_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the most recent OTP was generated/sent.']
  pan_linkage_status varchar [note: 'Current relationship status between the credential and its PAN/DPAN.']
  pin_last_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the PIN offset was last updated.']
  pin_offset varchar [note: 'Encrypted PIN offset used for offline PIN verification (PCI‑DSS).']
  sca_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True when the credential meets PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  sca_compliance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when SCA compliance was last verified.']
  three_ds_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'State of Strong Customer Authentication enrollment for this credential.']
  three_ds_method varchar [note: 'Method used for 3‑DS challenge‑response (e.g., OTP, biometric).']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'State of tokenization for the underlying PAN/DPAN.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credential record.']

  Note: 'Authentication credential set associated with a cardholder\'s payment instrument. Stores CVV/CVC hash reference, CVV2 validation flag, PIN offset (for debit/ATM), 3DS enrollment status, 3DS authentication method (OTP, biometric, out-of-band), SCA compliance flag per PSD2, credential version, last rotation timestamp, and HSM key reference identifier. Distinct from pan_record (which owns the PAN identity) — this entity owns the authentication secrets and SCA compliance state. Supports EMV chip credential management and NFC contactless authentication.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_sca_challenge {
  cardholder_sca_challenge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique identifier for the SCA challenge event.']
  auth_3ds_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.auth_3ds_result. Business justification: 3DS authentication outcome reporting requires linking the cardholder-side SCA challenge to the ACS/fraud-side auth_3ds_result for liability shift determination and PSD2 SCA compliance reporting. Domai']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_account. Business justification: SCA challenges under PSD2 are triggered in the context of a specific payment account transaction. While cardholder_sca_challenge already links to cardholder_profile_id (identity) and transaction_id (c']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used for the challenge (e.g., mobile device ID).']
  primary_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who received the challenge.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.request. Business justification: An SCA challenge is triggered by a specific gateway request. PSD2 and 3DS audit requirements mandate end-to-end traceability from the originating payment request through the SCA challenge outcome. The']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: SCA challenges are scheme-specific (Visa Secure vs Mastercard Identity Check), with different 3DS protocol versions, ECI indicators, and exemption rules per scheme. Scheme-level SCA compliance reporti']
  threeds_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.threeds_config. Business justification: Each SCA challenge is executed under a specific 3DS configuration (protocol version, challenge preference, exemption rules, risk threshold). PSD2 SCA compliance audit trails require tracing every chal']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction associated with the SCA challenge.']
  acs_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ACS that generated the challenge.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the challenge attempt for the same transaction.']
  authentication_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cryptographic authentication value returned by the ACS.']
  challenge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the challenge.']
  challenge_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the challenge was issued to the cardholder.']
  challenge_type varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the SCA challenge to the cardholder.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCA challenge record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  eci_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator of the authentication outcome used for liability shift.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why SCA was bypassed, if applicable.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of why the challenge failed, if applicable.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device that initiated the challenge.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the challenge was successfully completed (true) or not (false).']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds between challenge issuance and final response.']
  three_ds_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3‑Domain Secure protocol applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SCA challenge record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string presented during the challenge.']

  Note: 'Record of each Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) challenge event issued to a cardholder under PSD2/3DS protocols. Captures challenge type (OTP SMS, OTP email, biometric, push notification, out-of-band), challenge status (issued, passed, failed, expired, bypassed), 3DS version, ACS (Access Control Server) reference, authentication value (CAVV/AAV), ECI indicator, challenge timestamp, and exemption reason if SCA was bypassed (low-value, TRA, merchant-initiated). Provides the full SCA audit trail required by PSD2 RTS.'
}

  Note: 'Financial account record owned by a cardholder on the platform. Table cardholder.account_status_history {
  account_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each status transition record.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder account whose status changed.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was created in the system.']
  correlation_code varchar [note: 'Identifier to correlate this event across multiple systems.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the new status became effective.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the status transition event occurred.']
  initiating_actor varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the status change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the status change.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the status changed.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human readable description of the reason for status change.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if this record represents a reversal of a previous status change.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that generated the status change event.']
  status_after varchar [note: 'The status of the account after the transition.']
  status_before varchar [note: 'The status of the account prior to the transition.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of all status transitions for a cardholder account. Records each status change event (e.g., active→suspended, suspended→closed), the reason code, initiating actor (system, agent, cardholder self-service), effective timestamp, and reversal flag. Supports regulatory audit trail requirements under SOX, PCI DSS, and CFPB complaint handling. Enables lifecycle analytics and delinquency workflow tracking without mutating the parent cardholder_account record.'
}

Linking segment definitions to the regulatory_obligation that m']

  Note: 'Segmentation classification assigned to a cardholder for product targeting, risk tiering, and pricing differentiation. Stores segment code, segment name, segment tier (mass market, premium, ultra-premium, SMB, corporate), assignment method (rule-based, ML-assigned, manual), effective date, expiry date, and the segment criteria version used. Supports product eligibility gating, MDR/MSF pricing tiers, and personalized offer targeting. A cardholder may have one active segment per segment dimension (spend tier, risk tier, loyalty tier).'
}

Table cardholder.consent {
  consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each consent record.']

  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Optional expiry date/time after which the consent is no longer valid.']

  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string recorded during consent collection.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent text or policy that the cardholder agreed to.']
  withdrawal_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the consent was withdrawn.']
  withdrawal_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device used when consent was withdrawn.']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cardholder withdrew the consent, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Explicit consent and preference record for a cardholder covering data privacy, marketing, and regulatory consent obligations. Captures consent type (GDPR data processing, CCPA sale opt-out, marketing email, marketing SMS, data sharing with partners, biometric data), consent status (granted, withdrawn, pending), consent version, collection channel, IP address at consent time, and withdrawal timestamp. Provides the SSOT for all consent decisions required under GDPR, CCPA, and PSD2 open banking data sharing rules.'
}

  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful verification of the linked account.']

  Note: 'External bank account or financial account linked to a cardholder for funding, ACH pull, A2A transfer, or BNPL repayment purposes. Supports ACH debit authorization, IBAN/BIC for cross-border linked accounts, and open banking account aggregation.'
}

Table cardholder.device {
  device_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying a registered cardholder device.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: A device is registered by a specific cardholder; linking device to cardholder_profile eliminates the need for indirect look‑ups via events and enables direct device‑to‑profile queries.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: Unified device profiling links registered cardholder device to its fraud fingerprint record for accurate risk scoring.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the most recent compliance assessment was performed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance check (e.g., security posture, OS version).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the device record was created in the system.']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name assigned by the cardholder to the device (e.g., \'John’s iPhone\').']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of the device used for digital wallet or authentication.']
  dpan_provisioned boolean [note: 'True if a Device Primary Account Number has been provisioned to the device.']
  fingerprint_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash representing the device fingerprint used for risk scoring.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the device firmware, if applicable.']
  hce_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device can perform Host Card Emulation for tokenized payments.']
  imei varchar [note: 'Unique hardware identifier for mobile devices, used for device tracking and fraud detection.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address observed during the last activity of the device.']
  last_active_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent timestamp when the device was used for a transaction or authentication.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the device’s last known location (if available).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the device’s last known location (if available).']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactured the device (e.g., Apple, Samsung).']
  model varchar [note: 'Model identifier of the device (e.g., iPhone 13, Galaxy S22).']
  nfc_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports Near Field Communication for contactless payments.']
  os_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operating system running on the device (e.g., iOS, Android).']
  os_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the operating system (e.g., 16.4).']
  push_notification_token varchar [note: 'Token used to send push notifications to the device.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the device registration.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was first registered to the cardholder.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the device record.']
  uuid varchar [note: 'Universally unique identifier assigned to the device within the platform.']

  Note: 'Mobile device or hardware token registered by a cardholder for digital wallet provisioning, HCE (Host Card Emulation), NFC payments, or 3DS out-of-band authentication. Stores device type (iOS, Android, wearable, browser), device fingerprint hash, device name, OS version, NFC capability flag, HCE capability flag, device registration status (active, deactivated, suspended), DPAN provisioning status, and last active timestamp. Distinct from wallet domain device records — this entity owns the cardholder-device relationship and authentication eligibility.'
}

Table cardholder.authentication_event {
  authentication_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each authentication event record.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used for authentication.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Audit of authentication providers: each SCA event must record the authentication partner (e.g., 3DS provider) for SLA and dispute analysis.']
  primary_authentication_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder whose authentication was attempted.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Authentication events (3DS, biometric, OTP) are scheme-governed with distinct flows per scheme. Scheme-level authentication success/failure analytics, fraud liability shift reporting, and regulatory s']
  score_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.score_event. Business justification: Fraud step-up authentication decisions and false-positive analysis require linking each authentication event to the fraud score event that evaluated it. Fraud operations teams use this link to tune mo']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the attempt within the current authentication session.']
  authentication_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the authentication request was made.']
  authentication_factor varchar [note: 'Specific factor used in the authentication (e.g., password, OTP, biometric).']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate the cardholder (e.g., password, OTP, biometric).']
  authentication_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the authentication attempt.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authentication event record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of the device (e.g., mobile, desktop, POS terminal).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the authentication attempt occurred.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for authentication failure when outcome is failure or lockout.']
  geolocation_city varchar [note: 'City inferred from the IP address or device location.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address from which the authentication request originated.']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds between request receipt and response.']
  lockout_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder account was locked as a result of this attempt.']
  risk_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of risk indicators (e.g., high_velocity|blacklist|suspicious_location).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk assessment generated by fraud detection engine for this attempt.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authentication event record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string presented during authentication.']

  Note: 'Record of each cardholder authentication attempt across all channels — online banking login, mobile app login, IVR PIN entry, card activation, and password reset. Captures authentication method (password, OTP, biometric fingerprint, biometric face, FIDO2 passkey, knowledge-based), authentication outcome (success, failure, lockout), failure reason, IP address, geolocation, device reference, session ID, and risk signal flags. Supports fraud detection correlation, account takeover (ATO) detection, and PCI DSS access control audit requirements.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_velocity_control {
  cardholder_velocity_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the velocity control record.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_account. Business justification: cardholder_velocity_control explicitly describes per-account spending controls (\'per-cardholder or per-account\'). The existing cardholder_profile_id FK covers profile-level controls, but account-level']
  limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.limit. Business justification: Cardholder velocity controls implement product-level limit definitions. Compliance and fraud teams reconcile cardholder-level controls against the product.limit records they enforce (daily, weekly, mo']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: Merchant-scoped velocity controls are a real issuing feature: cardholders can have per-merchant spending limits (e.g., gambling, subscription merchants). Issuers enforce and report these controls at t']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_policy. Business justification: Velocity controls on cardholder accounts are operationalized from risk policies. Compliance and audit teams must trace each velocity control back to its governing risk policy for regulatory examinatio']
  primary_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder to whom this control applies.']
  risk_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_rule. Business justification: Each cardholder velocity control enforces a specific risk rule (e.g., max 5 transactions/hour). Fraud and risk operations teams need this link to audit which rule instantiated a velocity control, supp']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Velocity controls are frequently scheme-mandated (Mastercard contactless tap limits, Visa spend controls for prepaid). Linking velocity controls to a scheme enables scheme-specific limit enforcement, ']
  cardholder_velocity_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Payment channel to which the control applies (e.g., Card-Not-Present, Card-Present).']
  control_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the control definition: issuer-set, cardholder self-service, or regulatory mandate.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Category of the velocity control (e.g., spending limit, transaction count, MCC restriction).']
  control_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the control for change tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was created.']
  daily_spent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount spent by the cardholder today under this control.']
  daily_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions performed today under this control.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control expires or is no longer applicable; null for indefinite.']
  enforcement_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for rule evaluation when multiple controls apply; lower numbers have higher priority.']
  is_exempted boolean [note: 'Indicates if the cardholder is exempted from this control (e.g., corporate card).']
  last_evaluated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction evaluation against this control.']
  limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount limit for the control (e.g., daily spend cap).']
  limit_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the limit amount.']
  limit_period varchar [note: 'Time window for the monetary limit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information or business rationale.']
  transaction_count_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed in the defined period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of times the control was violated.']

  Note: 'Cardholder-level velocity and spending control configuration. Defines per-cardholder or per-account spending limits (daily, weekly, monthly), transaction count limits, single-transaction amount caps, MCC-level restrictions, geographic restrictions (allowed/blocked countries), channel restrictions (CNP, CP, ATM, online), and control source (issuer-set, cardholder self-service, regulatory mandate). Supports real-time authorization decisioning and parental/corporate card control programs. Each active control record represents a currently enforced rule.'
}

Table settlement.cycle {
  cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cycle']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Settlement cycles align with accounting periods; cycle net settlement amounts feed into period closing balances. Finance teams require cycle-to-period attribution for period-end close, net settlement ']
  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_profile. Business justification: Settlement cycles are jurisdiction-specific — cut-off times, value dates, and cross-border netting rules vary by jurisdiction. The cycle has `is_cross_border` but no FK to jurisdiction_profile. Multi-']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Settlement cycles are governed by a specific payment scheme (Visa, Mastercard, ACH) which defines cut-off times, settlement windows, and processing modes. Scheme-level cycle configuration and regulato']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.sla. Business justification: Settlement cycles are bound by scheme SLA commitments (T+1 settlement window, batch processing deadlines). Linking cycle to the governing SLA enables SLA breach monitoring and regulatory reporting on ']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification status for the settlement cycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement cycle record was created in the system.']
  cut_off_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the cut-off deadline for transaction inclusion in this cycle.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code identifying the settlement cycle, used in operational reporting.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement cycle.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement cycle ends; nullable for open-ended cycles.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the settlement cycle includes cross-border transactions.']
  net_position decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net position of the settlement participant after this cycle.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after fees and adjustments to be settled for the cycle.']
  processing_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement cycle is processed automatically by the system or requires manual intervention.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the cycle must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  settlement_cycle_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the settlement cycle purpose or characteristics.']
  settlement_cycle_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the settlement cycle.']
  settlement_window varchar [note: 'Human-readable representation of the time window for the cycle (e.g., \'09:00-17:00 UTC\').']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement cycle becomes effective.']
  total_fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees deducted from the gross amount for the cycle.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate gross amount of all transactions before fees and adjustments.']
  total_transactions varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions included in the settlement cycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement cycle record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which settled funds are considered available to the receiving party.']

  Note: 'Master definition of a settlement cycle — the scheduled window during which transactions are batched, cleared, and settled between acquiring banks, issuing banks, and card schemes. Owns cycle identifiers, cut-off times, settlement rail (RTGS, RTP, ACH, SWIFT), value date, cycle status, and participating network scheme. SSOT for settlement timing and cycle configuration.'
}

  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the net settlement was executed (nullable).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was submitted to the settlement engine.']
  total_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions included in the batch.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.']

  Note: 'Represents a discrete batch of transactions submitted for clearing and settlement within a given settlement cycle. Captures batch sequence number, batch file reference, originating acquirer, card scheme, total transaction count, gross debit amount, gross credit amount, net settlement amount, currency, batch status (open, submitted, accepted, rejected, settled), and submission timestamp. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform batch processing module.'
}

Each instruction maps to a net settlement position and drives the actual fund transfer. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform settlement module.'
}

Table settlement.net_position {
  net_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the net settlement position record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the second participant (e.g., issuing bank or scheme) in the settlement pair.']
  exposure_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.exposure_limit. Business justification: Net positions represent actual participant credit/debit exposure in a settlement cycle. Monitoring net_amount against exposure_limit.limit_amount is a core intraday risk management process — breach de']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Net positions in multilateral netting generate journal entries (debit/credit per participant net position) posted to the GL. Finance teams trace net position calculations to GL entries for netting dis']
  payment_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.payment_corridor. Business justification: Net positions for cross-border settlement are computed per payment corridor for liquidity management and corridor-level exposure reporting. Treasury and settlement ops teams require corridor-level net']
  primary_net_settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the first participant (e.g., acquiring bank) in the settlement pair.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: Cross-border net positions are calculated using a specific FX rate; regulatory reporting and end-of-day reconciliation require the exact rate used for netting. exchange_rate on net_position is a denor']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Net positions are calculated per scheme — multilateral netting is scheme-governed with scheme-specific netting_method rules. Scheme-level exposure reporting and netting compliance verification require']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement_batch. Business justification: Net positions are computed per settlement batch. The batch_number STRING is a denormalized reference; replacing with settlement_batch_id FK normalizes the relationship and enables batch-level net posi']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this net position belongs.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any adjustment applied to the net position.']
  confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the net position has been confirmed for fund movement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the net position record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the net position becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the net position expires or is superseded.']
  gross_credit_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross credit amount for the participant pair before netting.']
  gross_debit_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross debit amount for the participant pair before netting.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'Indicates if the net position has been adjusted post initial calculation.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after debits and credits are netted (credit minus debit).']
  net_position_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number for the net position.']

  net_position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the net position.']
  netting_method varchar [note: 'Method used to net the positions.']
  note varchar [note: 'Free‑text note or comment regarding the net position.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Category of the net position.']
  settlement_cycle_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the start of the settlement cycle.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement is scheduled to occur.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the net position record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the net position record for concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Computed net settlement position for each participant pair (acquirer–issuer, acquirer–scheme, or scheme–acquirer) within a settlement cycle. Stores gross debit total, gross credit total, net amount, currency, netting method (bilateral, multilateral), position type (transaction netting, interchange, scheme fee), position status, and confirmation flags. After merging interchange and scheme fee settlement records, this product is the unified SSOT for all settlement obligations before fund movement instructions are generated.'
}

  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement_batch. The batch_number STRING is a denormalized reference; replacing with settlement_batch_id FK normalizes the relatio']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle (e.g., daily, hourly) associated with this confirmation.']

  Note: 'Record of confirmed fund receipt or payment acknowledgment for a settlement instruction. Captures confirmation reference, confirming bank, confirmation timestamp, confirmed amount, currency, RTGS/RTP transaction reference, and reconciliation status. Provides the authoritative proof-of-payment for each settlement obligation. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform reconciliation module.'
}

  irf_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_table.   net_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.net_position. Business justification: Each cleared transaction contributes to a net settlement position.   primary_clearing_settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank that processed the transaction.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.program. Business justification: Interchange programs (e.g., commercial card, rewards, debit) determine which interchange rules apply during clearing. Clearing records must reference the applicable program for correct rate applicatio']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: Cross-border clearing records apply a specific FX rate for currency conversion during clearing; interchange fee calculations and scheme compliance reporting require the exact rate used. exchange_rate ']
  scheme_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme_fee_schedule. Business justification: Clearing records carry fee_amount computed from the scheme fee schedule. This link enables automated interchange fee validation and scheme fee audit — required for scheme compliance and dispute resolu']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement_batch. Business justification: Clearing records are produced within a settlement batch. The batch_number STRING column is a denormalized reference to the parent batch; replacing it with a proper FK settlement_batch_id normalizes th']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.cycle. Business justification: clearing_record.settlement_cycle is a STRING denormalization of the settlement cycle. Replacing it with a proper FK settlement_cycle_id → cycle.cycle_id normalizes the relationship and enables reliabl']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original authorization transaction that is being cleared.']
  authorization_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the acquiring network for the original authorization.']
  cleared_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the transaction cleared before fees.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the transaction was cleared.']
  clearing_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the clearing record by the clearing system.']
  clearing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the clearing record.']
  clearing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the transaction was cleared in the processing platform.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record required additional compliance review (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the clearing record was first persisted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total interchange and processing fees applied to the cleared transaction.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was flagged as fraudulent during clearing.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clearing record represents a reversal transaction.']
  issuing_bank_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing bank that issued the payment instrument.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount to be settled after deducting fees.']
  original_authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer approving the original transaction.']
  processing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the clearing event was processed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: many values — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reporting_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the reporting requirement status for this clearing record.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing why the transaction was reversed.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned by the risk engine at clearing time.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the clearing record.']

  Note: 'Individual cleared transaction record produced during the clearing phase — the post-authorization, pre-settlement representation of a payment transaction. Stores ARN (Acquirer Reference Number), original authorization code, MID, card scheme, interchange category, cleared amount, currency, clearing date, and clearing status. Bridges the transaction domain and the settlement domain. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform clearing module.'
}

Table settlement.reconciliation_record {
  reconciliation_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the reconciliation record.']
  billing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.billing. Business justification: Reconciliation records are matched against interchange billing records as part of the interchange reconciliation process. This is a mandatory financial close and regulatory reporting requirement in ca']
  clearing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.clearing_record. Business justification: Reconciliation records reconcile cleared transaction records against external counterparts. A direct FK to clearing_record enables matching reconciliation entries to the specific cleared transactions ']
  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_case. Business justification: Reconciliation records with `aml_check_status`, `kyc_status`, or `is_exception=true` are escalated to compliance cases.   downgrade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.downgrade. Business justification: Interchange downgrades are a primary source of settlement variances captured in reconciliation records.   duplicate_of_record_reconciliation_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original reconciliation record when this record is a duplicate.']

Business justification: Reconciliation records match internal records against external funding confirmations.   instruction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.instruction. Business justification: Reconciliation records match internal settlement records against external counterparts. Linking to the instruction being reconciled provides direct traceability from the reconciliation entry to the fu']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Reconciliation records identifying variances trigger corrective journal entries.   merchant_payout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.merchant_payout. Business justification: Reconciliation records validate merchant payout disbursements. Linking to merchant_payout enables reconciliation of actual payout amounts against expected net settlement values for merchants.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Reconciliation processes, exception thresholds, and variance tolerances are product-specific.   qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.qualification. Business justification: Reconciliation records identify variances between expected and actual settlement; interchange qualification outcomes are a primary source of variance. Direct qualification reference enables exception-']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Reconciliation records must be matched against scheme-specific transaction files. Scheme identity is essential for scheme-level reconciliation reporting, exception handling, and regulatory submissions']
