Scheme invoice reconciliation is a mandatory monthly financial close process; direct FK enables period-level scheme ']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement_batch. Business justification: Reconciliation records are produced per settlement batch. Linking to settlement_batch enables batch-level reconciliation reporting and exception tracking across all records in a batch.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle (e.g., daily, hourly) for this reconciliation.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement. Business justification: Reconciliation records validate settlement outcomes. Linking to the master settlement record enables reconciliation status to be tracked at the settlement level and supports settlement finalization wo']
  settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external party (e.g., acquiring bank, issuing bank, scheme) involved in the reconciliation.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening result for the counterparty.']
  amount_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of fees, discounts, or other adjustments applied to the gross amount.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount before any adjustments or fees.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount after adjustments, representing the amount to be settled.']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Closing balance of the account or statement at the end of the reconciliation period.']
  comment varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comment about the reconciliation.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance outcome of the reconciliation with respect to regulatory rules.']
  counterpart_record_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the external record (e.g., bank statement, scheme file) matched against.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation event occurred.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code categorising the type of exception, if any.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation was flagged for potential fraud.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'True if this record is identified as a duplicate of another reconciliation record.']
  is_exception boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation record required exception handling.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Know‑Your‑Customer verification status of the counterparty.']
  last_reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful reconciliation for this record.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Opening balance of the account or statement at the start of the reconciliation period.']
  processing_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time taken to complete the reconciliation process, measured in seconds.']
  reconciliation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the reconciliation.']
  reconciliation_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the reconciliation record.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'External or internal reference number for the reconciliation run (e.g., batch number, instruction ID).']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Category of reconciliation (batch, instruction, nostro statement, scheme file).']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this reconciliation must be included in regulatory reporting.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing how variances were investigated and resolved.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the reconciliation based on fraud or AML considerations.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement associated with this reconciliation was executed.']
  source_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external file (e.g., MT940, camt.053) used for reconciliation.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal settlement record used in the reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that produced the source record (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between internal settlement amount and external counterpart amount.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any variance detected during reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Reconciliation entry matching internal settlement records against external counterparts — including bank statements, scheme clearing files, RTGS confirmations, and nostro/vostro account statements (SWIFT MT940/MT950, camt.053). Captures reconciliation run reference, reconciliation type (batch, instruction, nostro, scheme file), matched/unmatched status, variance amount, source record reference, counterpart record reference, opening and closing balances (for statement-level reconciliation), reconciliation method, and resolution notes. SSOT for all settlement reconciliation including nostro statement matching and break management.'
}

  Note: 'Master record of a settlement bank account used to fund or receive settlement obligations. SSOT for settlement account registry distinct from the general ledger account in the ledger domain.'
}

  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product.   scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Funding schedules are scheme-specific — different schemes mandate different funding windows and hold_period_days requirements. Linking funding_schedule to scheme enables scheme-specific funding config']

  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code indicating the geographic region for regulatory or reporting purposes.']

  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule frequency or timing rule (e.g., daily, weekly, T+1).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master schedule defining when and how merchants, sub-acquirers, or PayFacs are funded from settled proceeds. SSOT for merchant funding timing, payout configuration, and settlement-to-payout waterfall rules.'
}

Payments operations requir']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total chargeback adjustments deducted.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payout passed all compliance checks.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from external payment network or partner.']
  interchange_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interchange fee deducted from the gross payout.']
  mdr_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'MDR fee deducted from the gross payout.']
  merchant_mid varchar [note: 'Merchant Identification Number of the receiving merchant.']
  msf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'MSF fee deducted from the gross payout.']
  net_payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount transferred to the merchant after all deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments regarding the payout.']
  party_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant party receiving the payout.']
  payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount before any deductions.']
  payout_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transfer the payout funds.']
  payout_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference code assigned to the payout transaction by the system.']
  payout_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the payout.']
  payout_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payout was initiated.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payout record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payout record was last updated.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total refunds deducted from the payout.']
  reserve_hold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount held in reserve and not released.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the payout based on fraud detection models.']
  scheme_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Card scheme fee deducted from the gross payout.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the payout amount is to be settled.']

  Note: 'Individual payout disbursement record representing the net funds transferred to a merchant or sub-merchant after applying the funding schedule waterfall — deducting MDR, MSF, interchange fees, scheme fees, chargebacks, refunds, and reserve holds from settled transaction proceeds. Captures payout reference, merchant MID, payout amount, currency, line-item deduction breakdown, funding account, payout status (pending, released, on-hold, paid, failed), value date, and remittance advice reference. SSOT for merchant settlement payouts and funding audit trail.'
}

Table settlement.settlement_exception {
  settlement_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the settlement exception record.']
  affected_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific settlement instruction linked to the exception.']
  chargeback_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.chargeback. Business justification: Chargeback-driven settlement exceptions are a named operational category. Exception resolution workflows must trace the exception to the triggering chargeback for root cause analysis, regulatory repor']
  clearing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.clearing_record. Business justification: Settlement exceptions can arise from clearing record failures or discrepancies. Linking settlement_exception to clearing_record identifies the specific cleared transaction that caused the exception, e']
  downgrade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.downgrade. Business justification: Interchange downgrades cause settlement exceptions when downgraded rates affect settlement amounts. Exception handling and SLA management require tracing which downgrade triggered the exception — a st']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: Settlement exceptions are directly triggered by fraud detection events (e.g., mid-settlement fraud flag causing a payment hold). A direct FK enables fraud-driven exception dashboards, SLA breach track']
  funding_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.funding_confirmation. Business justification: Settlement exceptions can arise from funding confirmation failures (e.g., RTGS rejection, bank acknowledgment failure). Linking settlement_exception to funding_confirmation identifies the specific con']
  merchant_payout_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.merchant_payout. Business justification: Settlement exceptions can arise from merchant payout failures (e.g., invalid account, insufficient funds). Linking settlement_exception to merchant_payout identifies the specific payout that triggered']
  net_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the net position record impacted by the exception.']
  participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.participant. Business justification: Settlement exceptions affect specific participants in the settlement network. Linking settlement_exception to participant identifies which participant is impacted, enabling participant-level exception']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Settlement exception SLA targets, root cause codes, and resolution workflows are product-specific — BNPL exceptions follow different paths than card or A2A. Operations triage and SLA breach reporting ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Settlement exceptions may be triggered by regulatory obligation breaches (e.g., settlement finality deadline missed under PSD2, RTGS cut-off violations). The exception has `regulatory_flag` and `sla_b']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch where the exception originated.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle during which the exception occurred.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement. Business justification: Settlement exceptions arise within the context of a master settlement record. Linking settlement_exception to settlement enables settlement-level exception tracking and supports settlement finalizatio']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the exception (e.g., shortfall amount).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the exception triggered compliance checks (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the action taken to remediate the exception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was created in the data lake.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Technical error code returned by the processing engine.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable error description associated with the exception.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the exception for tracking and reference.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was first detected in the settlement process.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of the settlement exception indicating the nature of the break.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a human manually intervened to resolve the exception.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the resolver for additional context.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original transaction amount before the exception was identified.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the original transaction amount.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'True if the exception has regulatory reporting or compliance implications.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the resolution attempt.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was marked as resolved.']
  retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of automated retry attempts performed before escalation.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the underlying cause of the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_mismatch|validation_error|network_failure|partner_error|regulatory_hold — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exception.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business-assigned severity level reflecting impact on settlement operations.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception breached its Service Level Agreement target.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed hours to resolve the exception as defined by SLA.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the exception record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the exception record.']

  Note: 'Operational record of any exception, break, or failure arising during the settlement lifecycle — including unmatched clearing records, funding shortfalls, rejected instructions, and reconciliation variances. Captures exception type, severity, affected batch or instruction reference, exception amount, assigned resolver, resolution status, and SLA breach flag. SSOT for settlement break management.'
}

Table settlement.settlement {
  settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for settlement']
  acquirer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.acquirer. Business justification: Settlements are originated by acquiring banks. Linking settlement to acquirer enables acquirer-level settlement tracking, daily limit enforcement, and regulatory reporting by acquiring institution.']

  instruction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.instruction.   net_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the net position record used for netting calculations.']
  network_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.endpoint. Business justification: Settlements are transmitted over a specific network endpoint. This link supports endpoint-level settlement volume analysis, network connectivity reporting, and incident root-cause analysis for settlem']
  nostro_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.nostro_account.   original_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on settlement (original_settlement_id)']
  participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.participant. Business justification: A settlement record represents the settlement obligation between participants. Linking settlement to participant identifies the primary participant (acquirer, issuer, or scheme) for the settlement, en']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.program. Business justification: Settlements are processed under specific interchange programs (commercial card, debit, rewards) that determine settlement rules and rates. Program-level settlement reporting and program-specific settl']
  routing_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.routing_table. Business justification: Settlements are executed over a specific network rail determined by routing rules. rail_details and rail_type on settlement are routing-table-driven. Rail performance analysis and routing optimization']
  scheme_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme_fee_schedule. Business justification: Settlement fee_amount is governed by the scheme fee schedule. This link enables scheme fee reconciliation reports and scheme billing validation — a mandatory requirement in Visa/Mastercard settlement ']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Each settlement is executed under a specific scheme\'s rules governing settlement method, currency, and regulatory reporting. Scheme-level settlement volume analytics and scheme billing reconciliation ']
  scheme_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_invoice. Business justification: Settlements are reconciled against scheme invoices for scheme fee and interchange reimbursement settlement. Settlement-level scheme invoice reconciliation is a mandatory process in card network operat']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file that contains this settlement.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.sla_profile. Business justification: Settlement performance reports reference the SLA profile governing gateway latency to ensure contractual compliance.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the settlement passed all regulatory compliance checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the start of the settlement cycle that includes this record.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when converting foreign currency to the settlement currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees deducted from the gross amount.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before fees and adjustments.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the settlement involves parties in different countries.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after fees and adjustments, transferred to the participant.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational comments or remarks.']
  processing_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement was processed in batch or real‑time mode.']
  rail_type varchar [note: 'Payment rail used for the settlement (e.g., RTGS, RTP, ACH, SWIFT).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the settlement must be reported to a regulator (e.g., AML, SAR).']

  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the settlement.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement event occurred in the real world.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the settlement (e.g., netting, gross, adjusted).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that generated the settlement record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which funds become available to the participant.']
  window varchar [note: 'Time window (e.g., morning, afternoon) during which the settlement is processed.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for settlement. '
}

  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_profile.   legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Payment schemes operate under specific legal entities for regulatory licensing, financial reporting, and liability. Essential for compliance reporting (PCI DSS, PSD2) and consolidation of scheme-relat']
  policy_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_document. Business justification: Payment schemes publish operating regulations and rulebooks that are formal policy documents. Compliance teams track which policy document (scheme rulebook version) governs each scheme. Scheme ruleboo']
  rate_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate_feed. Business justification: Payment schemes designate specific FX rate feeds for DCC and cross-border rate sourcing — Visa uses specific rate providers, Mastercard uses others. Scheme-specific rate feed configuration for DCC com']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Payment schemes (Visa, Mastercard) operate under specific regulatory obligations (PSD2, local mandates). Compliance teams must map each scheme to its governing regulatory obligation for scheme complia']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory and industry standards the scheme must adhere to.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for scheme support inquiries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for scheme support inquiries.']
  currency_supported varchar [note: 'Currencies in which the scheme can settle transactions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the scheme became effective for routing and processing.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'List of ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the scheme is accepted.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Legal entity that owns or governs the scheme (e.g., Visa Inc., Mastercard).']
  interchange_fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric fee rate applied to transactions (e.g., 0.0150 for 1.5%).']
  interchange_fee_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the interchange fee is calculated as a percentage or a fixed amount.']
  network_certification_date date [note: 'Date when the scheme achieved its most recent network certification.']
  network_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the scheme within the network.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scheme record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scheme record.']
  risk_profile varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the scheme based on fraud and chargeback history.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Canonical short code used to identify the scheme in messages and reports (e.g., VISA, MC).']
  scheme_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the scheme, its purpose and key characteristics.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  scheme_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the scheme within the platform.']
  scheme_type varchar [note: 'Category of the scheme based on the primary instrument or service it supports.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Default currency used for settlement of scheme transactions.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle transactions for the scheme.']
  supported_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of transaction types the scheme supports (e.g., purchase, refund, cash‑advance).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the scheme was retired or ceased to be supported (nullable).']
  three_ds_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scheme supports 3‑Domain Secure authentication.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scheme supports tokenization of PANs.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the scheme definition (e.g., v2.1).']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL providing scheme specifications and resources.']

  Note: 'Master record for each payment card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, UnionPay, etc.). Defines the scheme brand identity, governing body, supported transaction types, geographic reach, and scheme-level operational parameters. SSOT for payment scheme identity across the platform.'
}

Table network.endpoint {
  endpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the network endpoint record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Network endpoints (gateways, data centers) incur operational costs (hosting, bandwidth, maintenance). Linking to cost_center enables accurate allocation of infrastructure expenses for budgeting, varia']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Required for routing and settlement reports to map each network endpoint to the owning partner (acquirer/issuer) responsible for traffic and fee allocation.']
  pci_dss_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.pci_dss_audit. Business justification: Network endpoints are in-scope CDE (Cardholder Data Environment) components for PCI-DSS audits. Linking endpoint to pci_dss_audit enables tracking which endpoints were assessed in each audit cycle — a']
  policy_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_document. Business justification: Endpoints operate under specific security and connectivity policies (TLS policies, encryption standards, access control policies). Linking endpoint to policy_document enables compliance teams to verif']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Endpoint compliance audit requires mapping each network endpoint to the regulatory obligation it satisfies (PCI‑DSS, ISO‑20022).']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Endpoints belong to a payment scheme; adding scheme_id FK normalizes the relationship and removes the redundant network_scheme string column.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit events related to the endpoint.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate connections to the endpoint.']
  average_latency_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean round‑trip latency observed for the endpoint, measured in milliseconds.']
  certificate_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the associated TLS certificate expires.']
  compliance_iso_20022 boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with ISO 20022 messaging standards.']
  compliance_pci_dss boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the endpoint meets PCI DSS requirements.']
  compliance_regulatory varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulatory codes (e.g., PSD2, SOX) applicable to the endpoint.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether connections are persistent or stateless.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the endpoint record was first created.']
  data_center_location varchar [note: 'Physical data‑center location (city, facility) hosting the endpoint.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the endpoint became operationally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the endpoint is scheduled to be retired; null if indefinite.']
  encryption_protocol varchar [note: 'Encryption protocol negotiated for the endpoint.']
  endpoint_code varchar [note: 'Business‑level unique code used in operational processes.']
  endpoint_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the endpoint purpose and characteristics.']
  endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the endpoint.']
  endpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the endpoint.']
  health_check_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Interval between successive health checks, in seconds.']
  health_check_timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum time to wait for a health‑check response before marking it failed.']
  health_status varchar [note: 'Current health assessment of the endpoint.']
  host_url varchar [note: 'Fully qualified URL or hostname used to reach the endpoint.']
  ip_address_range varchar [note: 'CIDR notation representing the IP range allocated to the endpoint.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this endpoint is the primary for its scheme.']
  is_tls_mutual boolean [note: 'True if mutual TLS authentication is required.']
  last_health_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful health‑check.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Planned maintenance period (e.g., "Sat 02:00‑04:00 UTC").']
  max_connections varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous connections the endpoint can sustain.']
  max_message_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Maximum size of a single message the endpoint will accept, in bytes.']
  monitoring_endpoint varchar [note: 'URL used by monitoring tools to probe endpoint health.']
  network_interface varchar [note: 'Type of network interface (e.g., Ethernet, Fiber).']
  port varchar [note: 'TCP/UDP port number on which the endpoint listens.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the messaging protocol used (e.g., ISO8583, ISO20022).']
  routing_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when selecting among multiple eligible endpoints.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes the endpoint can process.']
  supported_message_format varchar [note: 'Message format(s) the endpoint can process.']
  throughput_tps decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average number of transactions the endpoint processes per second.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the endpoint record.']

  Note: 'Defines physical and logical connectivity endpoints for each payment network — host URLs, IP ranges, port configurations, protocol versions (ISO 8583, ISO 20022), TLS certificate references, and connection health thresholds. Represents the network infrastructure layer for scheme connectivity.'
}

Table network.routing_table {
  routing_table_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the routing rule record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Routing table entries are currency-specific for cross-border transactions. The plain currency_code attribute is a denormalized representation of the currency pair. Currency-aware routing configuration']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.endpoint. Business justification: Routing table should link to the endpoint entity rather than storing a URL string; this creates a true relational link and removes redundant data.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: Least-cost routing (LCR) is a core payments business process where routing decisions are made based on interchange rate categories. Routing tables must reference the target irf_rate_category to implem']
  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_profile. Business justification: Routing tables are jurisdiction-specific (Durbin Amendment in US, PSD2 in EU require specific routing rules). Linking routing_table to jurisdiction_profile enables compliance audits of jurisdiction-sp']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_policy. Business justification: Routing tables enforce risk policies — the risk_score_threshold attribute on routing_table confirms risk-policy-governed routing. Routing decisions for high-risk transactions (by MCC, BIN, amount) are']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Routing tables have a \'compliance_check_required\' flag — the specific regulatory obligation driving that check must be traceable. Compliance teams need to identify which regulatory obligation mandates']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Routing tables are defined per scheme; adding scheme_id FK creates parent relationship and removes need for any scheme string column (none present).']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending BIN of the inclusive range for which this rule applies.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting BIN (Bank Identification Number) of the inclusive range for which this rule applies.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or justification for the most recent modification.']
  compliance_check_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if regulatory compliance checks must be performed before routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing rule record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the routing rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the routing rule expires; null if indefinite.']
  fallback_sequence varchar [note: 'Order number for fallback routing attempts.']
  fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fee rate (as a decimal fraction) applied when routing through the target network.']
  is_fallback_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fallback routing is permitted if primary routing fails.']
  last_tested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent automated test of the routing rule.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for transactions that can be routed by this rule.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Merchant category code that the rule matches.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower monetary threshold for transactions applicable to this rule.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority determining rule precedence (lower = higher priority).']
  processing_rail varchar [note: 'Processing mode used for the routed transaction.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed fraud risk score for a transaction to be routed via this rule.']
  routing_table_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the routing rule purpose and logic.']
  routing_table_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing rule.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing logic applied.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the routing rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the routing rule.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed surcharge amount added to the transaction fee for this routing path.']
  target_network varchar [note: 'Payment network to which the transaction should be routed.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent routing rule test.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction the routing rule applies to.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the routing rule.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Defines transaction routing rules mapping BIN ranges, MCC codes, transaction types, and currency combinations to target network endpoints and processing rails. Supports multi-network routing logic, least-cost routing, and failover sequencing. Core operational artifact for the Payment Gateway and Authorization Engine.'
}

  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that last modified the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the routing rule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Individual routing rule entry within a routing table. Specifies the condition set (BIN range, card type, transaction type, amount threshold, currency, region) and the target network endpoint or rail. Supports priority ordering, effective date ranges, and override flags for dynamic routing decisions.'

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: floor_limit|velocity_threshold|cvv_requirement|avs_requirement|3ds_mandate|sca_exemption|chargeback_time_limit|emv_fallback|other — promote to reference product]']

Table network.sla {
  sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the SLA record.']

  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_profile. Business justification: SLAs are jurisdiction-specific — PSD2 mandates specific payment processing time SLAs in the EU, while other jurisdictions have different requirements. Linking SLA to jurisdiction_profile enables juris']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: SLAs are service contracts between legal entities (e.g., fintech platform and scheme network). Required for tracking penalty liabilities, revenue commitments, and ensuring contract obligations and fin']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: SLA contracts must reference the specific regulatory obligation they support for compliance reporting.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: SLAs are negotiated per scheme; replace scheme_code with scheme_id FK.']
  actual_authorization_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Measured average authorization response time during the reporting period.']
  actual_batch_settlement_window_hours varchar [note: 'Observed average time taken to settle batches.']
  actual_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Observed average latency during the reporting period.']
  actual_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured network availability percentage for the reporting period.']
  batch_settlement_window_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum time allowed to complete batch settlement after transaction capture.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for penalty and any monetary SLA components.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the SLA becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the SLA expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the person or team to notify on SLA breach.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for SLA breach escalation.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent SLA performance review.']
  max_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed network latency for transaction messages, in milliseconds.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often SLA performance metrics are measured and reported.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or special conditions.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the penalty for a breach, expressed in the contract currency.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing the financial penalty applied for SLA breaches.']

  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA record.']
  target_authorization_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Promised maximum time for a real‑time authorization response, expressed in milliseconds.']
  target_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contracted network availability expressed as a percentage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SLA record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the SLA contract (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Defines SLA commitments for each payment network connection — target authorization response time (TPS), uptime percentage, maximum latency thresholds, batch settlement window, and penalty clauses. Tracks contracted vs. actual performance metrics per scheme and endpoint.'
}

  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that initially created the record.']

  Note: 'Records the formal membership and participation agreements between the platform (as acquirer, issuer, or processor) and each card scheme. Stores membership type (principal, associate, affiliate), membership ID, effective date, annual fees, and compliance obligations. SSOT for scheme participation status.'
}

Table network.scheme_fee_schedule {
  scheme_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each scheme fee schedule record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Scheme fee schedules for cross-border transactions apply to specific currency pairs (e.g., USD/EUR interchange rates differ from USD/GBP). Cross-border interchange fee calculation and scheme fee repor']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Fee schedule entries are posted to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition in daily accounting.']
  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_profile. Business justification: Fee schedules have domestic/international/cross-border applicability flags that are jurisdiction-driven. Linking to jurisdiction_profile enables jurisdiction-specific fee compliance reporting (e.g., E']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Fee schedules are constrained by regulatory obligations (EU Interchange Fee Regulation caps, Durbin Amendment debit fee limits). Compliance teams must link fee schedules to the regulatory obligation t']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Fee schedules are defined per scheme; replace scheme_code with scheme_id FK.']
  applicable_to_domestic boolean [note: 'True if the fee applies to domestic (same‑country) transactions.']
  applicable_to_international boolean [note: 'True if the fee applies to international (cross‑border) transactions.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment card for which the fee schedule is applicable.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the fee schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount charged per transaction when the fee is flat‑rate.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount that can be charged for this fee per transaction.']
  fee_cap_indicator boolean [note: 'True if a fee cap is enforced for this schedule.']
  fee_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to the transaction amount for percentage‑based fees.']
  fee_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing code that uniquely identifies the fee schedule within the organization.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of the fee charged by the scheme (e.g., network access, processing, cross‑border assessment, value‑added service).']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the fee applies to cross‑border transactions; otherwise false.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance or policy review of the fee schedule.']
  maximum_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount for which the fee applies.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code representing the merchant\'s business category.']
  minimum_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount for which the fee applies.']
  scheme_fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, or special conditions of the fee schedule.']
  scheme_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee schedule.']
  surcharge_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the fee is a surcharge passed to the merchant or cardholder.']
  transaction_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the transaction (e.g., purchase, refund, cash‑advance) to which the fee applies.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the fee schedule record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the fee schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the fee schedule record for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the fee schedule record.']

  Note: 'Defines the fee structures charged by card schemes for network access, authorization, clearing, and value-added services. Stores fee type (network access fee, scheme processing fee, cross-border assessment), rate, effective date range, and applicable transaction categories. Distinct from interchange (owned by interchange domain).'
}

Table network.multi_network_routing {
  multi_network_routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the routing profile.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Multi-network routing profiles select optimal rails based on currency — EUR transactions may prefer SEPA, USD may prefer Fedwire. Least-cost routing decisions and currency-aware network preference ord']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: Multi-network routing profiles implement least-cost routing by selecting the network with the lowest interchange rate category. The least_cost_flag and durbin_compliance_mode fields confirm LCR intent']
  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_profile. Business justification: Multi-network routing profiles have a \'durbin_compliance_mode\' attribute indicating jurisdiction-specific compliance requirements. Linking to jurisdiction_profile enables compliance validation of rout']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Multi-network routing profiles have \'compliance_check_required\' and \'durbin_compliance_mode\' — these are driven by specific regulatory obligations (Durbin Amendment, PSD2). Linking to regulatory_oblig']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Multi-network routing profiles use risk scores to select optimal networks — the risk_score_threshold attribute on multi_network_routing confirms risk-based routing decisions. Linking to the entity\'s r']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Multi‑network routing profiles are scoped to a scheme; add scheme_id FK.']
  compliance_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional regulatory compliance checks must be performed before routing.']
  durbin_compliance_mode varchar [note: 'Specifies how the profile complies with the Durbin Amendment for debit transactions.']
  fallback_network_code varchar [note: 'Network code to use when primary networks are unavailable or fail compliance checks.']
  least_cost_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the routing engine should prioritize the lowest interchange cost.']
  network_preference_order varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of network codes (e.g., VISA,MC,DISC) in order of preference for routing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for operational comments, exceptions, or audit remarks.']
  priority_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric score used by the routing engine to rank this profile against others.']
  profile_created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the routing profile.']
  profile_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the routing profile was initially created in the system.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the routing profile.']
  profile_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the latest update.']
  profile_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the routing profile.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed fraud risk score for a transaction to be routed using this profile.']
  routing_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing profile based on transaction type or market segment.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the profile is a default rule, an override, or specific to a merchant or cardholder.']
  transaction_volume_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative transaction amount (in USD) required to trigger this routing profile.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the routing profile for change management.']

  Note: 'Manages multi-network routing configurations for transactions eligible to be processed across multiple networks (e.g., Visa vs. Interlink for debit, Mastercard vs. Maestro). Stores network preference order, merchant override rules, cardholder preference flags, Durbin Amendment compliance settings, and least-cost routing logic.'
}

Table interchange.irf_table {
  irf_table_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interchange reimbursement fee table record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Interchange rate tables publish rates per currency pair (e.g., USD/EUR domestic vs cross-border rates). Essential for multi-currency interchange rate management and scheme compliance reporting across ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Interchange rate tables drive automated GL posting rules for revenue recognition. Each rate category maps to specific revenue accounts for financial statement preparation and regulatory reporting.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: irf_table references its category; adds irf_rate_category_id FK to irf_rate_category']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.program. Business justification: irf_table.program_identifier (STRING) stores the scheme program code that governs this IRF rate entry (e.g., Visa Utility, Mastercard Merit). The program product is the authoritative master for card s']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Interchange rate tables are published by specific card schemes. Scheme bulletin reconciliation, rate audits, and interchange cost management all require linking each rate table to its governing scheme']
  acquirer_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the acquiring bank. Used for domestic vs. cross-border interchange rate determination. Null indicates rate applies regardless of acquirer country.']
  authorization_method varchar [note: 'Card authorization method required for this interchange rate (chip, contactless, magnetic stripe, manual entry, ecommerce, token). Null indicates no specific method requirement.']
  bin_range_high varchar [note: 'Upper bound of the BIN/IIN range to which this interchange rate applies. Used for BIN-level interchange qualification rules. Null indicates no BIN restriction.']
  bin_range_low varchar [note: 'Lower bound of the BIN/IIN range to which this interchange rate applies. Used for BIN-level interchange qualification rules. Null indicates no BIN restriction.']
  card_product_tier varchar [note: 'Card product tier or brand level (e.g., Standard, Rewards, Premium, Signature, World, World Elite, Platinum, Infinite). Higher tiers typically carry higher interchange rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|rewards|premium|signature|world|world_elite|platinum|infinite — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment card to which this interchange rate applies (credit, debit, prepaid, commercial, purchasing). Critical qualifier for rate selection.']
  cost_of_acceptance_category varchar [note: 'Internal classification category used for cost-of-acceptance reporting and analysis (e.g., low-cost, standard, premium, high-cost). Supports merchant pricing strategy and portfolio profitability analysis.']
  durbin_cap_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum interchange fee amount allowed under Regulation II Durbin Amendment for regulated debit transactions. Null if not subject to Durbin caps.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interchange rate is subject to Regulation II Durbin Amendment caps (True for regulated debit, False otherwise). Critical for US debit interchange compliance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this interchange rate schedule expires and is no longer applicable. Null indicates an open-ended schedule currently in effect.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this interchange rate schedule becomes effective and applicable to transactions. Part of the temporal validity window for this rate.']
  interchange_category_code varchar [note: 'Scheme-specific code identifying the interchange rate category (e.g., CPS Retail, Merit III, Core Value, Standard). This is the authoritative classification used by the card scheme.']
  interchange_category_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the interchange rate category (e.g., Card Present Signature Preferred Retail, Electronic Commerce Basic).']
  interchange_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the interchange fee amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  interchange_fixed_fee_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'Fixed per-transaction fee component of the interchange in the currency specified by interchange_currency_code (e.g., $0.10). Combined with percentage rate to calculate total IRF.']
  interchange_rate_percentage decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage rate component of the interchange fee applied to the transaction amount (e.g., 1.65% stored as 0.0165). Used in cost-of-acceptance calculations.']
  internal_rate_code varchar [note: 'Internal company-specific code or identifier for this interchange rate, used for mapping to internal pricing and cost-of-acceptance systems. May differ from scheme category code.']
  maximum_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount for which this interchange rate applies, in the currency specified by interchange_currency_code. Null indicates no maximum.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit ISO 18245 merchant category code to which this interchange rate applies. Null indicates rate applies across all MCCs. Used for industry-specific interchange qualification.']
  minimum_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount required for this interchange rate to apply, in the currency specified by interchange_currency_code. Null indicates no minimum.']
  qualification_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed business rules and conditions that must be met for a transaction to qualify for this interchange rate (e.g., EMV chip, AVS match, settlement within 24 hours). Free-text field capturing scheme-specific requirements.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this interchange rate schedule (active, pending, expired, superseded, withdrawn). Used for rate version management and historical analysis.']
  rate_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this interchange rate schedule, assigned by the card scheme. Used to track rate changes over time and ensure correct rate application.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interchange rate record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system from which this interchange rate data originated (e.g., Visa Rate Feed, Mastercard Portal, Manual Entry). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interchange rate record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting regulatory compliance considerations for this interchange rate (e.g., PSD2 caps, Durbin Amendment applicability, regional regulations). Used for compliance audit and legal review.']
  scheme_bulletin_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the card scheme bulletin or notice that announced this interchange rate change. Used for traceability and compliance documentation.']
  scheme_publication_date date [note: 'Date when the card scheme officially published this interchange rate schedule. Used for audit trail and regulatory reporting.']
  settlement_timeframe_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours between authorization and settlement for this interchange rate to apply (e.g., 24, 48, 72). Null indicates no settlement timeframe requirement.']
  transaction_region varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the transaction (domestic, intra-regional, inter-regional, cross-border). Determines applicable interchange rate tier.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction to which this interchange rate applies (card present, card not present, ecommerce, mail/telephone order, recurring, contactless). Key qualifier for interchange eligibility.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) schedules published by card schemes (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover). Stores interchange rate categories, rate percentages, per-transaction fees, effective dates, card type applicability, transaction type qualifiers, and scheme-specific program identifiers. This is the authoritative SSOT for all interchange rate definitions used in cost-of-acceptance calculations.'
}

Table interchange.irf_rate_category {
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interchange rate category. Primary key.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) that defines this interchange rate category.']
  authorization_timeframe_hours varchar [note: 'The maximum number of hours between authorization and capture for transactions to qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  avs_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Address Verification System (AVS) validation is required to qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'The ending Bank Identification Number (BIN) or Issuer Identification Number (IIN) in the range of card products that qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'The starting Bank Identification Number (BIN) or Issuer Identification Number (IIN) in the range of card products that qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  card_not_present_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interchange rate category applies to card-not-present (CNP) transactions such as e-commerce, mail order, or telephone order.']
  card_present_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interchange rate category applies to card-present (CP) transactions where the physical card is presented at the Point of Sale (POS).']
  category_code varchar [note: 'The unique code assigned by the card scheme to identify this interchange rate category (e.g., CPS_RETAIL, MERIT_III, OPTBLUE_TIER1).']
  category_name varchar [note: 'The full business name of the interchange rate category as published by the card scheme (e.g., Visa CPS/Retail, Mastercard Merit III, Amex OptBlue Standard).']
  category_type varchar [note: 'The classification of the interchange rate category based on card product type and transaction characteristics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|premium|commercial|debit|credit|prepaid|rewards|government|healthcare — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contactless_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless NFC transactions are eligible for this interchange rate category.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this interchange rate category record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_transaction_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interchange rate category applies to cross-border transactions where the acquiring and issuing banks are in different countries.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount thresholds and interchange rates associated with this category.']
  cvv_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Card Verification Value (CVV) validation is required to qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  domestic_transaction_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interchange rate category applies to domestic transactions where the acquiring and issuing banks are in the same country.']
  downgrade_category_code varchar [note: 'The interchange rate category code to which a transaction will be downgraded if it fails to meet the qualification criteria for this category.']
  downgrade_rules_description varchar [note: 'A detailed textual description of the conditions under which a transaction will be downgraded from this interchange rate category to a lower-tier category.']
  durbin_regulated_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this interchange rate category is subject to Durbin Amendment (Regulation II) caps for debit card transactions issued by regulated financial institutions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this interchange rate category becomes active and applicable to transactions.']
  emv_chip_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip authentication is required for transactions to qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this interchange rate category ceases to be active and is no longer applicable to new transactions.']
  irf_rate_category_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this interchange rate category within the card schemes published rate structure.']
  mcc_range_end varchar [note: 'The ending Merchant Category Code (MCC) in the range of merchant types that qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  mcc_range_start varchar [note: 'The starting Merchant Category Code (MCC) in the range of merchant types that qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form internal notes and comments about this interchange rate category for operational reference and business context.']
  parent_category_code varchar [note: 'The code of the parent or umbrella interchange rate category under which this category is nested in the schemes taxonomy.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'The priority ranking used to determine which interchange rate category applies when a transaction qualifies for multiple categories. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  qualification_criteria_description varchar [note: 'A detailed textual description of all the business and technical criteria that a transaction must meet to qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code indicating the geographic scope where this interchange rate category applies.']
  scheme_publication_reference varchar [note: 'The reference identifier or document name from the card schemes official publication where this interchange rate category is defined (e.g., Visa USA Interchange Reimbursement Fees April 2024).']
  settlement_timeframe_days varchar [note: 'The number of days within which transactions in this interchange rate category must be settled to qualify for the published interchange rate.']
  three_d_secure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3-D Secure authentication is required for card-not-present transactions to qualify for this interchange rate category.']
  transaction_amount_max decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum transaction amount allowed to qualify for this interchange rate category, in the base currency of the scheme.']
  transaction_amount_min decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum transaction amount required to qualify for this interchange rate category, in the base currency of the scheme.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this interchange rate category record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Defines the interchange rate category taxonomy used by each card scheme — e.g., Visa CPS/Retail, Mastercard Merit III, Amex OptBlue tiers. Captures category code, category name, qualifying criteria (card-present/card-not-present, EMV, AVS, MCC range), downgrade rules, and the parent scheme. Enables BIN-level and MCC-level interchange qualification logic.'
}

Table interchange.mdr_config {
  mdr_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MDR configuration record. Primary key.']
  acquiring_bank_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: MDR configuration must associate the acquiring bank legal entity for compliance and fee allocation reporting.']
  acquiring_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: Acquiring banks are legal entities requiring KYC/AML verification tracked as compliance parties. Regulatory reporting (SAR, CTR) and audit trails require party-level compliance data for all acquiring ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: MDR configurations are owned by specific cost centers for P&L attribution. Required for sales team performance tracking, revenue accountability, and merchant portfolio profitability analysis.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: MDR configurations specify pricing for specific currency pairs in cross-border merchant acquiring. Required for currency-specific merchant discount rate calculation and international merchant pricing ']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the acquiring bank or payment facilitator providing merchant services under this MDR configuration.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: mdr_config has mid as a plain denormalized attribute. MDR configuration is merchant-specific and must be linked to the merchant for pricing management, revenue reporting, and MDR rate change workflows']
  participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.participant. Business justification: MDR configurations are established by acquiring participants for their merchant portfolios. Linking mdr_config to the acquiring participant enables participant-level MDR portfolio management and inter']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: MDR configurations define merchant discount rates for specific payment products (credit cards, debit cards, digital wallets). Acquirers price merchants based on payment product type. Essential for mer']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: MDR configurations are scheme-specific — Visa interchange-plus pricing differs from Mastercard. Scheme-level MDR reporting, pricing audits, and acquirer scheme compliance reviews all require this link']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this MDR configuration before it becomes active.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MDR configuration was approved.']
  blended_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'The blended MDR percentage applied to all transactions for this merchant, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0275 for 2.75%).']
  configuration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this MDR configuration record.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the merchant service agreement or contract that governs this MDR configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MDR configuration record was first created in the system.']
  durbin_cap_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum interchange fee amount allowed for Durbin-regulated debit transactions under this configuration.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MDR configuration applies to Durbin-regulated debit transactions (subject to interchange fee caps under Regulation II).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this MDR configuration expires or is superseded. Null indicates open-ended configuration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this MDR configuration becomes active and applicable to merchant transactions.']
  fixed_transaction_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed per-transaction fee charged to the merchant in addition to percentage-based MDR.']
  interchange_plus_margin_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'The margin percentage added on top of interchange fees for interchange-plus pricing model, expressed as a decimal.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MDR configuration record was last updated.']
  maximum_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount for which this MDR configuration applies. Transactions above this threshold may use a different rate.']
  merchant_segment_code varchar [note: 'Classification code representing the merchant segment or tier (e.g., enterprise, SMB, micro-merchant) for tiered pricing strategies.']
  minimum_transaction_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount required for this MDR configuration to apply. Transactions below this threshold may use a different rate.']
  monthly_subscription_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly subscription fee for subscription-based pricing models.']
  monthly_volume_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly transaction volume threshold that triggers rate adjustments or tier changes for volume-based pricing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this MDR configuration, including special terms, exceptions, or business context.']
  pricing_model_type varchar [note: 'The pricing model applied to this merchant: flat rate, tiered pricing, interchange-plus, subscription-based, or blended rate.']
  revenue_share_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of MDR revenue shared with partners or sub-acquirers in payment facilitator or ISO arrangements, expressed as a decimal.']
  tier_1_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'MDR percentage applied to transactions in tier 1 pricing band, expressed as a decimal.']
  tier_1_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Transaction amount threshold for tier 1 pricing band in tiered pricing models. Transactions below this amount use tier 1 rate.']
  tier_2_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'MDR percentage applied to transactions in tier 2 pricing band, expressed as a decimal.']
  tier_2_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Transaction amount threshold for tier 2 pricing band in tiered pricing models. Transactions between tier 1 and tier 2 thresholds use tier 2 rate.']
  tier_3_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'MDR percentage applied to transactions above tier 2 threshold in tiered pricing models, expressed as a decimal.']
  transaction_channel varchar [note: 'Transaction channel or entry mode this MDR configuration applies to (e.g., card-present POS, ecommerce, mail/telephone order).']

  Note: 'Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) configuration master — stores the contractual MDR applied to each merchant or merchant segment. Captures blended rate, interchange-plus margin, tiered pricing bands, effective date range, currency, acquiring bank identifier, and pricing model type (flat, tiered, IC-plus, subscription). SSOT for merchant-facing pricing in the acquiring portfolio.'
}

Table interchange.msf_schedule {
  msf_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant service fee schedule record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: MSF schedules are managed by cost centers for revenue accountability. Links merchant service fee revenue to organizational units for performance measurement and budget tracking.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Merchant service fee schedules vary by currency pair for international merchants accepting multiple currencies. Enables currency-pair-specific fee structures and multi-currency merchant billing.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: MSF schedules define merchant service fee revenue streams that post to specific GL accounts. Required for revenue recognition, financial reporting, and merchant profitability analysis.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_account. Business justification: MSF schedules apply to specific merchant processing accounts (e.g., separate schedules for card-present vs. e-commerce accounts). Linking msf_schedule to merchant_account enables account-level fee sch']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant to whom this fee schedule applies.']
  participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.participant. Business justification: MSF schedules are configured by acquiring participants for their merchant portfolios. Participant-level MSF portfolio management, revenue reporting, and compliance monitoring require linking MSF sched']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Merchant service fee schedules apply to specific payment products. Fee structures vary by product (contactless vs. chip, domestic vs. international). Required for merchant billing systems and contract']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Merchant service fee schedules are risk-adjusted. High-risk merchants pay premium fees, early termination fees, and higher chargeback fees. Risk profile drives fee schedule assignment and approval.']
  annual_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Annual membership or service fee charged to the merchant.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this fee schedule configuration.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fee schedule was approved.']
  avs_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged per Address Verification System check performed during transaction authorization.']
  batch_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged per batch settlement submission by the merchant.']
  chargeback_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged to the merchant for each chargeback dispute processed.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months for which this fee schedule is contractually binding.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fee schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all fee amounts in this schedule are denominated.']
  durbin_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee schedule applies to Durbin Amendment exempt transactions (small issuer or government/reloadable prepaid cards).']
  early_termination_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged if the merchant terminates the agreement before the contract term expires.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this fee schedule expires or is no longer applicable. Null indicates open-ended schedule.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this fee schedule becomes active and applicable to merchant transactions.']
  interchange_pass_through_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether interchange fees are passed through to the merchant at cost (true) or bundled into the service fee (false).']
  international_transaction_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Additional fee charged for cross-border or international payment transactions.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fee schedule record was last modified.']
  monthly_account_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monthly account maintenance or service fee charged to the merchant.']
  monthly_minimum_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum monthly fee that the merchant must pay regardless of transaction volume.']
  msf_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the merchant service fee schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this fee schedule, including special terms or exceptions.']
  pci_compliance_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged for PCI DSS compliance validation, scanning, or non-compliance penalties.']
  per_transaction_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed fee amount charged per transaction processed under this schedule.']
  percentage_rate decimal(8,5) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to transaction amount as part of the merchant service fee (e.g., 2.5% represented as 0.02500).']
  retrieval_request_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged for processing a retrieval request (pre-chargeback inquiry) from the issuing bank.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to recognize revenue from this fee schedule (accrual, cash, deferred, straight-line).']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or label for this merchant service fee schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fee schedule structure (standard, custom, promotional, tiered, interchange-plus, flat-rate).']
  statement_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged for generating and delivering monthly or periodic account statements to the merchant.']
  voice_authorization_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee charged when a merchant obtains authorization via phone rather than electronic terminal.']
  volume_tier_threshold_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monthly transaction volume threshold amount that qualifies the merchant for this fee schedule tier.']
  volume_tier_threshold_count varchar [note: 'Monthly transaction count threshold that qualifies the merchant for this fee schedule tier.']

  Note: 'Merchant Service Fee (MSF) schedule defining the fee structures billed to merchants for payment acceptance services. Stores monthly/annual fee amounts, per-transaction service fees, minimum monthly fees, statement fees, PCI compliance fees, chargeback fees, and the applicable merchant tier or program. Supports revenue recognition for the acquiring portfolio.'
}

Table interchange.scheme_fee_table {
