  transaction_type varchar [note: 'The transaction channel or entry mode to which this exception applies. Value \'all\' indicates exception applies to all transaction types.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this pricing exception record was last modified.']
  volume_tier_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the volume tier threshold.']

  Note: 'Records negotiated pricing exceptions and custom interchange arrangements granted to specific merchants, portfolios, or partners that deviate from standard MDR or MSF schedules. Captures exception type, merchant or portfolio identifier, standard rate, exception rate, basis point discount, approval authority, effective date, expiry date, and business justification. Supports audit and contract compliance tracking.'
}

Table interchange.volume_tier_threshold {
  volume_tier_threshold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the volume tier threshold configuration record.']
  acquirer_id varchar [note: 'Acquiring bank or payment service provider identifier for acquirer-specific tier configurations. Null indicates tier applies across all acquirers.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_policy. Business justification: Volume-based pricing tiers are risk-gated. Threshold access requires risk policy compliance (e.g., fraud rate limits, chargeback ratios). Essential for tiered pricing eligibility and merchant portfoli']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Card scheme or payment network identifier (e.g., VISA, MASTERCARD, AMEX, DISCOVER) to which this tier applies. Null indicates tier applies across all schemes.']
  superseded_by_tier_volume_tier_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the volume_tier_threshold_id that replaces this configuration when a new version is published. Null if this is the current active version.']
  applicable_fixed_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed fee amount per transaction that applies when this tier threshold is met.']
  applicable_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Interchange rate percentage or MDR percentage that applies when this tier threshold is met. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.50%).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this tier configuration before it becomes effective.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who approved this tier configuration for production use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tier configuration was approved.']
  card_present_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier applies to card-present transactions (True), card-not-present transactions (False), or both (Null).']
  card_product_tier varchar [note: 'Card product tier or brand level (e.g., standard, gold, platinum, signature, infinite, world elite). Null indicates tier applies to all product tiers.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment card this tier applies to (debit, credit, prepaid, commercial). Null indicates tier applies to all card types.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the geographic market this tier applies to. Null indicates tier applies globally.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this volume tier threshold record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier applies to cross-border transactions (True), domestic transactions (False), or both (Null).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary thresholds (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for non-monetary thresholds.']
  discount_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Discount percentage applied to standard rates when merchant qualifies for this volume tier. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0050 for 0.50% discount).']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier applies to Durbin Amendment regulated debit transactions (True), non-regulated transactions (False), or both (Null).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this volume tier threshold configuration expires and is no longer applied. Null indicates no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this volume tier threshold configuration becomes effective and begins applying to merchant or issuer volume calculations.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Type of fee structure this tier applies to: IRF (Interchange Reimbursement Fee), MDR (Merchant Discount Rate), MSF (Merchant Service Fee), scheme fee, or assessment fee.']
  lower_bound decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum volume threshold value required to qualify for this tier (inclusive). Null indicates no lower limit.']
  measurement_period varchar [note: 'Time period over which volume is measured to determine tier qualification (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual, rolling 30 days). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annual|rolling_30_days|rolling_90_days — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold bounds (count for transaction volume, currency_amount for TPV, percentage for rate-based thresholds).']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit MCC that this tier applies to. Null indicates tier applies across all MCCs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, business rationale, or special instructions for this volume tier threshold configuration.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple tier configurations could apply to the same transaction. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rebate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed rebate amount provided to merchant or issuer when this volume tier is achieved during the measurement period.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Regional market identifier for multi-country tier configurations (e.g., EMEA, APAC, LATAM). Null indicates no regional restriction.']
  threshold_type varchar [note: 'Type of volume measurement used to determine tier qualification (transaction count, TPV amount, monthly volume, annual volume, or quarterly volume).']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the volume tier (e.g., TIER_1, TIER_GOLD, TIER_PLATINUM).']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of the tier within the pricing structure, where lower numbers typically represent entry-level tiers and higher numbers represent premium tiers.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the volume tier (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum).']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the volume tier threshold configuration.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction this tier applies to (purchase, refund, cash advance, balance inquiry). Null indicates tier applies to all transaction types.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this volume tier threshold record was last modified.']
  upper_bound decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum volume threshold value for this tier (exclusive). Null indicates no upper limit.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this tier configuration, incremented with each modification to support change tracking and audit requirements.']

  Note: 'Volume tier threshold configuration defining the transaction volume or TPV (Total Payment Volume) bands that trigger tiered interchange rates, MDR discounts, or scheme fee rebates. Captures tier level, lower volume bound, upper volume bound, applicable rate or discount, fee type, scheme or acquirer identifier, and effective date. Enables dynamic pricing adjustments based on merchant volume performance.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_case {
  fraud_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the fraud case record.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.application. Business justification: Application fraud (synthetic merchant identity, falsified onboarding documents) is a distinct fraud case type opened before a merchant_id exists. Acquirers and PayFacs must link fraud cases to the ori']
  authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.authorization. Business justification: Fraud cases opened at authorization time require direct reference to the triggering authorization record. Fraud investigation and chargeback workflows depend on auth code, AVS/CVV results, and issuer ']
  bnpl_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bnpl_plan. Business justification: Fraud cases on BNPL transactions require direct plan linkage for credit risk assessment, installment fraud pattern detection, early default analysis, and regulatory reporting on consumer credit fraud ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Internal cost allocation of fraud investigation resources is tracked via cost centers for budgeting and performance reporting.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Cross-border fraud cases must track the currency pair involved for regulatory reporting, fraud pattern analysis by corridor, and AML screening requirements specific to high-risk currency pairs.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device fingerprint record linked to the event.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner Fraud Risk Dashboard aggregates fraud cases per acquiring partner; linking case to ecosystem_partner enables required reporting.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule or model that generated the alert.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory filing and jurisdictional reporting of fraud cases require linking each case to the responsible legal entity.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_location. Business justification: Card scheme fraud reporting (Visa/MC) requires location-level fraud attribution to identify compromised POS terminals or specific store locations. Fraud investigators need to pinpoint which merchant_l']
  mdr_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.mdr_config. Business justification: Acquirers track fraud exposure by MDR configuration to adjust risk-based pricing and negotiate merchant discount rates. Fraud cases directly impact pricing exceptions and contract renewals for high-ri']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant associated with the case.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: Fraud cases involve specific parties (cardholders, merchants, counterparties) whose compliance profile (KYC, AML screening, sanctions status) is essential for investigation and regulatory reporting.']
  payment_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.payment_credential. Business justification: Compromised credential fraud cases require linking directly to the specific payment credential (tokenized card, biometric) that was compromised. payment_instrument_token is a denormalized string repre']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Required for regulatory fraud reporting and risk scoring that depend on the specific payment product (card, BNPL, A2A) used in the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'FK to transaction.payment_txn.payment_txn_id — MUST-HAVE: Enables linking a fraud case to its triggering transaction — essential for fraud investigation, loss calculation, and network reporting.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: Fraud cases on multi-currency transactions require the FX rate applied at transaction time for accurate loss calculation, chargeback amount validation, and regulatory reporting of cross-border fraud e']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Investigation reports require linking each fraud case to the original gateway request that initiated the disputed transaction.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Risk assessment reports require linking each fraud case to the party\'s risk profile to correlate fraud outcomes with risk scores.']
  routing_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.routing_decision. Business justification: Case reviews often examine the routing decision that selected the acquirer to understand risk exposure.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Regulatory loss and risk reporting are often aggregated by card scheme; associating each fraud case with its scheme enables accurate reporting.']
  scheme_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_invoice. Business justification: Fraud chargebacks appear as adjustments on scheme invoices. Fraud teams reconcile cases against scheme billing for dispute resolution and to validate chargeback reimbursement fees—required for scheme ']
  sub_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.sub_merchant. Business justification: Visa/MC PayFac rules require fraud monitoring and case attribution at the sub-merchant level, not just the parent merchant. PayFac acquirers must report sub-merchant fraud rates separately for complia']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the fraud alert.']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_type. Business justification: fraud_case needs a classification of the case type; linking to fraud_type provides hierarchy and risk weighting.']
  watchlist_fraud_watchlist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_watchlist. Business justification: fraud_case may reference a watchlist entry that triggered the case; linking to fraud_watchlist enables traceability.']
  aml_alert boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case triggered an AML screening alert.']
  blocklist_hit boolean [note: 'True if the entity matched an internal or external blocklist.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable case number used in investigations and reporting.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fraud case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of fraud scenario prompting the case.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the suspicious transaction originated.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the chargeback associated with the case.']
  chargeback_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the chargeback amount.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case reached a terminal state (e.g., resolved, rejected).']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory compliance review for the case.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the fraud case record was first created in the system.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'System or channel that initially flagged the suspicious activity.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the suspicious event was detected.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the case was escalated.']
  exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary loss if fraud is successful.']
  fraud_category varchar [note: 'High‑level fraud taxonomy for the case.']
  investigation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the investigation was concluded.']
  investigation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the formal investigation of the case began.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the associated account or card was blocked pending investigation.']
  is_chargeback_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a chargeback was filed related to the case.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'True when the case has been escalated to senior investigators or external teams.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'True if the case was concluded as confirmed fraud.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case is flagged as high risk for immediate action.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Analyst free‑form notes documenting investigation details.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the case for handling.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount successfully recovered from fraudster or merchant.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome after case investigation.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score generated by scoring models (0‑100).']
  sar_filed boolean [note: 'True if a Suspicious Activity Report was filed for this case.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity rating indicating potential impact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent time the case record was modified.']

  Note: 'Core operational record representing a fraud investigation lifecycle from initial detection through remediation. Tracks case type (card-not-present, account takeover, synthetic identity, first-party fraud, etc.), case status, assigned investigator, detection source, financial exposure, recovery amount, and resolution outcome. SSOT for fraud case management in the Fraud Detection and Prevention Platform.'
}

Table fraud.alert {
  alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the fraud alert record.']
  aml_screening_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_aml_screening_result. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires attaching AML screening results to each fraud alert for audit trails and SAR/STR filing.']
  authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.authorization. Business justification: Fraud alerts are generated during authorization processing based on auth response codes, AVS/CVV results, and 3DS outcomes. Real-time fraud decisioning systems correlate alerts to specific authorizati']
  billing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.billing. Business justification: Fraud alerts trigger billing adjustments and chargeback processing. Fraud operations teams trace alerts to billing cycles for revenue impact analysis and dispute reconciliation—critical for acquirer P']
  bnpl_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bnpl_plan. Business justification: Real-time fraud alerts on BNPL purchases need plan context for velocity checks against plan-specific limits, installment fraud pattern matching, and credit exposure monitoring during transaction autho']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder (primary account holder).']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.cross_border_payment. Business justification: Real‑time fraud alerts for cross‑border payments need the payment ID to fetch transaction details for immediate response.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device fingerprint record associated with the transaction.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Regulatory and SLA alerts are sent to the partner owning the merchant; linking alerts to partner supports AML/Fraud notification workflow.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_endpoint. Business justification: Forensic analysis of alerts needs the exact network endpoint that processed the transaction, supporting root‑cause investigations.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule that triggered the alert, if rule‑based.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_location. Business justification: Real-time fraud monitoring systems generate alerts at the terminal/location level for geographic clustering analysis and hotspot detection. Fraud operations teams need location-level alert attribution']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ML model that produced the fraud score, if applicable.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: Fraud alerts are generated for specific parties whose compliance profile (risk tier, PEP status, sanctions screening) determines alert disposition and escalation requirements.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Alerts must reference the payment product to apply product‑specific risk thresholds and generate compliance reports.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Fraud alerts are generated on payment_txn records during payment processing. fraud_alert.transaction_id links to transaction.transaction but payment_txn carries channel, instrument, and processing-spe']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: Fraud alerts on FX transactions need the applied rate to detect rate manipulation fraud, validate converted amounts against market rates, and trigger alerts on suspicious rate deviations.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Alert triage uses the exact gateway request payload to reproduce the event and assess fraud indicators.']
  response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_response. Business justification: Linking alerts to the gateway response enables auditors to see the response code and fraud flag that triggered the alert.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Real-time fraud monitoring systems route alerts to analysts based on entity risk tier (VIP/high-risk get priority SLA). Alert decisioning (auto-block vs manual review) depends on risk profile threshol']
  sanctions_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_check. Business justification: Sanctions screening is executed as part of fraud alert processing; linking records the check result for regulatory filing.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Scheme-specific fraud alert reporting and scheme-mandated notification SLAs (e.g., Visa CAMS, Mastercard SAFE) require direct scheme linkage on fraud_alert. The existing `payment_network` plain-text c']
  score_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.score_event. Business justification: A fraud alert is triggered by a fraud scoring evaluation (score_event). The alert\'s triggered_score and score_threshold are denormalized copies of data owned by score_event (fraud_score, score_band th']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the transaction that triggered the alert.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Category of the alert indicating the source logic (rule, machine‑learning score, velocity control, etc.).']
  auth_response_code varchar [note: 'ISO‑8583 response code returned by the issuer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 00|05|12|13|14|15|30|41|43|51|54|55|57|58|62|63|65|75|76|77|78|79|80|81|82|83|84|85|86|87|88|89|90|91|92|93|94|95|96|97|98|99 — 42 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which the transaction was initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert record was first created in the data lake.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the issuer for a declined transaction, if applicable.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fraud alert was generated by the detection engine.']
  fraud_alert_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alert within the case management workflow.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City name of the transaction source location, if available.']
  geo_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the transaction source.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the transaction originated.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transaction is confirmed as fraudulent after review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes added by analysts during investigation.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used for the transaction (e.g., card, bank transfer).']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Recommended action taken by the system or analyst in response to the alert.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rule that caused the alert.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity indicating potential impact of the alert.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that emitted the alert (e.g., gateway, fraud_engine).']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction in the transaction currency.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the underlying transaction occurred.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the alert record.']

  Note: 'Real-time fraud alert generated by the fraud detection engine when a transaction or account activity breaches a scoring threshold or rule condition. Captures alert type, severity level, triggering rule or model identifier, alert status (open, reviewed, escalated, closed), and the associated transaction or account reference. Feeds the case management workflow.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_rule {
  fraud_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the fraud rule.']
  bnpl_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bnpl_plan. Business justification: BNPL fraud rules (installment velocity limits, early default patterns, credit limit breach detection, application fraud rules) are plan-specific and require direct association for rule configuration a']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.card_program. Business justification: Fraud rules are frequently card-program-specific (BIN-based rules, contactless fraud limits, digital wallet authentication requirements, EMV vs. magstripe rules) and require direct program linkage for']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: FX‑specific fraud rules are defined per currency pair; the rule must reference the pair for correct applicability.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Fraud rules are commonly partner-specific in payments fintech (e.g., different risk thresholds for Visa vs Mastercard processors, wallet providers like Apple Pay). Partner-specific fraud controls are ']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.endpoint. Business justification: Fraud rules are operationally scoped to specific network endpoints — stricter thresholds apply on high-risk or cross-border endpoints. Endpoint-specific fraud rule configuration is a named operational']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_policy. Business justification: Fraud rules enforce risk policy limits (max transaction amounts, velocity thresholds, exposure caps). Policy changes trigger rule recalibration. Audit and compliance require traceability from rule to ']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each fraud rule is derived from a specific regulatory obligation; linking enables compliance impact analysis and rule audits.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Payment schemes have distinct fraud rule sets; linking rules to scheme_id allows scheme‑specific rule activation and compliance reporting.']
  txn_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.txn_type. Business justification: Fraud rules are scoped to transaction types in real fraud management systems — CNP rules differ from card-present rules, recurring transaction rules differ from one-time payment rules. Fraud rule conf']
  action varchar [note: 'Action taken when the rule condition evaluates to true.']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Executable logical expression evaluated by the rule engine (e.g., DSL or SQL‑like syntax).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the rule becomes active in production.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the rule is no longer applied (null for open‑ended).']
  fraud_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  is_test_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is deployed in a test environment only.']
  max_decline_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for automatic decline actions.']
  max_decline_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the max decline amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional comments or operational guidance.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple rules match (lower = higher priority).']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum fraud risk score required for the rule to fire.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business-friendly short code used to reference the rule in operational dashboards and logs.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule\'s purpose, scope and business rationale.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fraud detection rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rule defining its evaluation logic.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business impact rating of the rule when triggered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  velocity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed within the velocity window.']
  velocity_window_seconds varchar [note: 'Time window over which transaction count is evaluated for velocity rules.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version of the rule definition.']

  Note: 'Configuration record for a fraud detection rule deployed in the rule engine. Defines rule name, rule type (velocity, threshold, pattern, blocklist), condition logic expression, action (decline, flag, challenge, review), effective date range, channel applicability (POS, CNP, ATM, digital wallet), and rule owner. Supports versioned rule lifecycle management.'
}

Table fraud.rule_version {
  rule_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each version of a fraud rule configuration.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent fraud rule to which this version belongs.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: Certain fraud rule versions need to reference the applicable FX fee schedule for cost‑based risk calculations.']
  rate_margin_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate_margin_config. Business justification: Rule versions may depend on margin configuration parameters that affect FX pricing and fraud thresholds.']
  action_parameters varchar [note: 'Serialized parameters that define actions taken when the rule fires (e.g., block, challenge, alert).']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule version became effective for evaluation.']
  applies_to_payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument categories that the rule evaluates.']
  applies_to_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the geographic region where the rule is active.']
  applies_to_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Transaction event types to which the rule is applied.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the analyst who approved this rule version.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule version received formal approval.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual list of regulatory or internal compliance controls that this rule satisfies (e.g., PCI DSS, AML, PSD2).']
  condition_logic varchar [note: 'Serialized representation (e.g., JSON or DSL) of the rule\'s conditional expressions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when this rule version record was first created in the system.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule version was retired or superseded (nullable if still active).']
  is_fallback_rule boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this rule serves as a fallback when no other rule matches.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the most recent update.']
  max_amount_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary ceiling for cumulative transaction amount in a day that, if exceeded, triggers the rule.']
  max_transactions_per_minute varchar [note: 'Velocity control limit for the number of transactions allowed per minute before the rule triggers.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold that the computed risk score must exceed to trigger the rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fraud rule.']
  rule_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rule definition (built in‑house, supplied by a partner, or mandated by regulation).']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rule logic (e.g., velocity control, device fingerprint, 3‑DS outcome, behavioral analytics, machine‑learning model, or custom).']
  rule_version_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule purpose and scope.']
  rule_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rule version.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the risk score threshold (points or percent).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to this rule version record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the rule configuration.']

  Note: 'Versioned history of fraud rule configurations, capturing each published version of a fraud rule with its full condition logic, action parameters, activation timestamp, deactivation timestamp, and the analyst who approved the version. Enables audit trail and rollback capability for rule changes.'
}

Partner-specific velocity limits are e']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_rule. Business justification: A velocity control is a specific type of fraud rule configuration — it enforces transaction frequency and volume limits that are defined within the fraud rule engine. The rule_version string on fraud_']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Velocity controls vary by product type; linking enables enforcement of product‑specific transaction limits.']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_policy. Business justification: Velocity controls operationalize risk policy exposure limits and concentration thresholds. Policy defines max transaction counts/amounts per time window that controls enforce. Regulatory reporting req']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Velocity controls are scheme-specific: Visa and Mastercard mandate different velocity thresholds and compliance windows. Scheme-scoped velocity control configuration is a named compliance and operatio']
  threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.threshold. Business justification: Velocity controls enforce specific threshold values for breach detection. Each control references threshold configuration (value, unit, escalation action). Threshold changes propagate to controls. Ess']
  txn_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.txn_type. Business justification: Velocity controls are configured per transaction type in fraud management platforms — contactless tap limits differ from CNP velocity limits. fraud_velocity_control has applicable_channels but no txn_']
  applicable_channels varchar [note: 'Payment channels to which the control applies.']
  applicable_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Payment instrument types covered by the control.']
  breach_action varchar [note: 'Action taken when the threshold is exceeded: block transaction, flag for review, invoke step‑up authentication, or generate an alert.']
  breach_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the control has been breached since activation.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates regulatory compliance relevance of the control.']
  control_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code used to reference the control in operational interfaces.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the velocity control rule.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was first created.']
  effective_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the control expires; null for indefinite.']
  effective_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the control becomes effective.']
  fraud_velocity_control_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the control\'s purpose and business rationale.']
  fraud_velocity_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the velocity control.']

  last_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent occurrence where the control was breached.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Relative priority (1‑10) used when multiple controls conflict; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  risk_score_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weight applied to the control\'s outcome when calculating an overall fraud risk score.']
  scope_entity varchar [note: 'Entity to which the velocity limit applies (e.g., individual cardholder, merchant, BIN, device, IP address, or channel).']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the threshold value: number of transactions or monetary currency.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric limit that triggers the breach action; interpreted according to threshold_unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']

  Note: 'Velocity control configuration defining transaction frequency and volume limits applied per cardholder, merchant, BIN, or device within a rolling time window. Captures control type (count, amount, unique merchant), window duration, threshold value, breach action (block, flag, step-up auth), and applicability scope. Core fraud prevention mechanism for CNP and POS channels.'
}

Table fraud.velocity_breach {
  velocity_breach_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the velocity control breach event.']
  alert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_alert. Business justification: A velocity breach event triggers a fraud alert when the breach threshold is exceeded. Linking velocity_breach to the resulting fraud_alert closes the operational loop between breach detection and aler']
  authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.authorization. Business justification: Velocity breaches are detected during authorization processing. Fraud teams need to identify which authorization triggered a velocity breach to analyze decline reasons and tune velocity controls. Auth']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device fingerprint used in risk assessment.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_event. Business justification: Velocity breaches are material risk events that feed enterprise risk dashboards, exposure monitoring, and regulatory reporting (operational risk capital). Risk event aggregation requires linking breac']
  fraud_velocity_control_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the velocity control rule that was breached.']
  gateway_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.request. Business justification: Velocity breaches are detected during real-time request processing. Linking to the originating gateway request is essential for breach remediation, merchant notification of blocked transactions, regul']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual transaction that caused the breach.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Automated or manual response applied after the breach (e.g., block, flag, challenge).']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the response action was executed.']
  bin_number varchar [note: 'First six digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breach event.']
  breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the velocity breach was detected.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel of the transaction that triggered the breach.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates if the breach has regulatory or internal compliance implications.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the breached velocity control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Category of the control – e.g., transaction count, monetary amount, frequency, or custom value.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach record was first persisted.']
  detection_engine varchar [note: 'Engine that evaluated the transaction – real‑time rule engine, batch processor, or ML model.']
  entity_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the entity such as masked PAN (last 4), MID, or device token.']
  entity_reference varchar [note: 'System identifier of the entity (e.g., cardholder_id, merchant_id) that caused the breach.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity that triggered the breach (cardholder, merchant, device, BIN, or account).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address observed for the transaction that triggered the breach.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was classified as fraudulent.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the breach originated or is subject to regulation.']
  observed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual count or monetary amount observed that exceeded the threshold.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score associated with the transaction at detection time.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the velocity control rule at the time of breach.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that generated the breach record.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the threshold – either a simple count or a currency amount.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Configured limit for the control (count or amount) that should not be exceeded.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction that caused the breach.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the breach record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or client user‑agent string associated with the transaction.']
  velocity_breach_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing the context or rationale of the breach.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a velocity control breach event, capturing which velocity control was breached, the entity (cardholder, merchant, device, BIN) that triggered the breach, the observed count or amount versus the configured threshold, the breach timestamp, and the resulting action taken (block, flag, challenge). Feeds fraud alert generation.'
}

Used for device-based fraud detection and account takeover prevention.'
}

PSD2 SCA compliance reporting requires linking 3DS outcomes to the specific authorization record. Payments experts universall']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.card_program. Business justification: 3DS authentication results are card-program-specific (different programs have different SCA requirements, liability shift rules, exemption eligibility) and require direct program linkage for authentic']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: auth_3ds_result stores device fingerprint as a string; linking to device_fingerprint table normalizes device data and removes redundant column.']
  fraud_sca_challenge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_sca_challenge. Business justification: A 3DS authentication result is the outcome of an SCA challenge process. The fraud_sca_challenge record captures the challenge initiation and method, while auth_3ds_result captures the authentication o']
  gateway_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.request. Business justification: 3DS authentication results are generated from gateway authentication requests. Critical for SCA compliance tracking, linking authentication outcomes to originating API requests. Required for PSD2 audi']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: 3DS authentication results must meet PSD2 SCA regulatory obligations for liability shift and exemption handling. Essential for regulatory compliance reporting and scheme rule adherence.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: 3DS authentication is scheme-specific (Visa Secure, Mastercard Identity Check). Scheme-level 3DS compliance reporting, liability shift analysis, and ECI indicator interpretation require direct scheme ']
  acquirer_bin varchar [note: 'BIN of the acquiring bank that processed the transaction.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction associated with this 3DS authentication.']
  authentication_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of how many authentication attempts were made for this transaction.']
  authentication_error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code returned when authentication fails.']
  authentication_error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable error message describing the authentication failure.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used for authentication: frictionless, challenge, or fallback.']
  authentication_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the 3-D Secure authentication attempt.']
  authentication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the 3-D Secure authentication event occurred.']

  cardholder_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the cardholder at the time of authentication.']
  challenge_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a challenge was required, requested, or not needed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the authentication outcome meets internal and external compliance standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this 3DS result record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  eci_indicator varchar [note: 'ECI code indicating the level of liability shift and authentication strength.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by the fraud engine for this authentication event.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'True if the authentication resulted in a successful liability shift.']
  issuer_bin varchar [note: 'BIN of the issuing bank that issued the card.']
  liability_shift boolean [note: 'True if liability for the transaction shifted to the issuer per 3DS outcome.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or analyst comments.']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time elapsed between authentication request and final response, measured in milliseconds.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this authentication must be included in regulatory reporting (e.g., AML, PCI).']
  risk_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision based on fraud score and business rules.']
  sca_exemption_type varchar [note: 'Type of SCA exemption applied to the transaction, if any.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the 3DS result (e.g., gateway, digital_wallet).']
  three_ds_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3-D Secure protocol used for the authentication.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']

  Note: '3-D Secure authentication outcome record for a payment transaction. Captures 3DS version (1.0, 2.1, 2.2), authentication status (authenticated, attempted, failed, not enrolled), ECI indicator, authentication value (CAVV/AAV), DS transaction ID, ACS transaction ID, challenge indicator, SCA exemption type applied, and authentication timestamp. SSOT for 3DS outcomes in the fraud domain.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_sca_challenge {
  fraud_sca_challenge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SCA challenge record.']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.card_program. Business justification: SCA challenges are card-program-specific (contactless exemptions, digital wallet authentication flows, program-level SCA policies) and require direct program association for challenge configuration, e']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: fraud_sca_challenge stores device fingerprint as a string; linking to device_fingerprint table normalizes device data and removes redundant column.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud rule that triggered the SCA challenge.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  primary_fraud_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) subject to the SCA challenge.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: SCA challenges must comply with PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication regulatory obligations. Links authentication events to specific regulatory requirements for exemption validation and audit.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: SCA challenge flows and exemption rules are scheme-mandated under PSD2. Scheme-specific SCA configuration (exemption thresholds, challenge methods, regulatory reporting) requires direct scheme linkage']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction that triggered the SCA challenge.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the underlying transaction.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the issuer after successful challenge.']
  challenge_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the attempt for the same transaction.']
  challenge_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the SCA challenge: passed, failed, or abandoned by the cardholder.']
  challenge_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in milliseconds between initiation and completion of the challenge.']
  challenge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the challenge.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCA challenge was completed (success, failure, or abandonment).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCA challenge record was first created in the data lake.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'PSD2 exemption applied to the transaction, if any.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of why the challenge failed, if applicable.']
  idempotency_key varchar [note: 'Unique key to ensure the challenge request is processed exactly once.']
  initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCA challenge was presented to the cardholder.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address of the device that performed the SCA challenge.']
  is_challenge_abandoned boolean [note: 'True when the cardholder abandoned the challenge.']
  is_challenge_blocked boolean [note: 'True when the challenge was blocked by policy or system error.']
  is_challenge_exempted boolean [note: 'True when an SCA exemption was applied and no challenge was required.']
  is_challenge_successful boolean [note: 'True when the challenge outcome is passed.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  payment_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment instrument used for the transaction.']
  regulatory_context varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the challenge, e.g., PSD2.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by the fraud detection engine prior to the challenge.']
  sca_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the SCA challenge (One‑Time Password, biometric, push notification, knowledge‑based).']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the SCA challenge was presented (web, mobile app, POS terminal, API).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SCA challenge record.']

  Note: 'Strong Customer Authentication challenge record capturing the SCA method used (OTP, biometric, push notification, knowledge-based), challenge outcome (passed, failed, abandoned), SCA exemption applied (low-value, TRA, trusted beneficiary, recurring), PSD2 regulatory context, challenge initiation timestamp, and completion timestamp. Supports PSD2 SCA compliance reporting.'
}

Table fraud.blocklist_entry {
  blocklist_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each blocklist record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Blocklists are commonly maintained per partner in payments (e.g., processor-specific merchant blocklists, acquirer-managed card blocklists). Partner-managed blocklists are standard practice for distri']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: Blocklist entries should reference the compliance party record for the blocked entity to maintain single source of truth for entity risk profile, sanctions status, and PEP screening.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Blocklist decisions are risk-based; each entry should reference the risk profile that justified the block. Required for audit, customer appeals, periodic review workflows, and regulatory examination o']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Scheme-specific blocklists are a real operational pattern: a BIN or entity may be blocked only on the Visa network but permitted on Mastercard. Scheme-scoped blocklist management enables targeted bloc']
  block_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the entity was placed on the blocklist.']
  blocking_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the block action (e.g., decline transaction, flag for review, step‑up authentication, monitor only).']
  blocklist_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the blocklist record.']
  blocklist_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the blocklist record to support change tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blocklist entry was initially created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the block becomes active.']
  entity_identifier varchar [note: 'Tokenized or hashed value that uniquely identifies the blocked entity within the system.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity being blocked (e.g., PAN, BIN, IP address, email, phone, merchant MID).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the block expires or is scheduled to be removed (nullable for indefinite blocks).']
  is_global boolean [note: 'True if the block applies across all jurisdictions; false if limited to a specific jurisdiction.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the jurisdiction where the block applies.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent periodic review of the blocklist entry.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the block.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest review (e.g., reviewed, not reviewed, escalated).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the entity at the time of blocklist entry (higher indicates greater risk).']
  source_of_addition varchar [note: 'Origin of the blocklist entry (e.g., manual entry, automated fraud rule, network advisory).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the blocklist entry.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the blocklist entry.']

  Note: 'Blocklist record for an entity (PAN, BIN, device fingerprint, IP address, email, phone, merchant MID, IBAN) that has been flagged for blocking or enhanced scrutiny. Captures entity type, entity identifier (tokenized/hashed), blocklist reason code, source of addition (manual, automated rule, network advisory), effective date, expiry date, and blocking scope (decline, flag, step-up). SSOT for fraud blocklist management.'
}

Table fraud.score_event {
  score_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique identifier for the fraud scoring event record.']
  authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.authorization. Business justification: Fraud scoring engines evaluate transactions at authorization time. score_event records the fraud score produced during authorization processing. Fraud analytics dashboards and model performance report']
  bnpl_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bnpl_plan. Business justification: Fraud scoring for BNPL transactions requires plan context (credit limit, installment count, interest rate, repayment terms) for accurate risk assessment, credit exposure calculation, and early default']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (or account holder) involved in the transaction.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device fingerprint record associated with the transaction.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_rule. Business justification: A score event is evaluated against a specific fraud rule configuration. The rule_set_version string on score_event is a denormalized reference to the fraud_rule version. Adding fraud_rule_id creates a']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_model. Business justification: Fraud scoring uses a specific risk model; linking enables audit of model version per score event for compliance.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Score events need product context for analytics and to trigger product‑specific actions.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Fraud scoring engines score payment_txn records directly. score_event.transaction_id links to transaction.transaction but payment_txn is the atomic payment processing record. Fraud analytics and model']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Fraud scoring models incorporate the FX rate at transaction time to assess risk of currency conversion anomalies.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Scoring events evaluate specific entity risk profiles. Linking enables profile-level score history, trend analysis (score drift detection), model performance monitoring by risk tier, and regulatory mo']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the fraud scoring.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which the transaction originated.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the event complies with applicable regulatory requirements.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Actual decision taken by downstream systems after applying the recommended action.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Subsystem that generated the fraud scoring event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the fraud scoring evaluation was performed.']
  feature_set_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the input feature set used for the scoring run, enabling reproducibility.']
  fraud_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric fraud risk score produced by the model (higher indicates greater risk).']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code derived from the IP address.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address of the transaction request.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was automatically blocked by the system.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is classified as high risk.']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken to compute the fraud score.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the fraud scoring model.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to fund the transaction.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the primary reason for the fraud decision.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Suggested operational action based on the fraud evaluation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fraud score event record was first persisted.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fraud score event record.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if the event must be included in regulatory reporting feeds.']
  score_band varchar [note: 'Categorical band derived from the fraud score for operational handling.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction being evaluated.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a fraud scoring evaluation performed on a transaction or account event. Captures the scoring model identifier, model version, input feature set hash, composite fraud score, score band (low, medium, high, critical), scoring latency in milliseconds, and the recommended action output. This is an OPERATIONAL event record — not an analytics aggregate — representing each real-time scoring invocation.'
}

  loss_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the fraud loss record.']

Critical for temp']

  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner Chargeback Reconciliation Report needs to attribute loss amounts to the responsible partner for settlement and liability.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_event. Business justification: Realized losses are material risk events triggering risk model recalibration, exposure limit reviews, and regulatory capital calculations (operational risk under Basel). Linking enables loss-based ris']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Financial reporting of fraud loss expense requires posting each loss to a GL expense account for SOX compliance and audit trails.']
  investigation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.investigation. Business justification: A financial loss record is quantified and confirmed during a fraud investigation. While loss already links to fraud_case, the investigation record contains the specific outcome_decision and outcome_re']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Fraud losses must reference the specific journal entry recording the write-off or recovery for SOX audit trail, GL reconciliation, and regulatory compliance. Essential accounting control linking fraud']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Losses must be attributed to the specific legal entity bearing the loss for consolidated financial statements, inter-company reconciliation, jurisdiction-specific regulatory reporting, and entity-leve']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., 2023-Q1) to which the loss is booked for financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loss record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency in which the loss is measured.']
  gross_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the fraudulent transaction before any recovery.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the regulatory jurisdiction governing the loss.']

  net_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final loss after subtracting recovered amount from gross loss.']
  occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the fraudulent loss was realized in the payment flow.']
  recovered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount successfully recovered through chargeback, insurance, or law‑enforcement actions.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to recover funds from the fraudulent loss.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference code for the loss, used in reporting and audit trails.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loss has been reported to a regulator (true) or not (false).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the loss record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the loss record.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date the loss was formally written off in the accounting system.']

  Note: 'Financial loss record associated with a confirmed fraud case, capturing gross fraud loss amount, recovered amount, net loss, loss currency, loss category (card-present, CNP, account takeover, synthetic identity), write-off date, recovery method (chargeback, insurance, law enforcement), and accounting period. SSOT for fraud financial impact tracking and regulatory loss reporting.'
}

Table fraud.type {
  type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each fraud type. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup for fraud classification.']
  parent_fraud_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent fraud type for hierarchical classification.']

  detection_method varchar [note: 'Primary detection technique used for this fraud type.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the fraud type definition becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the fraud type definition expires (null if indefinite).']
  fraud_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping similar fraud types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: card_not_present|card_present|account_takeover|synthetic_identity|merchant_fraud|money_mule|phishing|first_party — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fraud_type_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the fraud type (e.g., CNP for Card-Not-Present).']
  fraud_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the fraud scenario and typical characteristics.']
  fraud_type_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fraud type.']
  fraud_type_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the fraud type definition.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fraud type is currently active in the system.']
  mitigation_action varchar [note: 'Standard remediation action taken when this fraud type is detected.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel where the fraud type is observed.']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used in regulatory reports (e.g., Visa reason code, Mastercard reason code).']
  requires_manual_review boolean [note: 'Indicates if cases of this fraud type automatically trigger manual investigation.']
  risk_score_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default weight applied to this fraud type in aggregate risk scoring models.']
  typical_loss_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary loss historically associated with this fraud type.']
  typical_loss_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the typical loss amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fraud type record.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of fraud types recognized by the platform, including type code, type name (card-not-present, card-present counterfeit, lost-and-stolen, account takeover, synthetic identity, first-party, merchant fraud, money mule, phishing), parent category, applicable payment channel, and regulatory reporting code mapping (e.g., Visa reason code, Mastercard reason code). Used for consistent fraud categorization across cases and reporting.'
}

Table fraud.watchlist {
  watchlist_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each watchlist entry.']

  contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred contact channel for the watchlist entity used for notifications or escalations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the watchlist entry was initially created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the watchlist entry becomes effective.']
  entity_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the entity (e.g., PAN, MID, device fingerprint, IP address, email domain).']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity placed on the watchlist (e.g., cardholder, merchant, device, IP range, email domain).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the watchlist entry expires or is scheduled for removal.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the watchlist entry applies globally across all jurisdictions.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction relevant to the watchlist entry.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review action performed on the entry.']
  monitoring_level varchar [note: 'Intensity of monitoring applied to the entity (passive = observation only, active = heightened scrutiny, step_up = requires step‑up authentication).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the watchlist entry.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for placing the entity on the watchlist (e.g., high velocity, suspicious geography, known fraud pattern).']
  review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next manual review of the watchlist entry.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the entity at the time of watchlist inclusion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the watchlist entry.']
  watchlist_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the watchlist entry (internal risk team, external network advisory, law‑enforcement notification).']
  watchlist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the watchlist entry.']

  Note: 'Watchlist of entities (cardholders, merchants, devices, IP ranges, email domains) under enhanced fraud monitoring without full blocking. Captures entity type, entity identifier, watchlist reason, monitoring level (passive, active, step-up auth required), watchlist source (internal, network advisory, law enforcement), effective date, review date, and assigned analyst. Distinct from blocklist — watchlist entities are monitored but not blocked.'
}

Table risk.risk_risk_profile {
  risk_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the risk profile record.']
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.correspondent_bank. Business justification: Risk profiles evaluate correspondent banking relationships for credit risk, sanctions compliance, and counterparty exposure limits. Core treasury risk management requires tracking risk assessment per ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the counterparty (merchant, cardholder, partner, issuer, or acquirer) to which this risk profile belongs.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk model (e.g., model name or UUID) that produced the risk_score.']
  payment_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.payment_corridor. Business justification: Risk profiles assess entity risk per payment corridor for cross-border compliance, AML screening, and exposure management. Corridor-specific risk scoring is required for regulatory reporting and trans']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk policy governing this profile.']
  aml_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting anti‑money‑laundering risk based on screening and transaction patterns.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall compliance rating derived from regulatory checks, KYC, AML, and sanctions results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk profile record was first created.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) indicating likelihood of fraudulent activity associated with the counterparty.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of Know‑Your‑Customer verification.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent comprehensive risk assessment.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled risk review.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the counterparty indicating its role in the payment ecosystem.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel used to reach the counterparty for risk communications.']
  primary_contact_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contact detail corresponding to the selected primary_contact_method (e.g., email address or phone number).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is subject to special regulatory reporting.']
  risk_approval_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the risk approval.']
  risk_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manual risk approval is required for this counterparty.']
  risk_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required risk approval.']
  risk_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk approval decision was made.']
  risk_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to generate the risk score.']
  risk_assessment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the risk assessment data.']
  risk_decision_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the risk decision applied to the counterparty.']
  risk_exposure_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary exposure the organization is willing to accept for this counterparty.']
  risk_exposure_used decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current monetary exposure incurred by the counterparty.']
  risk_flags varchar [note: 'Delimited list of risk flags or alerts (e.g., \'high_velocity|geo_mismatch\').']
  risk_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any risk‑related field.']
  risk_limit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the risk_exposure_limit that is currently utilized.']
  risk_model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the risk model used for scoring.']
  risk_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version of the risk policy applied to this profile.']
  risk_profile_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the risk profile record.']
  risk_review_notes varchar [note: 'Analyst comments and observations from the latest risk review.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score (0‑100) derived from underwriting models, fraud signals, AML checks, and other risk inputs.']
  risk_score_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the current risk_score was calculated.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from risk_score thresholds used for decisioning and monitoring.']
  sanction_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the counterparty.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version number for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Master record for each counterparty (merchant, cardholder, partner) capturing aggregated risk scores, risk tier classification, risk appetite alignment, and overall risk posture. SSOT for entity-level risk assessment used in underwriting, authorization decisioning, and ongoing portfolio monitoring.'
}

  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory compliance requires each risk rule to be traceable to its governing regulatory obligation for audit and reporting.']

  threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.threshold. Business justification: risk_rule has inline threshold_value and threshold_unit attributes that duplicate the threshold table\'s threshold_value and unit_of_measure. Normalizing via FK to threshold eliminates this redundancy,']
  action varchar [note: 'Automated action taken when the rule condition is met.']
  applicable_channel varchar [note: 'Payment channel (e.g., online, mobile, POS) where the rule is evaluated.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating geographic scope of the rule.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes for audit or change‑management purposes.']

  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code associated with monetary thresholds.']

  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires or is no longer applied (null if open‑ended).']

  is_compliance_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule enforces regulatory compliance.']
  is_fraud_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is part of the fraud detection engine.']
  last_evaluated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction evaluation against this rule.']
  logic varchar [note: 'Executable expression or script that defines the rule condition.']
  max_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound amount for which the rule applies (optional).']
  min_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound amount for which the rule applies (optional).']
  parameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded parameter set used by the rule logic.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple rules fire (higher = higher priority).']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the rule definition is retained for audit purposes.']
  risk_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule logic and purpose.']
  risk_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
