  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this rule configuration is currently active and being applied in dispute processing workflows.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this rule was last executed in a dispute processing workflow, recorded in ISO 8601 format.']
  max_dispute_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of disputes this rule can process within a defined time window, used for rate limiting or risk control.']
  merchant_segment varchar [note: 'Merchant classification or segment to which this rule applies, enabling segment-specific dispute handling strategies. May reference MCC (Merchant Category Code) ranges or internal merchant tiers.']
  mid varchar [note: 'Specific Merchant Identification Number to which this rule applies. Null indicates the rule applies broadly per merchant segment or globally.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user or system process that last modified this rule configuration record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dispute rule configuration record was last modified, recorded in ISO 8601 format.']
  notification_recipient_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of email addresses or user identifiers to be notified when this rule is triggered.']
  notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a notification must be sent to stakeholders when this rule is triggered.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for rule evaluation order when multiple rules may apply to the same dispute. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Chargeback or dispute reason code to which this rule applies. May be a specific code or a wildcard pattern for multiple codes. Aligns with card scheme reason code taxonomies.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the dispute rule configuration, used for external reference and system integration.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the rule logic, business rationale, and intended use case for this dispute rule configuration.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the dispute rule configuration describing its purpose and scope.']
  rule_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or team responsible for maintaining and governing this dispute rule configuration.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispute rule configuration indicating whether it is actively applied in dispute processing.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dispute rule configuration indicating the type of automated action or decision logic it governs.']
  success_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of successful rule executions where the rule conditions were met and the action was successfully applied.']
  threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold value that triggers or qualifies the rule action. Used for auto-accept or auto-representment eligibility based on dispute amount.']
  threshold_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the threshold amount.']
  time_window_hours varchar [note: 'Time window in hours for evaluating max dispute count or other time-bound rule conditions.']
  transaction_age_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Maximum age of the original transaction in days for this rule to apply, used for representment eligibility assessment.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this rule configuration, incremented with each modification to support audit trail and rollback capabilities.']
  win_rate_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum historical win rate percentage for similar disputes required for auto-representment eligibility under this rule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user or system process that created this rule configuration record.']

  Note: 'Business rule configuration governing automated dispute handling decisions — auto-accept thresholds, auto-representment eligibility criteria, reason-code-specific response strategies, merchant-level dispute handling overrides, and scheme-specific processing rules. Captures rule ID, rule name, rule type, applicable reason code scope, merchant segment scope, threshold amount, currency, auto-action type, effective date, expiry date, and approval status. Enables configurable dispute automation without code changes.'
}

Table dispute.ratio_monitor {
  ratio_monitor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ratio_monitor']
  acquirer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.acquirer. Business justification: Scheme chargeback ratio monitoring programs (Visa VDMP, Mastercard MATCH) track ratios at acquirer level. ratio_monitor has mid and mcc as text but no acquirer FK. Direct acquirer_id enables acquirer-']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.card_program. Business justification: Chargeback ratio thresholds and monitoring programs (VDMP, VCFP, EFM) are card program-specific with different enrollment criteria and fees. This link enables program-specific ratio threshold enforcem']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_account. Business justification: Scheme monitoring programs operate at MID/account level. A merchant can have multiple merchant_accounts with distinct MIDs. Linking ratio_monitor to merchant_account enables direct enforcement of enha']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: Scheme monitoring programs (VDMP, ECP) track chargeback/fraud ratios per merchant. ratio_monitor has mid as denormalized text but no merchant_id FK. Risk and compliance teams must link monitoring reco']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_risk_profile. Business justification: When a ratio_monitor record shows threshold breach (VDMP/ECP enrollment), risk ops must update the merchant_risk_profile to adjust reserve_percentage, risk_tier, and enhanced_monitoring_required. This']
  rule_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.rule_config. Business justification: ratio_monitor tracks merchant chargeback ratios against card scheme program thresholds. rule_config governs automated dispute handling decisions including ratio thresholds (standard_threshold_percenta']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Chargeback ratio monitoring is performed per scheme; scheme_id enables the Ratio Monitoring Report used for compliance.']
  scheme_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme_parameter. Business justification: Scheme monitoring programs (Visa VDMP/VFMP, Mastercard MATCH) define chargeback and fraud ratio thresholds via scheme_parameter records. ratio_monitor must reference the governing scheme_parameter to ']
  acquirer_notification_date date [note: 'The date the acquiring bank notified the merchant of the chargeback ratio threshold breach and required remediation actions.']
  assessment_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary penalty or assessment fee imposed by the card scheme for the chargeback ratio threshold breach during this monitoring period. Fees escalate with severity and duration of non-compliance.']
  assessment_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the assessment fee amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  chargeback_ratio_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The calculated chargeback ratio expressed as a percentage: (total_chargeback_count / total_transaction_count) * 100. Core metric for determining scheme program threshold breaches.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the merchant with respect to card scheme chargeback ratio requirements for this monitoring period.']
  consecutive_months_in_program varchar [note: 'The number of consecutive months the merchant has been enrolled in the chargeback monitoring program as of this monitoring period. Used to track escalation and potential termination risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this chargeback ratio monitoring record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  excessive_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The excessive chargeback ratio threshold percentage defined by the card scheme program for this monitoring period. Typically 1.8% for Visa VDMP excessive threshold.']
  fraud_chargeback_count varchar [note: 'The subset of total chargebacks that were classified as fraud-related (reason codes 10.4, 4837, etc.) during the monitoring period. Used for Visa Fraud Monitoring Program (VFM) tracking.']
  fraud_ratio_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The calculated fraud chargeback ratio expressed as a percentage: (fraud_chargeback_count / total_transaction_count) * 100. Used for Visa Fraud Monitoring Program (VFM) compliance.']
  high_risk_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the merchant is classified as high-risk by the acquirer or card scheme, which may result in stricter chargeback ratio thresholds or enhanced monitoring.']
  mcc varchar [note: 'Four-digit code classifying the merchants business type. Certain MCCs have different chargeback ratio thresholds or heightened monitoring requirements.']
  minimum_chargeback_threshold varchar [note: 'The minimum number of chargebacks required during the monitoring period for the merchant to be subject to the chargeback monitoring program. Typically 100 chargebacks for Visa VDMP standard threshold.']
  minimum_transaction_threshold varchar [note: 'The minimum number of transactions required during the monitoring period for the merchant to be subject to the chargeback monitoring program. Typically 1,000 transactions for Visa VDMP.']
  monitoring_period_month date [note: 'The calendar month for which this chargeback ratio is calculated and monitored. Typically the first day of the month (YYYY-MM-01 format).']
  monitoring_program_type varchar [note: 'The specific card scheme chargeback monitoring program under which this merchant is being tracked. Examples: VDMP (Visa Dispute Monitoring Program), VFM (Visa Fraud Monitoring), MCP (Mastercard Chargeback Program), ECMP (Excessive Chargeback Merchant Program).']
  monitoring_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this monitoring record. Active indicates ongoing monitoring; Closed indicates the monitoring period has concluded; Under Review indicates pending scheme or acquirer review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, comments, or context regarding this monitoring record, remediation efforts, or communications with the merchant or card scheme.']
  program_enrollment_date date [note: 'The date the merchant was enrolled into the card scheme chargeback monitoring program due to threshold breach. Null if merchant is not currently enrolled.']
  program_exit_date date [note: 'The date the merchant successfully exited the card scheme chargeback monitoring program by achieving compliance. Null if merchant is still enrolled or was never enrolled.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code where the merchant operates. Card scheme monitoring programs may have regional variations in thresholds and rules.']
  remediation_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document number for the merchants chargeback remediation plan submitted to address the threshold breach. Required for merchants enrolled in monitoring programs.']
  review_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the rolling review period used to calculate the chargeback ratio.']
  review_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the rolling review period used to calculate the chargeback ratio. Card schemes typically use a rolling 12-month or 6-month window.']
  scheme_notification_date date [note: 'The date the card scheme officially notified the acquirer and merchant of the chargeback ratio threshold breach and program enrollment.']
  standard_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The standard chargeback ratio threshold percentage defined by the card scheme program for this monitoring period. Typically 0.9% for Visa VDMP standard threshold.']
  threshold_level_breached varchar [note: 'The severity level of the chargeback ratio threshold breach, if any. Indicates which tier of the card scheme monitoring program the merchant has entered. None indicates compliance; other values indicate escalating severity.']
  total_chargeback_count varchar [note: 'The total number of chargebacks received against the merchant during the monitoring period month. Numerator for chargeback ratio calculation.']
  total_transaction_count varchar [note: 'The total number of transactions processed by the merchant during the monitoring period month. Denominator for chargeback ratio calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this chargeback ratio monitoring record was last modified. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']

  Note: 'Operational monitoring record tracking a merchant\'s chargeback ratio against card scheme program thresholds (Visa VDMP, Mastercard MCP) on a monthly basis. Captures monitor record ID, MID, monitoring period month, total transaction count, total chargeback count, chargeback ratio percentage, scheme program threshold breached (standard, excessive), program enrollment date, remediation plan reference, and scheme notification date. Distinct from analytics — this is the operational compliance record used to manage merchant scheme program status.'
}

Table dispute.claim {
  claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for claim']
  bnpl_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bnpl_plan. Business justification: Consumer claims on BNPL products require plan-specific resolution (installment adjustment, credit limit disputes, interest disputes). This link enables BNPL-specific claim processing, provisional cred']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cardholder account that initiated the dispute claim.']
  claim_chargeback_case_chargeback_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal chargeback case record if this claim was escalated to the chargeback process.']
  claim_chargeback_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal chargeback case record if this claim was escalated to the chargeback process.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.dispute_case. Business justification: A claim is the cardholder-initiated dispute that feeds into the dispute lifecycle. dispute_case is the top-level container. Linking claim to dispute_case enables tracing from the initial cardholder as']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Claims often involve partner-issued cards or partner-processed transactions. Tracking the partner enables liability allocation, partner performance monitoring, regulatory reporting, and scheme complia']
  evidence_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.evidence_package. Business justification: A claim may have an associated evidence_package assembled from the cardholder\'s supporting documentation. The supporting_documentation_provided_flag on claim confirms documentation is tracked. Linking']
  financial_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.financial_adjustment. Business justification: A claim can result in a financial_adjustment (provisional credit issuance, credit reversal). The provisional_credit_amount, provisional_credit_date, and provisional_credit_issued_flag on claim confirm']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant involved in the disputed transaction.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: Claims are filed by cardholders (parties) requiring identity verification, AML screening, and sanctions checks per KYC regulations. Links claim to cardholder party record for compliance verification, ']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Cardholder dispute claims are analyzed per payment_product to assess product‑level risk and offer product‑specific resolutions.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: A cardholder claim is always filed against a specific payment transaction. claim has `disputed_transaction_amount` and `disputed_transaction_date` as denormalized plain attributes but no FK to payment']
  retrieval_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.retrieval_request. Business justification: A claim can escalate to a retrieval_request when the issuing bank requests transaction documentation. The escalated_to_chargeback_flag on claim and the escalated_to_chargeback flag on retrieval_reques']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Cardholder dispute claims are adjudicated under a card scheme; scheme_id links each claim to the governing scheme for settlement.']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the claim was resolved (approved, denied, or withdrawn).']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount the cardholder is claiming as disputed, which may differ from the full transaction amount if disputing a partial charge.']
  cardholder_statement varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the cardholder describing the reason for the dispute and supporting details.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique claim identifier provided to the cardholder for tracking and reference purposes.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispute claim: submitted, under review, pending documentation, approved, denied, withdrawn, or escalated to chargeback. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|under_review|pending_documentation|approved|denied|withdrawn|escalated_to_chargeback — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dispute claim based on the nature of the cardholders assertion: unauthorized transaction, item not received, item not as described, duplicate charge, credit not processed, or service not rendered.']
  contact_attempt_count varchar [note: 'The number of times the dispute resolution team attempted to contact the cardholder for additional information or clarification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this claim record was first created in the dispute management system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the claim and disputed transaction amounts.']
  documentation_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the document management system where supporting evidence files are stored.']
  escalated_to_chargeback_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this claim was escalated to a formal chargeback filing with the card network.']
  expected_resolution_date date [note: 'The target date by which the claim investigation and resolution is expected to be completed, based on regulatory timelines and internal SLAs.']
  fraud_indicator_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the claim was flagged as potentially fraudulent and routed to the fraud investigation team.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical fraud risk score assigned by the fraud detection platform, ranging from 0.00 (low risk) to 100.00 (high risk).']
  initial_triage_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome of the initial automated or manual triage assessment: accepted for review, rejected due to insufficient information, rejected as out of timeframe, escalated to fraud investigation, or provisional credit issued.']
  last_contact_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent contact attempt or successful communication with the cardholder regarding this claim.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this claim record was last updated in the dispute management system.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit code classifying the type of goods or services provided by the merchant involved in the disputed transaction.']
  provisional_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of provisional credit issued to the cardholder, if applicable.']
  provisional_credit_date date [note: 'The date on which provisional credit was posted to the cardholder account.']
  provisional_credit_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a provisional credit was issued to the cardholder account while the claim is under investigation, as required by Regulation E for certain dispute types.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this claim must be included in regulatory reporting submissions to CFPB, card networks, or other governing bodies.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes documenting the investigation findings, decision rationale, and resolution details.']
  resolution_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the claim resolution outcome, aligned with card scheme reason code taxonomies.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the cardholder submitted the dispute claim: mobile app, call center, online portal, branch, email, or chat.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the cardholder submitted the dispute claim, representing the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  supporting_documentation_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the cardholder provided supporting documentation (receipts, correspondence, delivery confirmations) with the claim submission.']
  transaction_arn varchar [note: 'The 23-digit Acquirer Reference Number uniquely identifying the disputed transaction across the payment network.']
  triage_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the initial triage assessment was completed.']

  Note: 'Cardholder-initiated dispute claim record capturing the consumer\'s assertion of a transaction problem before or during the formal chargeback process. Captures claim ID, cardholder account reference, disputed transaction ARN, claim type (unauthorized transaction, item not received, item not as described, duplicate charge, credit not processed), claim submission channel (mobile app, call center, online portal), claim date, claim amount, supporting statement, and initial triage outcome. Represents the consumer-facing entry point into the dispute lifecycle.'
}

Table dispute.merchant_response {
  merchant_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for merchant_response']
  chargeback_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.chargeback. Business justification: A merchant_response is submitted in response to a dispute notification, which is typically triggered by a chargeback filing. While merchant_response links to dispute_case, a direct FK to chargeback en']
  evidence_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.evidence_package. Business justification: A merchant_response includes evidence submission. The evidence_submitted_flag, evidence_document_count, and evidence_type_list fields on merchant_response are denormalized from the evidence_package. L']
  merchant_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_contact. Business justification: merchant_response stores merchant_contact_email, merchant_contact_name, merchant_contact_phone as denormalized text. merchant_contact has can_receive_dispute_notifications specifically for this purpos']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant submitting the response. Links to merchant master data.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Merchant dispute responses are filed against a specific payment transaction. merchant_response has `original_transaction_amount`, `original_transaction_currency_code`, `original_transaction_date` as d']
  pre_arbitration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.pre_arbitration. Business justification: A merchant_response can be submitted in response to a pre-arbitration filing. Linking merchant_response to pre_arbitration enables tracking merchant responses at the pre-arb stage, supporting pre-arbi']
  representment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal representment record created from this merchant response if the acquirer approved it for submission to the issuer. Null if not yet represented.']
  retrieval_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.retrieval_request. Business justification: A merchant_response can be submitted in response to a retrieval_request (the issuing bank\'s request for transaction documentation). The document_delivery_method and fulfillment_status on retrieval_req']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Merchant responses must comply with scheme‑specific rebuttal rules; scheme_id provides the reference for compliance checks.']
  webhook_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.webhook_delivery. Business justification: Dispute notification webhooks (chargeback.received, retrieval_request.created) trigger merchant responses. Tracking which webhook delivery initiated each merchant_response is essential for SLA complia']
  acquirer_feedback_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or feedback provided by the acquirer reviewer regarding the quality, completeness, or deficiencies of the merchant response.']
  acquirer_review_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the acquirer completed their review of the merchant response and made a decision on whether to proceed with representment.']
  acquirer_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the acquirers internal review of the merchant response. Indicates whether the response meets quality standards for formal representment to the issuer.']
  arn varchar [note: 'Unique 23-digit reference number assigned by the acquirer to the original transaction. Used to link the response to the specific transaction under dispute.']
  automated_response_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this response was generated automatically by the merchants dispute management system or submitted manually by a merchant representative. True if automated.']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the cardholder who initiated the dispute. Included for merchant reference and evidence matching purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this merchant response record was first created in the dispute management system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  dispute_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant was first notified of the dispute or retrieval request. Used to calculate response deadline and track merchant response time.']
  evidence_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchant has attached supporting documentation or evidence files to substantiate their response. True if evidence package is included.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this merchant response record was last updated. Tracks changes throughout the response lifecycle from draft to final submission.']
  liability_acceptance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that the merchant accepts liability for in cases of partial acceptance. Null if merchant contests the full dispute amount.']
  liability_acceptance_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the liability acceptance amount. Typically matches the original transaction currency.']
  merchant_rebuttal_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the merchant describing their position, justification, and defense against the dispute claim. May include transaction details, customer interaction history, and policy references.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Card scheme reason code associated with the original dispute that this response addresses. Used to determine required evidence types and response strategy.']
  representment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this merchant response qualifies for formal representment to the issuer based on acquirer review, evidence quality, and card scheme rules. True if eligible.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the merchant submitted their response to the acquirer (e.g., dispute management portal, API integration, email submission).']
  response_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated confidence score (0-100) indicating the predicted likelihood of winning the dispute based on evidence quality, reason code, and historical outcomes. Used for prioritization and strategy.']
  response_deadline_date date [note: 'Final date by which the merchant must submit their response to preserve representment rights per card scheme rules. Typically 7-30 days from dispute notification.']
  response_language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the merchant rebuttal narrative and evidence were submitted.']
  response_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this merchant response for tracking and correspondence purposes.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the merchant response in the dispute workflow. Tracks progression from draft through acquirer review to final disposition.']
  response_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the merchant formally submitted the response to the acquirer for review. Critical for deadline tracking and SLA compliance.']
  response_turnaround_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between dispute notification and merchant response submission. Used for performance tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  response_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the merchants position in response to the dispute notification. Indicates whether merchant accepts, contests, or partially accepts the chargeback claim.']
  response_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number tracking revisions to the merchant response. Incremented each time the merchant updates their response before final submission.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchant response was submitted after the deadline, resulting in automatic forfeiture of representment rights. True if deadline missed.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the merchant for withdrawing their response and accepting liability. Null if no withdrawal requested.']
  withdrawal_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchant has requested to withdraw this response and accept the chargeback liability. True if withdrawal requested.']

  Note: 'Merchant-submitted response record to a dispute notification or retrieval request, capturing the merchant\'s position and supporting documentation prior to formal representment. Captures response ID, dispute case ID, MID, merchant response date, response type (accept liability, contest dispute, partial acceptance), merchant rebuttal narrative, evidence submitted flag, response channel, and acquirer review status. Distinct from representment — this is the merchant\'s raw input before the acquirer packages the formal representment.'
}

Table dispute.fee {
  fee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fee']
  arbitration_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.arbitration_case. Business justification: Fees are assessed for arbitration proceedings (scheme arbitration fees). The scheme_arbitration_fee_amount on arbitration_case confirms arbitration-specific fees exist. Linking fee to arbitration_case']
  chargeback_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.chargeback. Business justification: Fees are assessed in connection with specific chargeback events (scheme chargeback fees, processing fees). While fee already links to dispute_case, a direct FK to chargeback enables precise fee attrib']
  claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.claim. Business justification: Fees can be assessed in connection with a claim (e.g., investigation fees, provisional credit processing fees). Linking fee to claim enables precise fee attribution to the specific claim that triggere']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Dispute processing fees must be allocated to cost centers for cost accounting, profitability analysis by merchant segment, and operational budgeting. Enables tracking of scheme fee expenses and intern']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent dispute case record to which this fee is associated. Links the fee to the full dispute lifecycle including chargeback filings, representment, and arbitration.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Dispute fee expense tracking: fees arising from disputes need a GL account for expense recognition and reporting.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Dispute fees (scheme arbitration fees, chargeback processing fees, pre-arbitration fees) must be recorded as journal entries for expense recognition, P&L reporting, and cost allocation. Required for m']
  pre_arbitration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.pre_arbitration. Business justification: Fees can be assessed at the pre-arbitration stage. The fee_amount field on pre_arbitration confirms pre-arb-specific fees exist. Linking fee to pre_arbitration enables precise fee attribution to the p']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_fee_schedule. Business justification: Dispute fees (chargeback fees, arbitration fees) are assessed per product fee schedules. This link enables fee calculation validation, audit trails, and regulatory compliance reporting—fundamental to ']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Dispute fee calculations may be converted; fee reporting needs the exact FX rate applied.']
  representment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.representment. Business justification: Fees can be assessed at the representment stage (scheme fees for representment processing). The dispute_stage field on fee confirms stage-specific fee assessment. Linking fee to representment enables ']
  scheme_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule or pricing configuration that determined the assessed fee amount. Links to the applicable scheme fee table or internal pricing rule used to calculate this charge.']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross monetary amount of the fee assessed in the fee currency. Represents the base charge before any waivers or adjustments. Stored with four decimal places to support multi-currency precision.']
  amount_usd decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee amount converted to US Dollars (USD) using the FX rate applied at assessment date. Enables normalized cross-currency reporting and analytics across all dispute fee records.']
  arn varchar [note: 'Acquirer Reference Number uniquely identifying the original transaction linked to the dispute that generated this fee. Enables end-to-end traceability from fee back to the originating payment transaction per card scheme rules.']
  assessing_entity_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the specific entity that assessed the fee (e.g., \'Visa Inc.\', \'Mastercard International\', \'Chase Merchant Services\'). Complements assessed_by for reporting and reconciliation.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the fee was formally assessed or levied by the assessing entity. Used as the primary business event date for billing period assignment and regulatory reporting.']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the fee was assessed. Provides sub-day precision for SLA tracking, real-time processing audit, and dispute deadline calculations.']
  billing_period varchar [note: 'The billing cycle period (YYYY-MM format) to which this fee is attributed for invoicing and financial reporting. Aligns with monthly scheme billing cycles and ERP accounts payable periods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fee record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Provides audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the fee amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency dispute processing across 200+ countries.']
  dispute_fee_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by dispute operations staff regarding this fee record. May include waiver justification details, scheme correspondence references, or escalation context.']
  dispute_stage varchar [note: 'Stage of the dispute lifecycle at which this fee was assessed. Identifies whether the fee relates to a retrieval request, first chargeback, second chargeback, pre-arbitration, arbitration, or compliance filing.']
  due_date date [note: 'Contractual or scheme-mandated deadline by which the fee must be paid. Drives overdue detection, collections escalation, and SLA compliance monitoring.']
  fee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee record. Tracks progression from initial assessment through invoicing, payment, waiver, or secondary dispute of the fee itself.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fee assessed in connection with the dispute. Distinguishes scheme chargeback fees, arbitration filing fees, compliance assessment fines, retrieval request fees, pre-arbitration fees, and internal processing fees. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: chargeback_fee|arbitration_fee|compliance_assessment|retrieval_request_fee|internal_processing_fee|pre_arbitration_fee|scheme_penalty|network_fine — promote to reference product]']
  fx_rate_applied decimal(18,8) [note: 'The FX exchange rate used to convert the fee amount from the fee currency to USD at the time of assessment. Supports audit trail for currency conversion and DCC-related fee reconciliation.']
  invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the invoice or billing statement on which this fee appears. Links the fee record to the ERP accounts payable or accounts receivable invoice for payment tracking and reconciliation.']
  mid varchar [note: 'Merchant Identification Number of the merchant associated with the disputed transaction that triggered this fee. Used for merchant-level fee reporting, MDR reconciliation, and chargeback ratio monitoring.']
  net_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final collectible fee amount after applying any waivers or adjustments (fee_amount minus waiver_amount). Represents the actual amount due for billing and GL posting.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee was settled or paid. Populated when payment_status transitions to \'paid\' or \'partially_paid\'. Used for cash flow reporting and aging analysis.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment collection status of the net fee amount. Tracks whether the fee has been paid, remains outstanding, is overdue, or has been written off. Distinct from fee_status which tracks the assessment lifecycle.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this fee record as assigned by the assessing entity (card scheme, acquirer, or internal billing system). Used for reconciliation and invoice matching.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee record must be included in regulatory or compliance reports (e.g., FinCEN, CFPB, or scheme compliance filings). Supports automated regulatory data aggregation.']
  scheme_reason_code varchar [note: 'Card scheme-specific reason code associated with the chargeback or dispute event that triggered this fee (e.g., Visa reason code 10.4, Mastercard reason code 4853). Aligns with ISO 8583 and scheme rule classifications.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this fee record originated. Identifies whether the fee was generated by the Dispute and Chargeback Management System, ERP Oracle Financials, a card scheme network feed, or manual entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this fee record in the Silver layer. Supports change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail requirements.']
  waiver_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount waived from the original fee_amount. Supports partial waivers where only a portion of the assessed fee is forgiven. Zero if no waiver applied.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee has been fully waived by the assessing entity. When True, the fee amount is not expected to be collected. Distinct from partial adjustments tracked in fee_waiver_amount.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the fee was waived. Applicable only when waiver_flag is True. Supports audit and compliance reporting for fee waiver decisions.']

  Note: 'Fee record for all charges assessed in connection with dispute processing — scheme chargeback fees, arbitration filing fees, compliance assessment fines, retrieval request fees, and internal processing fees. Captures fee ID, dispute case ID, fee type, fee amount, currency, fee assessed by (scheme, acquirer, internal), fee assessment date, billing period, invoice reference, payment status, and waiver flag. Distinct from dispute_financial_adjustment which tracks transaction-level debits/credits; this tracks fee-based charges.'
}

Table compliance.aml_screening_result {
  aml_screening_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each AML screening result record.']
  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_case. Business justification: An AML screening result that triggers an investigation is associated with a compliance case. One compliance case may aggregate multiple AML screening results (e.g., a pattern of suspicious transaction']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the screened entity (transaction, cardholder, merchant, or partner).']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: AML screening results must be scoped to the legal entity being screened for regulatory reporting, jurisdictional compliance obligations, and entity-level risk aggregation required by financial regulat']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: AML screening is performed against a party (cardholder, merchant, or business entity). The party table is the compliance SSOT for all screened entities. Adding party_id normalizes the relationship and']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: AML screening of cross-border transactions must capture the FX rate at screening time for regulatory reporting, suspicious activity pattern analysis, and USD-equivalent threshold calculations required']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: AML screening results are governed by a regulatory obligation; linking enables obligation‑centric reporting.']
  screened_account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.account_holder. Business justification: AML screening is performed on account holders during onboarding and ongoing monitoring per FATF/BSA requirements. Compliance operations teams must trace each AML screening result to the specific accou']
  analyst_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an analyst manually overrode the automated disposition.']
  analyst_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation provided by the analyst for the override.']
  compliance_aml_screening_result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the screening result record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the screening result record was first created in the data lake.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Outcome of the screening: pass, requires manual review, or block.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of business entity that was screened.']
  is_sar_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) must be filed.']
  is_str_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) must be filed.']
  match_type varchar [note: 'Category of data element that triggered a match during screening.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the screening.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by the AML engine (0.00 – 100.00).']
  sar_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SAR filing process.']
  sar_filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SAR was filed, if applicable.']
  screening_engine varchar [note: 'Name of the AML screening engine or vendor used (e.g., World-Check, LexisNexis).']
  screening_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the rule set applied during the screening.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the AML screening was performed.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component within the source system that executed the screening.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that generated the screening result (e.g., Risk and Compliance Management System).']
  str_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the STR filing process.']
  str_filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the STR was filed, if applicable.']
  typology varchar [note: 'AML typology or pattern identified (e.g., structuring, smurfing, terrorist financing).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the screening result record.']

  Note: 'SSOT for Anti-Money Laundering screening outcomes. Records each AML screening event against a transaction, cardholder, or merchant entity — including the screening engine used, risk score assigned, matched typology, disposition (pass/review/block), analyst override, and regulatory obligation reference. Sourced from the Risk and Compliance Management System AML module.'
}

Table compliance.sanctions_check {
  sanctions_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each sanctions screening event.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (cardholder, merchant, partner, beneficiary) screened.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Sanctions checks are performed by/for specific legal entities; required for OFAC reporting, entity-level compliance status tracking, and regulatory examination responses in payments fintech operations']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: Sanctions screening is performed against a party (cardholder, merchant, or counterparty). The party table is the compliance master for all screened entities. Adding party_id to sanctions_check links e']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Sanctions screening must record which payment product was used to support product‑level risk analytics.']
  primary_sanctions_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with this screening, if applicable.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: Sanctions screening requires capturing the FX rate to calculate USD-equivalent amounts for OFAC reporting thresholds. Transactions exceeding USD thresholds trigger mandatory reporting; rate at screeni']
  screened_account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.account_holder. Business justification: Sanctions screening is performed against account holders per OFAC/UN requirements. Compliance teams must link each sanctions check result to the cardholder-domain account_holder for blocking decisions']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch in which the screening was executed, if applicable.']
  watchlist_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.watchlist_entry. Business justification: Sanctions checks reference entries in the watchlist master; linking provides source of the hit.']
  block_action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken as a result of the hit (e.g., blocked transaction, allowed, sent for review).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sanctions check record was first created.']
  false_positive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hit was later determined to be a false positive.']
  hit_details varchar [note: 'Details of the matched sanctioned entity (e.g., name, address).']
  hit_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the screening produced a potential match against the sanctions list.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the screening result is considered high‑risk and requires immediate attention.']
  list_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sanctions list used for the screening (e.g., OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated, UN).']
  list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sanctions list at the time of screening.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score (0‑100) indicating the strength of the match.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes added by analysts or reviewers.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the screened party.']
  reporting_obligation varchar [note: 'Regulatory filing triggered by the hit (Suspicious Activity Report, Suspicious Transaction Report, or None).']
  reporting_obligation_filed boolean [note: 'True if the required regulatory report was filed.']
  review_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the compliance review.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance officer completed the review.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score combining match score and other risk factors.']
  screening_method varchar [note: 'Algorithmic method used for the screening.']
  screening_source varchar [note: 'System or module that executed the screening (e.g., Risk and Compliance Management System).']
  screening_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the screening.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the sanctions screening was performed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sanctions check record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Records each OFAC/SDN and global sanctions screening event performed against a party (cardholder, merchant, partner, beneficiary). Captures the list version screened (SDN, EU Consolidated, UN), match score, hit details, false-positive determination, blocking action taken, and OFAC reporting obligation triggered. SSOT for sanctions screening history.'
}

  sar_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SAR filing record.']

  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_case. Business justification: A SAR filing is submitted as part of a compliance investigation case.   payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: SAR filings often relate to a specific payment product (e.g., card vs ACH) for product‑level AML reporting.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: FinCEN, FINTRAC, and AUSTRAC require SARs to reference the specific transactions that triggered suspicious activity. sar_filing links to compliance_aml_screening_result and compliance_case but not dir']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: A SAR filing is a specific type of regulatory filing submitted to FinCEN. The regulatory_filing table is the master record of all regulatory submissions. Linking sar_filing to its corresponding regula']

Business justification: SAR filings are made about specific account holders per FinCEN/BSA requirements. subject_party_id references compliance.party; a direct FK to account_holder enables compliance officers to pull all SAR']
  subject_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: The SAR filing subject (subject_entity_name, subject_identifier, subject_address) corresponds to a party in the compliance party master. Adding subject_party_id links the SAR to the authoritative part']
  activity_end_date date [note: 'Date when the suspicious activity ended.']

  attachment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documents attached to the filing.']
  compliance_review_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance review was completed.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the internal compliance review of the SAR.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification level indicating data sensitivity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SAR record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_retention_expiry date [note: 'Date after which the SAR record must be purged per retention policy.']
  filing_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SAR was submitted to the regulator.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'External tracking number assigned by BSA for the filing.']
  filing_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the officer responsible for the filing.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the SAR with the regulator.']
  is_amended boolean [note: 'True if this filing is an amendment to a prior SAR.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing is flagged as high risk.']
  narrative_summary varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative describing the suspicious activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional remarks.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority to which the SAR was submitted.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score generated by the compliance scoring engine.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the SAR (e.g., FraudDetectionPlatform).']
  subject_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the subject entity.']
  subject_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the subject\'s residence.']
  subject_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subject entity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SAR record.']

  Note: 'Suspicious Activity Report filing record — the authoritative record of each SAR submitted to FinCEN or equivalent regulator. Captures the subject entity, suspicious activity type, activity date range, filing date, BSA tracking number, narrative summary, filing officer, and acknowledgement receipt. Supports regulatory examination and audit trail requirements under BSA.'
}

  verification_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the verification after processing.']

  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the KYC verification process.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when verification was completed.']

  Note: 'Know Your Customer identity verification record for cardholders, merchants, and business entities. Captures verification method (document check, biometric, database lookup), identity document type and number, verification provider, outcome (pass/fail/pending), risk tier assigned, re-verification due date, and CDD/EDD classification. SSOT for KYC status across the platform.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_obligation {
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the regulatory obligation record.']
  amendment_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the obligation has been amended since its original issuance.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with the obligation is mandatory, optional, or voluntary.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance outcome for the obligation.']
  control_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit, department, or role responsible for ensuring compliance with the obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the obligation record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the official regulatory document or guidance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the obligation becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the obligation expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  enforcement_penalty varchar [note: 'Description of penalties, fines, or sanctions associated with non‑compliance.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the authority that issued the obligation (e.g., PCI Security Standards Council, EU PSD2, OFAC).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the jurisdiction to which the obligation applies.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the obligation was most recently reviewed.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the obligation (active, inactive, pending, retired).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next compliance review.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used internally to reference the obligation.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the regulatory obligation as defined by the governing body.']
  regulation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory instrument (law, directive, standard, or guideline).']
  regulatory_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the regulatory requirement, including scope and key provisions.']
  review_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the obligation is reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the obligation based on potential impact and likelihood of non‑compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the obligation record (e.g., Risk and Compliance Management System).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the obligation record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the obligation (e.g., v1.0, v2.3).']

  Note: 'Master registry of all regulatory obligations applicable to the business — laws, directives, rules, and standards (PCI DSS, PSD2, BSA, GDPR, CCPA, Reg E, Reg II, OFAC, SOX). Each record defines the obligation name, governing body, jurisdiction, effective date, applicability scope, control owner, and review cadence. Serves as the compliance obligation inventory for control mapping.'
}

Table compliance.control {
  control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the compliance control.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Compliance control budgeting uses cost centers for internal financial reporting; linking enables allocation of control costs to the appropriate cost center.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.endpoint. Business justification: Controls govern specific endpoint security configurations (TLS version controls, encryption controls, access controls for network endpoints). Linking control to endpoint enables endpoint-level control']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Posting expenses or adjustments from compliance activities requires a GL account; this FK supports accurate financial statements and audit trails.']
  policy_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy_document. Business justification: A compliance control is implemented to satisfy a policy document (AML program policy, PCI DSS information security policy, etc.). Linking control to policy_document establishes the policy-to-control t']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each compliance control implements one or more regulatory obligations; linking provides direct mapping.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Controls are scheme-specific — Visa mandates different controls than Mastercard (e.g., Visa\'s VAMP program, Mastercard\'s MATCH list controls). Linking control to scheme enables scheme-specific control']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.sla_profile. Business justification: Operational controls (uptime targets, latency thresholds, error rate limits) are enforced and measured via SLA profiles. Links control definition to monitoring/enforcement mechanism for control effect']
  txn_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.txn_type. Business justification: Compliance controls are scoped to specific transaction types — AML controls apply to cross-border transactions, SCA controls apply to card-not-present, PCI controls apply to card-present. control.cont']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Reference to audit log entries related to the control.']
  control_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the control across systems.']
  control_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the control\'s purpose and scope.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the compliance control.']
  control_scope varchar [note: 'Business area or process that the control applies to.']
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control based on its nature: preventive, detective, or corrective.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was created in the system.']
  criticality varchar [note: 'Criticality level of the control for business continuity.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control expires or is superseded, if applicable.']
  evidence_collected boolean [note: 'True if required evidence has been collected for the latest assessment.']
  evidence_required varchar [note: 'Description of the evidence needed to demonstrate control execution.']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored evidence artifact.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is performed or reviewed.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'True if the control is performed automatically by systems.']
  is_key_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control is considered a key control for regulatory reporting.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'True if the control requires manual execution.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent assessment of the control.']
  last_assessment_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent control assessment.']
  next_assessment_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next control assessment.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that the control satisfies.']
  regulatory_requirement_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific regulatory requirement mapped to this control.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned action to address control deficiencies.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation effort.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the control based on its impact and likelihood.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the control\'s risk level.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the control information.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated tags for categorizing the control (e.g., finance, security, privacy).']
  testing_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to test the effectiveness of the control.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the control definition.']

  Note: 'Defines each internal compliance control implemented to satisfy one or more regulatory obligations. Captures control ID, control name, control type (preventive/detective/corrective), frequency, owner, mapped obligation references, testing methodology, and last assessment outcome. Forms the control library used in PCI DSS, SOX, and regulatory examination responses.'
}

  Note: 'Records each periodic testing or assessment of a compliance control — including assessment date, assessor (internal audit, QSA, external auditor), testing approach, evidence collected, finding severity, remediation required flag, and pass/fail outcome.   cde_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Technical description of the CDE boundaries assessed.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']

  findings_count varchar [note: 'Total count of non‑conformities discovered.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the primary jurisdiction.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the audit record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']

  report_submission_date date [note: 'Date the ROC or SAQ report was submitted to the acquiring bank or regulator.']
  requirements_tested varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of PCI DSS requirement numbers examined.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating derived from audit findings.']
  saq_type varchar [note: 'Specific SAQ variant applied when audit_type = SAQ.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the CDE scope covered by the audit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']

  Note: 'PCI DSS compliance audit record capturing each annual or interim assessment cycle — QSA firm, assessment scope (SAQ type or full ROC), cardholder data environment (CDE) boundaries assessed, requirements tested, findings, compensating controls, and Report on Compliance (ROC) or Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) submission details. SSOT for PCI DSS compliance posture.'
}

Table compliance.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each consent record.']
  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_case. Business justification: A consent record violation (e.g., processing data without valid consent, GDPR breach) may trigger a compliance case. Linking consent_record to compliance_case enables traceability from consent events ']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device (e.g., mobile device ID) used during consent capture.']
  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction_profile. Business justification: Consent requirements are jurisdiction-specific (GDPR in EU, CCPA in California, PDPA in Thailand, etc.). Linking consent_record to jurisdiction_profile establishes which regulatory jurisdiction govern']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Consent records must track which legal entity collected consent for GDPR/privacy law compliance, data controller identification, and regulatory examination responses in payments fintech operations.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the data subject (cardholder or merchant) who gave the consent.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Consent collection varies by payment product (BNPL credit checks, card tokenization, P2P data sharing). GDPR/CCPA compliance requires tracking which product triggered consent and for what processing p']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Consent records are collected to meet specific regulatory obligations; linking clarifies which obligation the consent satisfies.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.request. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA consent records must be traceable to the specific API request that collected consent (IP address, timestamp, user agent, collection channel). Required for regulatory audit trails proving law']
  collection_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the consent was obtained (e.g., website, mobile app, in‑store terminal, call centre, email).']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent record.']
  consent_text_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the consent text version to ensure integrity and support audit.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the consent was recorded.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent covering marketing, data sharing, open banking, or Strong Customer Authentication (SCA).']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the consent text to support change management and re‑consent tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  data_processing_purpose varchar [note: 'Narrative description of why the data is being processed under this consent.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the data may be retained after consent, as defined by policy.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Optional expiry timestamp; null if consent is open‑ended.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'Higher‑level region or jurisdiction (e.g., EU, APAC) associated with the consent capture.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device used to provide consent, required for audit and fraud investigations.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the consent text presented to the data subject.']
  lawful_basis varchar [note: 'Legal justification for processing under GDPR/CCPA (e.g., consent, contract, legal obligation, legitimate interest).']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the consent was provided by a cardholder (consumer) or a merchant (business).']
  reconsent_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the data subject must provide renewed consent, if required.']
  reconsent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a new consent must be obtained due to regulatory or policy changes.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Describes the data processing activities covered by the consent (e.g., marketing, analytics, third‑party sharing, risk assessment).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that captured the consent (e.g., Digital Wallet Platform, Merchant Management System).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or service account that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the consent record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string providing context about the client environment.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason provided by the data subject for withdrawing consent.']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the data subject withdrew the consent, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of data processing consent obtained from cardholders and merchants under GDPR, PSD2, and CCPA. Captures consent type (marketing, data sharing, open banking, SCA), consent version, collection channel, timestamp, IP address, withdrawal status, and re-consent triggers. Supports lawful basis documentation and data subject rights fulfillment.'
}

  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory filings are made to satisfy specific obligations; linking enables tracking of filing status per obligation.']

  Note: 'Master record of all regulatory filings submitted to governing bodies — FinCEN CTR (Currency Transaction Reports), PSD2 incident reports to ECB/NCAs, Reg E error resolution notices, CFPB complaint responses, and SWIFT gpi compliance reports. Captures filing type, regulator, submission date, filing reference number, status, and acknowledgement. Distinct from SAR/STR which have dedicated entities.'
}

SSOT for regulatory examination support and response management.'
}

Distinct from fraud_case in the fraud domain.'
}

  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the watchlist entry.']
  address varchar [note: 'Physical address associated with the entity on the watchlist.']

  identification_type varchar [note: 'Type of the identification number provided.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional context about the entry.']
  place_of_birth varchar [note: 'Geographic location where the individual was born.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the entity\'s compliance review.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned to the entity by the source list or internal assessment.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) assigned by the compliance team.']
  sanctions_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of sanction applied to the entity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the watchlist data (e.g., OFAC Feed, UN Sanctions Feed).']
  source_url varchar [note: 'Web address or document reference where the entry was originally published.']

  Note: 'Reference master of watchlist entries used in AML, sanctions, and CTF screening — sourced from OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Consolidated List, HM Treasury, PEP databases, and adverse media feeds. Captures entity name, aliases, entity type (individual/organization/vessel), list source, list version date, risk category, and active status. Feeds the sanctions_check and aml_screening_result processes.'
}

Table compliance.jurisdiction_profile {
  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the jurisdiction profile record.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Jurisdiction profiles define the regulatory environment; linking to obligations ties profiles to applicable rules.']
  amendment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the record reflects an amendment to a prior version.']
  aml_ctf_regime varchar [note: 'Classification of the jurisdiction\'s anti‑money‑laundering and counter‑terrorism financing regime (e.g., FATF, Non‑FATF).']
  applicable_regulations varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulation codes that apply in this jurisdiction (e.g., PSD2, Reg E, APRA CPS 234).']
  classification varchar [note: 'Category of the jurisdiction (e.g., sovereign, non‑sovereign, special economic zone).']
  compliance_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the internal owner responsible for this jurisdiction\'s compliance data.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the jurisdiction profile record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Default currency used for transactions in the jurisdiction.']
  data_protection_law varchar [note: 'Primary data‑privacy legislation governing the jurisdiction (e.g., GDPR).']
  data_residency_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific data residency or localisation obligations imposed by the jurisdiction.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the jurisdiction profile becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the jurisdiction profile ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  enforcement_penalty decimal(18,2) [note: 'Typical monetary penalty for non‑compliance in this jurisdiction.']
  fatf_mutual_evaluation_rating varchar [note: 'Latest FATF mutual‑evaluation rating for the jurisdiction.']
  is_cross_border_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cross‑border payments are permitted under local regulations.']
  jurisdiction_profile_description varchar [note: 'Extended textual description of the jurisdiction profile.']
  jurisdiction_profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the jurisdiction (e.g., United States, European Union).']
  jurisdiction_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the jurisdiction profile.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Specific type classification of the jurisdiction.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the jurisdiction profile was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  legal_entity_identifier varchar [note: '20‑character global identifier for legal entities operating in the jurisdiction.']
  max_daily_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Regulatory limit on total daily transaction volume for a participant.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Regulatory ceiling for a single transaction amount in the jurisdiction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the jurisdiction.']
  privacy_regulation varchar [note: 'Specific privacy regulation reference applicable in the jurisdiction.']
  regulatory_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic regulatory zone grouping (e.g., EU, APAC).']
  reporting_obligation varchar [note: 'Description of mandatory regulatory reporting requirements (e.g., SAR, STR).']
  review_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the jurisdiction profile must be reviewed.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall compliance risk rating assigned to the jurisdiction.']
  sanctions_regime_membership varchar [note: 'Sanctions lists or regimes the jurisdiction participates in.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the jurisdiction data.']
  tax_identification_number_format varchar [note: 'Pattern or description of the tax ID format used in the jurisdiction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the jurisdiction profile.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the jurisdiction profile record.']

  Note: 'Reference master of regulatory jurisdictions in which the business operates — country, regulatory zone (EU, EEA, APAC), applicable payment regulations (PSD2, Reg E, APRA CPS 234), AML/CTF regime classification, FATF mutual evaluation rating, data residency requirements, and sanctions regime membership. Used to determine applicable obligations per transaction corridor and entity domicile.'
}

Table compliance.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for party']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.account_holder.   legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity.   data_subject_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has provided consent for data processing.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of an individual party.']

  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑declared gender of the individual party.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code (e.g., NAICS) describing the party\'s business sector.']
