  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations about the party.']
  ofac_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the sanctions screening against the OFAC SDN list.']
  party_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party record.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the party (e.g., individual, organization).']
  primary_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the party\'s primary mailing address.']

  primary_city varchar [note: 'City component of the party\'s primary address.']
  primary_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the party\'s primary address.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for electronic communications with the party.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for contacting the party.']
  primary_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the party\'s primary address.']
  primary_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the party\'s primary address.']
  residency_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO country code where the party currently resides.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk rating (0‑100) derived from AML, fraud, and regulatory checks.']
  sanctions_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of sanctions identifiers that matched this party, if any.']
  source_of_funds varchar [note: 'Declared origin of the party\'s funds (e.g., salary, investment, loan).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that originated the party record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status of the party.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the party type, such as corporate, non‑profit, or sovereign.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the party record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by party_id.'
}

Table compliance.case_party_involvement {
  case_party_involvement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this case-party involvement record']
  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance case being investigated']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the party involved in the investigation']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this involvement record was created']
  interview_date date [note: 'Date when this party was interviewed regarding this case (nullable)']
  involvement_date date [note: 'Date when this party was identified as involved in the case']
  involvement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes about this specific party\'s involvement in this specific case']
  involvement_role varchar [note: 'The role this party plays in the compliance case investigation (e.g., primary subject, witness, associated entity, beneficial owner)']
  involvement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this party\'s involvement in the investigation (e.g., identified, under review, interviewed, cleared, implicated)']
  is_primary_subject boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this party is the primary subject of the compliance investigation']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this involvement record was last updated']
  risk_contribution_level varchar [note: 'Assessment of how much this party contributed to the compliance risk in this case']

  Note: 'This association product represents the investigative involvement relationship between compliance_case and party. It captures which parties are subjects, witnesses, or associated entities in each compliance investigation, along with their role, involvement timeline, and investigative status. Each record links one compliance_case to one party with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific investigation relationship.. Existence Justification: In compliance investigations, a single case routinely involves multiple parties (subject, witnesses, beneficial owners, associated entities), and a single party can be involved in multiple compliance cases over time in different roles. Compliance officers actively manage case party involvement as a distinct investigative concept, tracking each party\'s role, involvement status, interview dates, and risk contribution for each case. This is an operational M:N relationship that cannot be modeled with a simple FK without losing critical investigative tracking data.'
}

Table product.payment_product {
  payment_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the payment product.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.acquirer_endpoint. Business justification: Primary acquirer endpoint assigned to a payment product for settlement and processing, required for settlement reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Regulatory reporting of product issuance requires linking each payment product to its issuing partner for compliance and revenue sharing.']
  irf_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_table. Business justification: Required for pricing engine to map each payment product to the specific IRF table that defines its interchange rates, used in rate calculation reports.']
  multi_network_routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.multi_network_routing. Business justification: Durbin Amendment compliance mandates debit payment products support multiple unaffiliated networks. Linking payment_product to a multi_network_routing profile enables least-cost routing assignment, Du']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each payment product is subject to specific regulatory obligations; mapping enables compliance monitoring.']
  revenue_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue Posting: Each payment product’s transaction fees and interchange income must be posted to a specific GL account for financial reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Required for mapping each payment product to its card scheme for interchange fee calculation, compliance reporting and settlement routing.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AML screening is performed on transactions of this product.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification state of the product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the product becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the product ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which merchants or cardholders may use the product.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for contactless (NFC) transactions.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for cross‑border payments.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for online e‑commerce transactions.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product supports tokenization of sensitive data.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether KYC verification is mandatory for using the product.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper limit for a single transaction processed with this product.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower limit for a single transaction processed with this product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for internal comments or special handling instructions.']
  payment_product_category varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the product.']
  payment_product_code varchar [note: 'Business‑unique alphanumeric code used to reference the product in downstream systems.']
  payment_product_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the product, its purpose and key features.']
  payment_product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment product.']
  payment_product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the payment product.']
  product_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the product definition.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory regimes that apply to the product.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the product based on fraud and compliance exposure.']
  supported_rails varchar [note: 'Payment networks or clearing rails the product can be processed on.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed surcharge amount applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'True if a surcharge may be added to the transaction fee.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to generate tokens for the product.']
  transaction_fee_fixed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat fee amount applied per transaction, in the product\'s currency.']
  transaction_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variable percentage fee applied to each transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the product record.']
  version_effective_from date [note: 'Date when this product version became effective.']
  version_effective_until date [note: 'Date when this product version was superseded; null if current.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all payment products offered by the platform — card programs (credit, debit, prepaid), BNPL offerings, P2P transfer products, A2A rail products, virtual account products, and digital wallet products. Each record defines a distinct product type with its commercial identity, lifecycle status, supported rails, and regulatory classification. SSOT for product identity across merchant and cardholder agreements.'
}

Captures program type (credit, debit, prepaid, commercial), co-brand partnerships, EMV configuration, contactless enablement, and program-level spending controls. Links to BIN registry and interchange qualification rules.'
}

Table product.bnpl_plan {

Business justification: bnpl_plan carries three denormalized eligibility fields (eligibility_cardholder_segments, eligibility_merchant_category_codes, eligibility_merchant_ids) that duplicate the structured eligibility logic']
  interest_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Interest Revenue: BNPL plans generate interest income that must be recorded in a designated GL account for accurate revenue recognition and compliance.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: BNPL plans are defined for a specific payment product; child BNPL plan references parent payment_product.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_fee_schedule. Business justification: bnpl_plan carries a denormalized fee_schedule_description string field and embedded fee parameters (early_repayment_fee_percent, late_payment_fee_amount) that should be governed by a formal product_fe']
  allowed_countries varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the plan is offered.']

  bnpl_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan record was first created in the system.']
  disallowed_countries varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the plan is prohibited.']

  installment_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between successive installment due dates.']

  is_promotion_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is currently being marketed as a promotion.']
  late_payment_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat fee applied when an installment is missed or paid after the due date.']
  late_payment_grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the due date before a late fee is assessed.']
  max_installment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound on the amount of any single installment.']
  max_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total purchase amount that can be financed under this plan.']
  maximum_credit_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound on the total credit exposure a cardholder can have under this plan.']
  min_installment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound on the amount of any single installment.']
  min_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum total purchase amount required to qualify for the plan.']

  promotional_apr_start_date date [note: 'First date the promotional APR becomes effective.']

  requires_credit_check boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a creditworthiness assessment is performed before approval.']
  requires_kyc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification is mandatory for enrollment.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum risk score a cardholder must have to be eligible for this plan.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or process that last updated the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plan record.']

  Note: 'Master definition of Buy Now Pay Later installment plan structures offered to cardholders and merchants. Captures installment count, interest rate, promotional APR windows, minimum purchase threshold, maximum credit limit, merchant eligibility criteria, and repayment schedule logic. Distinct from a cardholder\'s active BNPL enrollment — this is the plan template in the product catalog.'
}

Table product.product_fee_schedule {
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each fee schedule record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.agreement. Business justification: Fee schedules are often governed by partner agreements, especially for scheme fees, acquirer fees, and revenue-sharing arrangements. Real business process: contract compliance verification, fee reconc']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Multi-currency fee schedules require currency pair specification for cross-border merchant pricing. Essential for determining applicable fees when transaction currency differs from settlement currency']
  fee_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Fee Booking: Each product fee schedule defines fees that are posted to a specific GL account to ensure proper expense/revenue tracking.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Fee schedules can be governed by regulatory rules; linking captures which obligations drive fee structures.']
  scheme_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme_fee_schedule. Business justification: Product fee schedules must be reconciled against scheme-published fee schedules for scheme billing and pass-through fee compliance. Linking product_fee_schedule to network.scheme_fee_schedule enables ']
  scheme_fee_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_fee_table. Business justification: Fee schedules for products must align with scheme fee tables; FK ensures consistency and supports fee audit reports.']
  applicable_card_type varchar [note: 'Card types eligible for the fee.']
  applicable_mcc varchar [note: 'MCC values for which the fee applies.']
  applicable_product_code varchar [note: 'Product code to which this fee schedule applies.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the fee is valid.']
  applicable_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Transaction message types to which the fee applies.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to compute the fee.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing the fee (e.g., PSD2, PCI DSS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PSD2|PCI_DSS|SOX|GDPR|CCPA|FATF|OFAC|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount charged per applicable event, expressed in the fee_currency.']
  fee_application_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the fee.']
  fee_category varchar [note: 'Category of the fee such as transaction, monthly, setup, chargeback, FX, BNPL, interchange, MDR, MSF. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transaction|monthly|annual|setup|chargeback|fx|bnpl|interchange|mdr|msf|other — promote to reference product]']
  fee_code varchar [note: 'Business‑defined unique code for the fee schedule entry.']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the fee purpose and calculation.']
  fee_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fee (e.g., "MDR Tier 1", "FX Markup").']
  fee_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage‑based rate applied to the transaction amount (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.5%).']
  fee_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the fee cost.']
  fee_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee is subject to tax.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'True if the fee is part of a promotional offer.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee recurs on a schedule.']
  max_fee_cap decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper limit on the fee amount that can be charged.']
  min_fee_floor decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound on the fee amount that will be charged.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business rationale for the fee.']
  product_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee schedule.']
  promotional_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional fee expires.']
  promotional_start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional fee becomes effective.']
  recurrence_interval varchar [note: 'Interval at which a recurring fee is applied.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the fee has been validated against applicable regulations (e.g., PSD2, PCI).']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (e.g., 0.0700 for 7%).']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Ordinal number of the tier when the fee uses a tiered structure.']
  tier_max_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum transaction volume or amount for this tier.']
  tier_min_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum transaction volume or amount that qualifies for this tier.']
  tier_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Rate applied within this tier (percentage or flat).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master fee schedule defining all fee types, rates, and calculation methods applicable to a payment product or pricing plan. Covers MDR tiers, MSF rates, interchange pass-through configurations, monthly maintenance fees, transaction fees, chargeback fees, FX markup fees, and BNPL origination fees. Each schedule is versioned and effective-dated to support regulatory and commercial changes.'
}

Table product.fee_schedule_line {
  fee_schedule_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each fee schedule line record.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fee schedule to which this line belongs.']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount applied when rate_type is flat.']
  applicable_product_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product (e.g., card program, BNPL) for which the fee is applicable.']
  applicable_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction that triggers this fee line.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates if the fee line is subject to specific compliance regimes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fee schedule line was first inserted.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the fee line\'s validity period.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the fee line\'s validity period; null indicates indefinite.']
  fee_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the fee (e.g., interchange, network, service).']
  fee_component_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the fee component (e.g., "MDR Flat Fee").']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing context for the fee component.']
  fee_schedule_line_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the fee line.']
  fee_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the fee definition (e.g., internal policy, partner agreement, regulatory mandate).']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'True if the fee is subject to tax, otherwise false.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the record.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential position of the line within the fee schedule.']
  max_cap decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound on the fee amount for this line.']
  min_cap decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound on the fee amount for this line.']
  percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage applied to the transaction amount when rate_type is percentage.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Calculation method for the fee line.']
  regulatory_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory rule or standard that mandates or influences the fee.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage tax rate applied to the fee when is_taxable is true.']
  tier_end_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount that qualifies for this tier.']
  tier_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage fee applied to transactions whose amount falls within the tier range.']
  tier_start_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount that qualifies for this tier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the fee line.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the fee line for audit and change management.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item entries within a fee schedule, each representing a single fee component with its own rate type (flat, percentage, tiered, blended), applicable transaction type, currency, minimum/maximum cap, and effective date range. Enables granular fee modeling for complex MDR structures, interchange-plus pricing, and tiered volume discounts.'
}

Table product.product_pricing_plan {
  product_pricing_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the pricing plan record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Pricing plans are negotiated with partners; the FK supports partner‑specific pricing rules and audit.']
  pricing_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Pricing Impact: Pricing plans affect transaction fees and discounts; linking to a GL account enables automated posting of pricing-related revenue and expense.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule definition applied by the plan.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.interchange_program. Business justification: Pricing plans are built on specific interchange programs; linking allows automated plan generation and regulatory reporting of program‑based rates.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the plan received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state of the plan.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that approved the plan.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the compliance review for the plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing plan record was first created in the system.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Discount applied to fees within the tier, expressed as a decimal fraction.']
  discount_tier_end decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction volume for a discount tier.']
  discount_tier_start decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction volume for a discount tier.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the pricing plan ceases to be binding (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the pricing plan becomes binding for the counterparties.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which merchants or cardholders may use the plan.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partners or legacy systems to reference the plan.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed fee component of the plan, expressed in the plan currency.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum fee that can be charged under the plan per defined period.']
  fee_cap_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the fee cap (per transaction, monthly, or annual).']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Variable fee component expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50%).']
  is_default_plan boolean [note: 'True if this plan is the default offering for its category.']
  is_test_plan boolean [note: 'True if the plan is used for testing or sandbox environments.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction value for which the plan fees apply.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction value for which the plan fees apply.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the plan.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the product offering (e.g., transaction‑based, subscription, BNPL).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the pricing plan, used in contracts and merchant agreements.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the plan applies to merchants, cardholders, or both.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing methodology used in the plan.']
  product_pricing_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the plan, including purpose and scope.']
  product_pricing_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  promotional_discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Discount rate applied during the promotional window.']
  promotional_window_end date [note: 'End date of any promotional pricing period.']
  promotional_window_start date [note: 'Start date of any promotional pricing period.']
  regulatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan has passed all required regulatory approvals.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the plan was offered to the counterparties.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the plan was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for plan termination (e.g., regulatory, business decision).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the pricing plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the plan for change management.']

  Note: 'Commercial pricing plan assigned to merchants or cardholders, bundling a fee schedule with interchange model (flat-rate, interchange-plus, tiered, blended), volume discount thresholds, and promotional pricing windows. Tracks plan version, effective dates, approval status, and the sales channel through which the plan was offered. SSOT for the commercial pricing agreement at the plan level.'
}

Table product.eligibility_rule {
  eligibility_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the eligibility rule.']
  payment_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.payment_corridor. Business justification: Eligibility rules restrict products by payment corridor (e.g., only EU→UK corridor eligible for certain card programs due to regulatory or risk constraints). Business need: corridor-based product elig']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment product or pricing plan governed by this rule.']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Last six digits of the issuing BIN that marks the end of the applicable range.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'First six digits of the issuing BIN that marks the start of the applicable range.']
  cardholder_segment varchar [note: 'Segment label of cardholders (e.g., consumer, corporate) to which the rule applies.']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Logical expression (e.g., SQL‑like or DSL) that defines the rule criteria.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for geographic eligibility; empty when not a geography rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the rule expires; null for open‑ended.']
  eligibility_rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the rule purpose and business rationale.']
  eligibility_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an authorized user can override the rule for a specific case.']
  kyc_tier varchar [note: 'KYC verification tier required for eligibility.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'MCC that the rule filters on; empty when not an MCC‑type rule.']
  merchant_segment varchar [note: 'Segment label of merchants (e.g., enterprise, SMB) to which the rule applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the rule.']
  override_authority varchar [note: 'Entity authorized to perform an override when allowed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating rule precedence when multiple rules apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Sub‑national region or state code used for finer geographic filtering.']
  risk_score_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest risk‑score value (inclusive) for which the rule applies.']
  risk_score_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest risk‑score value (inclusive) for which the rule applies.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of what the rule applies to (e.g., merchant, cardholder, segment).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the rule within the product catalog.']
  rule_logic varchar [note: 'Indicates whether matching entities are included or excluded by the rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rule used in UI and reports.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of eligibility filter expressed by the rule.']
  tpv_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative TPV amount that must be met or exceeded for the rule to be applicable.']
  transaction_amount_max decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount for which the rule applies.']
  transaction_amount_min decimal(18,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount (in the product currency) for which the rule applies.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rule definition; increments on each change.']

  Note: 'Business rules governing which merchants, cardholders, or segments are eligible for a given payment product, pricing plan, or promotional offer. Captures rule type (MCC restriction, BIN range filter, geography, TPV threshold, KYC tier, risk score band), rule logic (include/exclude), effective dates, and override authority. Used by onboarding and agreement workflows to validate product assignment.'
}

Table product.version {
  version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product version record.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Product versions track changes to a payment product; child version references parent payment_product.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AML screening is performed for transactions using this product.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual/committee that approved the version.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the version was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the approval workflow for this version.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining why the product definition was changed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the product version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the version record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when this product version becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when this product version ceases to be binding (null for open‑ended).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which merchants or cardholders may use the product.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Primary monetary fee associated with the product version.']
  interchange_fee_category varchar [note: 'Category of interchange fee applied to the product.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for contactless transactions.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for cross‑border payments.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for online e‑commerce transactions.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product supports tokenization of payment credentials.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification is required for this product.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed transaction amount for this product version.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum allowed transaction amount for this product version.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the product version.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the product in external systems.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment product.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment product (e.g., card, BNPL, P2P, A2A, virtual account, digital wallet).']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category (e.g., PCI‑DSS, PSD2, AML) applicable to the product.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the product version.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard settlement timing for the product (e.g., T+0, T+1).']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed surcharge amount applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge may be applied to the product.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used for tokenizing payment data (e.g., DPAN, HCE, Token Service Provider).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the version record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the product definition.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product version.']

  Note: 'Tracks the versioned lifecycle of a payment product definition — capturing each material change to product terms, fee structures, regulatory classifications, or feature sets. Stores version number, change reason, approval authority, effective date, and the delta from the prior version. Supports regulatory audit trails and backward-compatible product agreement references.'
}

Table product.rate_tier {
  rate_tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the rate tier definition.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_fee_schedule. Business justification: rate_tier is described as \'volume-based rate tier definitions used within pricing plans AND fee schedules.\' Currently rate_tier only has product_pricing_plan_id, but product_fee_schedule also has embe']
  product_pricing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_pricing_plan. Business justification: Rate tiers are defined within a pricing plan; linking rate_tier to product_pricing_plan clarifies hierarchy and eliminates the redundant payment_product_id column.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of internal compliance review for the tier definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tier record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the tier becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the tier expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules or conditions that must be satisfied for a merchant or portfolio to qualify for this tier.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum fee that can be charged under this tier, if a cap applies.']
  fee_cap_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or basis of the fee cap (e.g., per transaction, per month, per year).']
  is_default_tier boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this tier is the default when no other tier matches.']
  max_volume decimal(20,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the volume band for this tier.']
  min_volume decimal(20,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the volume band that triggers this tier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the tier.']
  rate_tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tier.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the tier rate is a percentage of the transaction amount or a fixed monetary amount.']
  rate_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the rate applied within the tier (percentage expressed as decimal or fixed amount).']
  regulatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tier has received required regulatory sign‑off.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the tier in pricing rules.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing tier.']
  tier_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve overlapping tiers; lower numbers have higher precedence.']
  tier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tier based on the metric it governs (e.g., volume‑based, transaction‑based, value‑based).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tier record.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit used for the volume thresholds (e.g., transaction count or currency).']

  Note: 'Volume-based rate tier definitions used within pricing plans and fee schedules — specifying TPV bands (e.g., $0–$10K, $10K–$100K, $100K+) and the corresponding MDR or MSF rate applicable at each tier. Supports both merchant-level and portfolio-level tiered pricing models. Effective-dated to support periodic rate reviews.'
}

  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Interchange qualification rules differ by currency pair (domestic vs cross-border, specific currency combinations). Business need: scheme compliance requires currency-specific interchange qualificatio']

  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Interchange qualification settings apply to a payment product; child qualification references parent payment_product.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Interchange qualification categories (Visa CPS, Mastercard Merit, Amex OptBlue) are scheme-specific. Linking interchange_qualification to scheme enables scheme-specific qualification rule management, ']
  authorization_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to obtain transaction authorization.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of card instrument the qualification applies to.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the qualification against applicable standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the qualification becomes effective for new transactions.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the qualification is no longer applicable (null for open‑ended).']
  entry_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which card data was entered for the transaction.']
  interchange_category varchar [note: 'Resulting interchange rate category that the qualification maps to.']
  interchange_qualification_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the qualification logic and any special notes.']
  interchange_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification configuration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.']
  qualification_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in downstream systems to reference this qualification configuration.']
  qualification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the qualification rule set (e.g., "Visa CPS Retail Qualification").']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of product to which the qualification applies (e.g., card program, BNPL offering, P2P transfer).']
  regulatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'True if the qualification has received formal regulatory approval.']
  sca_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction flow meets Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the qualification record.']
  version_effective_from date [note: 'Date when this version of the qualification became effective.']
  version_effective_until date [note: 'Date when this version ceased to be effective (null if current).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier for change management.']

  Note: 'Product-level interchange qualification configuration mapping card program and transaction attributes (card type, entry mode, MCC, authorization method, SCA compliance) to the expected interchange rate category (e.g., Visa CPS Retail, MC Merit III). Distinct from the interchange rate table owned by the interchange domain — this entity defines the qualification logic for a product\'s expected interchange outcome.'
}

  Note: 'Master definition of Peer-to-Peer transfer products offered on the platform — including instant P2P, scheduled P2P, and cross-border P2P variants. Distinct from A2A rail products which are bank-to-bank.'
}

Captures supported rails, settlement windows, transaction limits, STP eligibility, ISO 20022 message schema version, and regulatory reporting obligations (Reg E, PSD2). Distinct from P2P products which are consumer-wallet-funded.'

Operations require tracking which partner provides each virtual account product for settlement, compliance, and']
  liability_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Virtual account products hold customer balances that must be tracked as liabilities on the balance sheet. Financial reporting and regulatory capital adequacy calculations require mapping virtual accou']
  nostro_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.nostro_account. Business justification: Virtual accounts for cross-border use must be backed by specific nostro accounts for settlement. Business need: liquidity management and settlement routing for multi-currency virtual accounts require ']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule that defines fees applicable to this product.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Virtual accounts are e-money instruments regulated under EMD2/PSD2 in EU, state money transmitter laws in US. Compliance teams must map obligations to products for licensing and audit purposes.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Virtual accounts inherit scheme rules (tokenization, KYC, fee models); a FK provides accurate scheme association for audit and compliance.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the virtual account becomes active and usable.']
  allowed_mcc_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of Merchant Category Codes permitted for transactions on this virtual account.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening is performed on the account holder.']
  bin_end varchar [note: 'Last six digits of the Bank Identification Number range assigned to this product.']
  bin_start varchar [note: 'First six digits of the Bank Identification Number range assigned to this product.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification that groups the product for reporting and eligibility rules.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the product meets all applicable compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was first created in the system.']
  daily_spend_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate spend allowed per calendar day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the product becomes effective for issuance.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the product ceases to be offered (null if open‑ended).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the virtual account automatically expires if not used.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account can be used for contactless transactions.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account can be used for cross‑border payments.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account can be used for online e‑commerce transactions.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account number can be tokenized for secure usage.']
  issuance_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'One‑time fee charged when the virtual account is created.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if Know‑Your‑Customer verification is mandatory for activation.']
  maintenance_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Recurring fee for maintaining the virtual account product.']
  max_balance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound on the cumulative balance that can be held in the virtual account.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute ceiling for any single transaction processed with this virtual account.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Floor amount below which a transaction will be declined.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Business‑defined alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the product in catalogs and pricing systems.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account is single‑use, multi‑use, or a virtual IBAN.']
  promo_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product can be included in promotional offers.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the product.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification used for underwriting and fraud monitoring.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which funds are settled to the underlying account.']
  spend_control_rules varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded rules that define merchant category restrictions, geographic limits, and velocity controls.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge may be applied to transactions using this product.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party token service provider, if tokenization is enabled.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to tokenize the virtual account number (Token Service Provider, Host Card Emulation, or none).']
  transaction_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for a single transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the product record.']
  virtual_account_product_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the virtual account product, including marketing and functional details.']
  virtual_account_product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the virtual account product.']
  virtual_account_product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product.']

  Note: 'Master definition of virtual account products — single-use virtual card numbers, multi-use virtual accounts, and virtual IBANs issued for B2B payables, expense management, or marketplace disbursements. Captures account type, BIN assignment, spending controls, expiry logic, tokenization method (TSP/HCE), and the issuing program it belongs to.'
}

Table product.feature {
  feature_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product feature.']
  fee_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Features with fee_amount or fee_percentage generate incremental revenue requiring GL mapping. Revenue recognition and product margin analysis need to allocate feature fees to appropriate GL revenue ac']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Features may be mandated by particular regulations; linking tracks required regulatory obligations per feature.']
  activation_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules or conditions that must be satisfied for the feature to be activated (e.g., eligibility criteria, risk thresholds).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the feature record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the feature becomes available for activation.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the feature is retired or no longer available (null if indefinite).']
  feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the feature in configurations and contracts.']
  feature_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the feature, its purpose and behavior.']
  feature_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the feature.']
  feature_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feature.']
  feature_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the feature based on its functional role.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount charged when the feature is enabled (applicable when fee_impact_type = \'fixed\').']
  fee_impact_type varchar [note: 'How the feature influences pricing (no impact, fixed fee, percentage fee, or tiered fee).']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of transaction amount charged when the feature is enabled (applicable when fee_impact_type = \'percentage\').']
  is_enabled_by_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feature is enabled by default on new product instances.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the feature.']
  regulatory_dependency varchar [note: 'Regulations or standards that govern the use of this feature.']
  requires_3ds boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3‑D Secure authentication is required when the feature is used.']
  requires_aml boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening is required for the feature.']
  requires_contactless boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless (NFC) capability is required for the feature.']
  requires_dcc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Dynamic Currency Conversion is required for the feature.']
  requires_international boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feature can be used for cross‑border or international payments.']
  requires_kyc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification is required to enable the feature.']
  requires_online boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feature is applicable only to online transactions.']
  requires_tokenization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tokenization of payment credentials is mandatory for the feature.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating associated with the feature (higher values indicate greater risk).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the feature record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the feature definition, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Catalog of discrete features and capabilities that can be enabled or disabled on a payment product — e.g., contactless NFC, 3DS authentication, DCC, instalment conversion, rewards accrual, spend controls, real-time notifications, and open banking data sharing. Each feature has its own activation rules, regulatory dependencies, and fee implications. Supports modular product configuration.'
}

Table product.feature_assignment {
  feature_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each product-feature assignment record.']
  feature_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the feature (e.g., 3DS, DCC, tokenization) being enabled for the product.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment product or card program to which the feature is assigned.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the feature became active for the product.']
  agreement_code varchar [note: 'Reference code of the merchant or cardholder agreement that triggered the feature activation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the governance review for enabling the feature.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that approved the feature activation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or source through which the feature was activated (e.g., online portal, API call, partner integration).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the feature assignment.']
  configuration_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded key‑value pairs for feature‑specific settings (e.g., 3DS version, DCC markup rate).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the assignment record was first created in the system.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the feature was de‑activated or expired; null if still active.']
  feature_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feature assignment.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feature is the default setting for the product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or audit trail notes related to the assignment.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with enabling the feature for the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the assignment record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking changes to the assignment record.']

  Note: 'Association entity recording which features are enabled on a specific payment product or card program, including activation date, configuration parameters (e.g., 3DS version, DCC markup rate), approval status, and the channel or agreement that triggered the feature activation. Enables per-product feature matrix management.'
}

Table product.limit {
  limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each product limit definition.']
  bnpl_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bnpl_plan. Business justification: The limit table has a bnpl_credit_exposure_limit field and max_installments/installment_interval_days fields that are clearly BNPL-specific limit configurations. Limits can be scoped to specific BNPL ']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.payment_product']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric value representing the primary monetary threshold for the limit.']
  atm_withdrawal_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cash withdrawal amount per ATM transaction.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit actions or change rationale.']
  bnpl_credit_exposure_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit exposure allowed for a BNPL product.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the limit has been reviewed and approved for compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit record was first created.']
  cross_border_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed for cross‑border transactions.']
  daily_cumulative_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for all transactions within a calendar day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the limit becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the limit expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  enforcement_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the limit is a hard block (transaction declined) or a soft alert.']
  installment_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between successive BNPL installments.']
  is_cross_border_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the limit applies to cross‑border transactions.']
  limit_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the limit, indicating the business purpose.']
  limit_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the limit in downstream systems.']
  limit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context for the limit.']
  limit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the limit (e.g., "Daily Transaction Cap").']
  limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the limit definition.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the limit applies per transaction (single) or over a period (cumulative).']
  max_installments varchar [note: 'Maximum number of installments allowed for BNPL plans.']
  monthly_cumulative_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for all transactions within a month.']
  period varchar [note: 'Time window to which a cumulative limit applies.']
  regulatory_rationale varchar [note: 'Explanation of the regulatory requirement that drives this limit (e.g., AML, PSD2).']
  risk_rationale varchar [note: 'Business or risk‑based justification for the limit (e.g., fraud exposure).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the limit record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version for optimistic concurrency control.']
  weekly_cumulative_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for all transactions within a week.']

  Note: 'Defines transaction and velocity limits configured at the product level — single transaction maximum, daily/weekly/monthly cumulative limits, cross-border limits, ATM withdrawal caps, and BNPL credit exposure limits. Captures limit type, currency, enforcement mode (hard block vs. soft alert), and the regulatory or risk rationale for each limit. Distinct from cardholder-level spending controls.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all ecosystem partners in the payments network — acquiring banks, issuing banks, card schemes, PSPs, ISOs, PayFacs, aggregators, TSPs, and technology partners. Owns the authoritative partner identity including legal entity name, partner type classification, registration jurisdiction, regulatory license numbers, PCI DSS compliance status, network participation flags, operational status, and onboarding lifecycle stage. SSOT for partner identity across the enterprise.'
}

  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner profile.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the partner.']

  Note: 'Extended business profile for each ecosystem partner capturing operational details beyond identity — headquarters address, primary contact information, website, business description, annual TPV tier, supported payment rails (ACH, SWIFT, RTP, card), geographic footprint (countries served), supported currencies, API capability flags, and partner tier classification (Gold, Silver, Bronze). Complements ecosystem_partner with rich operational metadata.'
}

Table partner.agreement {
  agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each partner agreement.']

Standard practice in fintech finance operations for cost all']

Business justification: Partner agreements must specify the default currency pair for cross-border settlements and FX conversions. Critical for multi-currency contract execution, settlement processing, and revenue share calc']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the ecosystem partner associated with this agreement.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: Agreements often embed an FX fee schedule governing cross‑border fees; required for invoicing and compliance.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Links revenue‑share agreements to a GL account for accurate revenue recognition and compliance reporting.']
  partner_contact_id varchar [note: 'FK to partner.partner_contact']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Partnership agreements must satisfy specific regulatory obligations (PCI-DSS compliance, data privacy laws, AML requirements). Contract management systems track which regulations each agreement addres']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Partner agreements reference consolidated risk assessments (risk_rating, aml_screening_status, kyc_completed_flag attributes present). Link to risk_profile provides authoritative risk tier, compliance']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Agreements are often scheme‑specific (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) and the scheme context is needed for fee, rule, and regulatory compliance calculations.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the agreement, often referenced in legal and operational documents.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the partnership contract (e.g., acquiring bank, ISO/PayFac, Token Service Provider, technology partner, network participation).']
  amendment_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the latest amendment to the agreement (e.g., v1.2).']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent anti‑money‑laundering screening for the partner.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during audits or reviews of the agreement.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiry.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the agreement data must be retained for regulatory compliance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement expires or terminates; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement becomes legally binding.']
  irf_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'IRF rate applicable to the partner, expressed as a percentage.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner has exclusive rights for the covered product or service.']
  kyc_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification has been completed for the partner.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the agreement record.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction amount permitted for the partner.']
  max_transaction_volume decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate transaction volume the partner is allowed to process under the agreement.']
  mdr_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard MDR applied to the partner\'s transactions, expressed as a percentage.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry that a renewal notice must be issued.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction value shared with the partner when revenue_share_type = percentage.']
  revenue_share_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate revenue sharing (percentage of volume, fixed fee, or tiered structure).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the partner based on underwriting criteria.']
  settlement_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between transaction processing and settlement for the partner.']
  signatory_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the signatory for correspondence and audit trails.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual signing the agreement on behalf of the partner.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the signatory within the partner organization.']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the SLA tier committed to the partner.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Textual description of the conditions under which the agreement may be terminated.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Contractual agreement records between Payments Fintech and ecosystem partners — covering acquiring agreements, ISO/PayFac sponsorship agreements, network participation agreements, TSP licensing agreements, and technology partner contracts. Captures agreement type, effective date, expiration date, auto-renewal flag, governing law jurisdiction, signatory details, MDR/IRF revenue share terms reference, SLA tier reference, termination clauses, and agreement status lifecycle (draft, active, suspended, terminated).'
}

  revenue_sharing_model varchar [note: 'Model used to calculate revenue share with the partner.']
  risk_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned after assessment.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of sanctions list checking.']
  sla_commitment_level varchar [note: 'Level of service‑level agreement committed to the partner.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the onboarding record.']

  Note: 'Partner onboarding application record tracking the full lifecycle of a new partner joining the ecosystem — from initial application submission through KYC/KYB verification, due diligence, compliance screening (OFAC/AML), technical integration assessment, and final approval or rejection. Captures application date, applicant legal entity, partner type requested, assigned onboarding analyst, KYC status, AML screening result, PCI DSS attestation status, approval decision, rejection reason codes, and go-live date.'
}

  usage_reset_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which usage quota resets.']

  Note: 'API integration credentials issued to partners for accessing Payments Fintech platform services — gateway APIs, tokenization APIs, reporting APIs, and webhook endpoints. Captures credential type (API key, OAuth2 client, mTLS certificate), environment (sandbox, production), issued date, expiration date, last rotation date, permission scopes, rate limit tier, IP allowlist, revocation status, and associated SDK version. Critical for partner integration security and access governance.'
}

Table partner.revenue_share_schedule {
  revenue_share_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the revenue share schedule record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Revenue share schedules must align with accounting periods for proper revenue recognition timing, period-end close processes, and ensuring revenue is recognized in the correct financial reporting peri']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the master agreement that governs the revenue share terms.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ecosystem partner associated with this revenue share schedule.']
  bonus_structure_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any bonus or performance‑based components of the schedule.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance review outcome for the schedule.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance approval was performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue share schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary values in the schedule.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the revenue share schedule becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the revenue share schedule expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  incentive_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary volume threshold that triggers additional incentive payments.']
  irf_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pass‑through rate applied to the Interchange Reimbursement Fee (expressed as a decimal fraction of the fee).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule.']
  mdr_split_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the MDR that is allocated to the partner.']
  msf_tier varchar [note: 'Tier identifier (e.g., A, B, C) that determines the MSF sharing percentage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or operational comments.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often revenue share amounts are calculated and paid to the partner.']
  residual_income_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate applied to residual income calculations for referral partners (e.g., ISO/PayFac).']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the revenue share schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Category of revenue sharing mechanism (e.g., Interchange Reimbursement Fee pass‑through, Merchant Discount Rate split, Merchant Service Fee tier, bonus, or incentive).']
  tier_logic_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining how volume bands map to rates and percentages.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue share schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the schedule for change tracking.']
  volume_threshold varchar [note: 'Transaction volume (count) required to qualify for the defined tier or incentive.']

  Note: 'Revenue sharing schedule defining the economic terms between Payments Fintech and a partner — IRF pass-through rates, MDR split percentages, MSF sharing tiers, volume-based incentive thresholds, residual income rates for ISO/PayFac referrals, and bonus structures. Captures effective date, expiration date, tiering logic (volume bands), currency, payment frequency (monthly, quarterly), and the associated partner agreement. SSOT for partner economics.'
}

  revenue_share_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.revenue_share_schedule. Linking the statement directly to the schedule that drove its calculation is essential for t']
  adjustments_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of chargebacks, refunds, penalties, and other adjustments affecting the partner\'s share.']

  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  gross_interchange_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total interchange fees earned from transactions processed through the partner before any deductions.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the invoice generated for the partner payout.']

  mdr_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total MDR collected from merchants and attributed to the partner.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue after subtracting MDR and IRF from gross interchange.']
  partner_share_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of net revenue allocated to the partner as per agreement.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the partner must be paid for the statement.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payout to the partner.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes related to the statement.']
  revenue_share_statement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the statement.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payout was settled in the financial system.']

  statement_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the statement period.']
  statement_type varchar [note: 'Frequency of the statement issuance.']
  total_payable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable to the partner after all adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the statement record.']

  Note: 'Periodic revenue share statement generated for each partner — capturing the calculation period, total TPV processed through the partner, gross interchange earned, MDR collected, IRF paid to networks, net revenue, partner share amount, adjustments (chargebacks, refunds, penalties), and final payable amount. Supports partner billing reconciliation and Oracle Financials AP integration for partner payouts.'
}

Table partner.sla_commitment {
  sla_commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SLA commitment record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.agreement. Business justification: Link SLA commitment to its governing agreement for traceability; agreement_number string is redundant.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the ecosystem partner to which this SLA applies.']
  escalation_partner_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_contact. Business justification: sla_commitment carries three denormalized escalation contact fields (escalation_contact_email, escalation_contact_name, escalation_contact_phone) that duplicate data already managed in partner_contact']
  breach_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the SLA has been breached.']
  breach_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent SLA breach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA commitment record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the SLA commitment expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the SLA commitment becomes effective.']
  last_measured_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent metric measurement for this SLA.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the metric is measured within the measurement window.']
  measurement_frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for measurement frequency (day, hour, minute).']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method or system used to capture the metric (e.g., real‑time monitoring, batch report).']
  measurement_window varchar [note: 'Length of the time window over which the metric is measured.']
  measurement_window_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the measurement window (day, hour, minute).']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the performance metric measured against the SLA (e.g., "Uptime Percentage").']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments regarding the SLA commitment.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied for each breach of the SLA.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of penalty terms and conditions.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often SLA performance reports are generated.']
  reporting_frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for reporting frequency (day, hour, minute).']
  reporting_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which SLA performance is reported to the partner (e.g., API, dashboard, email).']
  sla_commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA commitment.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Category of the SLA commitment (e.g., uptime guarantee, authorization response time).']
  target_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target value (e.g., percent, seconds).']
  target_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric target that the partner must achieve for the metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SLA commitment record.']

  Note: 'SLA commitments defined for each partner relationship — covering uptime guarantees, authorization response time targets (TPS thresholds), settlement cutoff SLAs, dispute resolution turnaround times, API availability SLAs, and support response tiers. Captures SLA type, metric name, target value, measurement unit, measurement window, breach penalty terms, and effective period. Distinct from generic product SLAs — these are partner-specific negotiated commitments.'
}

Critical for routing decisions and interchange qualification.'
}
