Table partner.integration_endpoint {
  integration_endpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the integration endpoint configuration.']
  partner_api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the stored credential (API key, certificate, secret) used for authentication.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to authenticate calls to the endpoint.']
  change_log varchar [note: 'Free‑form log of configuration changes and version history.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance posture of the endpoint configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the endpoint record was first created.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date when the endpoint is scheduled to be retired.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to technical documentation or integration guide.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the endpoint configuration becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the endpoint configuration expires or is superseded (null if indefinite).']
  encryption_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used for payload encryption.']
  endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Full URL or network address used to invoke the partner API or messaging service.']
  environment varchar [note: 'Operational environment where the endpoint is provisioned.']
  error_handling_policy varchar [note: 'Policy dictating how errors from the endpoint are processed.']
  health_check_url varchar [note: 'Endpoint used by monitoring tools to verify service health.']
  integration_endpoint_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the endpoint purpose and usage.']
  integration_endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the integration endpoint.']
  integration_endpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the endpoint.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address of the endpoint (if applicable).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the endpoint is currently enabled for traffic.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the endpoint has been deprecated.']
  last_test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent connectivity test.']
  max_retries varchar [note: 'Maximum number of retry attempts allowed.']
  message_format varchar [note: 'Standard format of messages exchanged on the endpoint.']
  monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether continuous health monitoring is active.']
  monitoring_endpoint varchar [note: 'URL or address of the monitoring service for this endpoint.']
  network_zone varchar [note: 'Logical network zone where the endpoint resides.']
  owner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the internal owner responsible for the endpoint.']
  owner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the internal owner responsible for the endpoint.']
  payload_encryption boolean [note: 'Indicates whether request/response payloads are encrypted.']
  port varchar [note: 'TCP/UDP port used for the connection.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol used by the endpoint (e.g., REST, SOAP, ISO 8583, ISO 20022, SFTP, Webhook).']
  rate_limit_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of requests allowed per minute.']
  retry_policy varchar [note: 'Strategy applied when a request fails.']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed response time per service level agreement.']
  sla_uptime_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Targeted service availability expressed as a percentage.']
  supported_operations varchar [note: 'Directionality of traffic the endpoint supports.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the last connectivity test.']
  timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum time in seconds to wait for a response before aborting.']
  tls_version varchar [note: 'Transport Layer Security version required for the connection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the endpoint record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label of the endpoint configuration (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Technical integration endpoint configurations for partner API and messaging connections — REST API endpoints, ISO 8583 socket connections, ISO 20022 messaging endpoints, SFTP batch file exchange endpoints, and webhook delivery URLs. Captures endpoint URL, protocol type, message format standard (ISO 8583, ISO 20022, JSON REST), environment (sandbox/production), TLS version, authentication method, timeout settings, retry policy, and health check URL.'
}

Table partner.partner_settlement_account {
  partner_settlement_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the partner settlement account record.']

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Settlement accounts are tied to cost centers for treasury budget tracking, cash flow forecasting by department, and cost center-level liquidity management.   ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the partner that owns this settlement account.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Required for mapping each partner settlement transaction to a GL account for financial reporting and audit compliance.']
  nostro_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.nostro_account. Business justification: Settlement accounts are linked to internal Nostro accounts for liquidity management of cross‑border flows.']
  account_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the settlement account.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual or entity that holds the settlement account.']

  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the settlement account for accounting and payout purposes.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement account record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the settlement account data must be retained to satisfy regulatory obligations.']
  designated_use varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which payouts are made to this account.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the settlement account is no longer valid for payouts (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the settlement account became effective for payouts.']
  iban varchar [note: 'Standardized international account identifier used for cross‑border payouts.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance or risk audit performed on the account.']
  partner_settlement_account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the settlement account.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the settlement account based on AML/KYC and fraud monitoring.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Domestic routing number used for ACH transfers in the United States.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for disbursing funds to the settlement account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement account record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement account was successfully verified.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the account verification process.']

  Note: 'Bank account and settlement account details registered for partner payouts — revenue share disbursements, referral commissions, and incentive payments. Captures account holder name, bank name, account type, IBAN, BIC/SWIFT code, routing number (ACH), account currency, account verification status, verification date, and designated use (revenue share, penalty, refund). Distinct from merchant settlement accounts — these are partner-level financial accounts.'
}

Table gateway.gateway_api_credential {
  gateway_api_credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the API credential record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner onboarding requires each gateway API credential to be linked to the owning partner for billing, compliance audit, and credential lifecycle management.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_account. Business justification: API credentials in PayFac/gateway models are scoped to specific processing accounts (e.g., separate credentials for card-present vs card-not-present accounts). Credential provisioning, rotation audits']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: API credentials are issued per merchant; linking enables authentication audit, usage reporting, and revocation per merchant.']
  rate_limit_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.rate_limit_policy. Business justification: gateway_api_credential has rate_limit_per_minute (raw integer) — a denormalized rate limiting value that should reference the authoritative rate_limit_policy master record. API credentials are subject']
  allowed_ip_ranges varchar [note: 'Network CIDR blocks from which the credential may be used; empty means unrestricted.']
  audience varchar [note: 'Recipient(s) that the token is intended for, often a service identifier.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking to a detailed audit trail stored in the compliance system.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Specifies the protocol or mechanism by which the credential authenticates API calls.']
  certificate_thumbprint varchar [note: 'SHA‑1/256 hash of the public certificate associated with the credential.']
  client_identifier varchar [note: 'Public identifier for an OAuth client used during token acquisition.']
  client_secret varchar [note: 'Confidential secret associated with an OAuth client credential.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the credential meets current PCI DSS, GDPR, and internal security policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credential record was first created in the system.']
  credential_key varchar [note: 'Public API key or client identifier presented by the merchant/partner when invoking gateway APIs.']
  credential_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name assigned to the credential for easy identification by operations and security teams.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the technical mechanism of the credential (e.g., static API key, OAuth client, HMAC secret, X.509 certificate).']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Organizational classification indicating handling and access controls for the credential data.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date on which the credential is permitted to be used.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date after which the credential must no longer be accepted; null indicates no fixed expiry.']
  encryption_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm employed to protect the credential material.']
  environment varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the credential is intended for live production traffic or testing/sandbox environments.']
  gateway_api_credential_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the credential is usable, disabled, revoked, or awaiting activation.']
  hash_algorithm varchar [note: 'Digest algorithm used for HMAC or signature verification.']
  hmac_secret varchar [note: 'Shared secret used to compute HMAC signatures for request authentication.']
  issuer varchar [note: 'Name of the authority or system that issued the credential.']
  key_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the cryptographic key, used in token headers.']
  last_rotated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credential was most recently rotated.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the last successful API call authenticated with this credential.']
  max_concurrent_sessions varchar [note: 'Upper limit on simultaneous active sessions or tokens for this credential.']
  next_rotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the next required rotation of the credential.']
  regulatory_review_date date [note: 'Date when the credential was last examined for regulatory compliance.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation for why the credential was revoked.']
  revoked_flag boolean [note: 'True if the credential has been revoked and must no longer be accepted.']
  revoked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the revocation became effective.']
  rotation_interval_days varchar [note: 'Policy‑driven interval after which the credential must be regenerated.']
  scopes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of permission scopes granted to the credential (e.g., transactions:read, settlements:write).']
  token_endpoint varchar [note: 'OAuth token endpoint used by the client to obtain access tokens.']
  token_expiry_seconds varchar [note: 'Lifetime of issued access tokens, expressed in seconds.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the token follows Bearer or MAC token semantics.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service account that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the credential record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Running total of successful API calls made with this credential.']
  usage_quota decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total number of allowed invocations or monetary value before the credential is throttled or blocked.']

  Note: 'Master record for merchant and partner API authentication credentials used to access the payment gateway. Stores API keys, OAuth client IDs/secrets, HMAC signing keys, certificate thumbprints, credential rotation schedules, permission scopes, and revocation status. SSOT for gateway authentication identity — every inbound API call is authenticated against this record.'
}

Business justification: Routing rules often have associated fee structures and revenue/expense implications (routing optimization fees, premium routing charges). ']
  routing_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.routing_table. Business justification: Gateway routing rules implement network-layer routing table configurations.   sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.sla_profile.   target_acquirer_acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Primary acquirer endpoint to which matching transactions are routed.']

  gateway_routing_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  is_default_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule serves as the fallback/default routing rule.']
  is_rate_limited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule enforces a transaction rate limit.']
  load_balancing_weight varchar [note: 'Relative weight for load‑balancing among multiple target acquirers.']
  match_criteria varchar [note: 'JSON representation of complex matching conditions not covered by individual columns.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes for operational teams.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Evaluation order; lower numbers are evaluated first.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country/region code for geographic routing.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the rule.']
  routing_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to select among multiple target acquirers.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the rule in external systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the routing rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule logic (e.g., least‑cost, scheme‑preference, geographic, custom).']
  tokenization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the transaction must be tokenized before routing.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction the rule applies to.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule.']

  Note: 'Business-managed transaction routing rule defining how inbound payment requests are directed to acquirer endpoints. Captures rule priority, matching criteria (BIN range, MCC, currency, amount threshold, card scheme, region), target acquirer endpoint, failover sequence, load-balancing weight, effective date range, and rule status. Supports least-cost routing, scheme-preference routing, and geographic routing strategies.'
}

}

Table gateway.request {
  request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound API request record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Payment gateway requests generate revenue recognition obligations under IFRS 15. Linking requests to accounting periods enables period-based revenue analysis, supports cutoff testing for financial aud']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_account. Business justification: Payment authorization requests target a specific cardholder account (credit/debit/prepaid). The gateway applies account-level velocity controls, credit limit checks, and account-status validation per ']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Required for transaction monitoring report linking each gateway request to the originating cardholder profile for fraud detection and compliance.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: Request logs reference captured device fingerprint to feed fraud risk models and support device‑based investigations.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_location. Business justification: Gateway requests originate from specific merchant locations (POS terminals, e-commerce endpoints). Location-level fraud monitoring, PCI scope enforcement, and operational reporting require knowing whi']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant (MID) that originated the request.']
  merchant_integration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.merchant_integration. Business justification: Every inbound API request arrives via a specific merchant integration (API or SDK configuration). Linking request → merchant_integration enables analysis of request patterns per integration type, prot']
  pan_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.pan_record. Business justification: Every gateway payment request is tied to a specific PAN used for the transaction. The gateway uses the PAN record for BIN-based routing, scheme selection, tokenization validation, and fraud scoring. c']
  payment_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.payment_credential. Business justification: The gateway must validate which credential (PIN, CVV, biometric, OTP) was presented in the authorization request to enforce SCA compliance and 3DS authentication flows. PSD2/SCA regulatory requirement']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Transaction request includes payment product identifier to apply correct fee schedule and routing logic.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: FX rate lookup needed during processing of multi‑currency transactions; request stores the applied rate for compliance and reporting.']
  rate_limit_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate‑limit rule that applied (if any).']
  client_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the client application.']
  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'Client‑provided identifier for tracing the request across systems.']
  endpoint_path varchar [note: 'API endpoint path invoked by the request (e.g., /v1/authorize).']
  headers varchar [note: 'Serialized HTTP headers of the inbound request.']
  http_method varchar [note: 'HTTP verb used for the request.']
  idempotency_key varchar [note: 'Client‑provided key to ensure idempotent processing of the request.']
  is_rate_limited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request was blocked or throttled by rate‑limit rules.']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time elapsed from request receipt to completion of initial validation, measured in milliseconds.']
  payload_truncated varchar [note: 'First 256 characters of the request payload for quick inspection (full payload stored elsewhere).']
  processing_node_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the gateway server node that processed the request.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Protocol used for the inbound request (e.g., ISO8583, REST, SOAP).']
  raw_message_hash varchar [note: 'Hash (e.g., SHA‑256) of the raw request message for integrity verification.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the gateway received the API request (event time).']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Business operation the request is attempting (authorization, capture, void, refund, inquiry).']
  schema_version varchar [note: 'Version of the API request schema used by the client.']
  size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the raw request payload in bytes.']
  source_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the client that initiated the request.']
  source_port varchar [note: 'Network port on the client side used for the request.']
  tls_version varchar [note: 'TLS protocol version negotiated for the request.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'User‑agent string supplied by the client application.']
  validation_error_code varchar [note: 'Error code returned when validation_outcome is \'rejected\' or \'error\'.']
  validation_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the gateway\'s initial syntactic/semantic validation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every inbound API request received by the payment gateway before authorization processing. Stores request timestamp, originating MID, TID, API credential reference, request type (authorization, capture, void, refund, inquiry), raw message hash, protocol used, source IP, request size, TLS version, and initial validation outcome. Serves as the immutable entry-point audit record for every gateway interaction.'
}

  pan_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.pan_record. Business justification: Authorization responses must reference the specific PAN authorized or declined for card-level decline rate analysis, fraud pattern detection, and usage tracking (pan_record.usage_count, last_used_time']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Response records the FX rate used for the transaction to support post‑settlement audit and dispute handling.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating gateway request that generated this response.']
  routing_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.routing_decision. Business justification: A gateway response is the outcome of processing a request that was routed via a specific routing decision. Linking response → routing_decision completes the end-to-end transaction chain (request → rou']
  acquirer_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code returned by the acquiring bank or processor.']
  amount_cents varchar [note: 'Transaction amount expressed in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents).']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Issuer-provided code confirming successful authorization; sensitive PCI data.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the card (credit, debit, prepaid, commercial).']
  compliance_check_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text details of any compliance issues detected.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of regulatory compliance checks (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier used to correlate this response with upstream request logs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the response record was first persisted.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reason why the transaction was declined, if applicable.']
  destination_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the merchant endpoint that received the response.']
  destination_port varchar [note: 'Network port on the merchant side used for the response.']
  entry_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which card data was captured.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Code representing any internal processing error that occurred.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the internal error.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the response after retries (e.g., success, failed, partial).']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by the fraud engine.']
  is_fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged as potentially fraudulent.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was processed in test mode.']
  message varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text message included in the response.']
  network_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Measured network round‑trip time in milliseconds.']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds from request receipt to response generation.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Protocol used to deliver the response to the merchant.']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Standard response code indicating approval, decline, or error (e.g., ISO 8583 response codes).']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the gateway sent the response to the merchant.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of retry attempts performed before delivering the final response.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled with the acquiring bank.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the response met the defined SLA latency threshold.']
  source_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the gateway node that sent the response.']
  source_port varchar [note: 'Network port on the gateway used for the response.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Business purpose of the transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the response record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the gateway\'s outbound response for each processed request. Stores response timestamp, linked gateway_request_id, response code, authorization code, decline reason, acquirer response code, processing latency (ms), response protocol, retry count, and final disposition. Enables SLA measurement, decline analysis, and end-to-end latency tracking.'
}

Table gateway.routing_decision {
  routing_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the routing decision record.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank or payment network selected (or considered) for the transaction.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_account. Business justification: Routing decisions are influenced by account type (debit vs credit), account currency, and account status. Debit accounts route differently than credit accounts; multi-currency accounts trigger FX rout']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Routing decisions drive operational costs (acquirer fees, network costs, processing overhead). Cost center allocation enables profitability analysis by routing strategy, supports operational expense b']
  gateway_routing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the routing rule that was evaluated to produce this decision.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: Routing decisions can optimize based on expected interchange rate category to minimize total cost of acceptance. Real business process: predictive cost-optimized routing that considers anticipated int']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that initiated the payment request.']
  multi_network_routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.multi_network_routing. Business justification: When a routing_decision uses least-cost or Durbin-compliant multi-network routing, it must reference the multi_network_routing profile that governed network selection. Required for Durbin Amendment co']
  pan_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.pan_record. Business justification: BIN/IIN-based routing is a core gateway function — the routing decision selects acquirer endpoints based on the PAN\'s BIN range, card scheme, and issuing bank. Linking routing_decision to pan_record e']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Routing decisions consider real‑time FX rates for currency‑based routing rules; storing the rate links decision to the rate source.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.request. Business justification: Every routing decision is made in direct response to a specific inbound API request. The routing_decision table captures which acquirer was selected for a given payment request — without a request_id ']
  scheme_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme_fee_schedule. Business justification: Least-cost routing decisions are driven by scheme fee schedules. Routing decisions must reference the scheme_fee_schedule consulted to select the optimal acquirer, enabling interchange optimization re']
  alternative_acquirer_endpoints varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of other eligible acquirer endpoints evaluated during routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing decision record was first persisted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the request amount.']
  decision_error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code if the routing decision failed.']
  decision_error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable error message associated with a failed routing decision.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the routing decision as applied to the transaction.']
  decision_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation or code describing why the particular endpoint was selected.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the routing decision was evaluated and made.']
  failover_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a failover path was taken due to primary endpoint failure.']
  load_balancing_outcome varchar [note: 'Algorithmic outcome used to select the acquirer endpoint.']
  request_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction that triggered the routing decision.']
  request_protocol varchar [note: 'Protocol used by the originating request (e.g., ISO8583, REST, SOAP, JSON).']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment request arrived (e.g., web, mobile app, POS terminal).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score generated by the fraud detection platform for this request.']
  routing_decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing decision.']
  routing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Measured time in milliseconds from request receipt to routing decision output.']
  selected_acquirer_endpoint varchar [note: 'Endpoint (e.g., URL, host:port) of the acquirer that was chosen to receive the transaction.']
  sla_achieved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual routing latency met the SLA target.']
  sla_target_ms varchar [note: 'Configured service‑level‑agreement target latency for routing decisions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the routing decision record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the real-time routing decision made for each payment request. Records which routing rule was applied, the selected acquirer endpoint, evaluated alternative endpoints, routing latency, load-balancing outcome, failover triggered flag, and the reason for endpoint selection. Provides full auditability of routing logic for dispute resolution, performance tuning, and regulatory review.'
}

Table gateway.failover_event {
  failover_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each failover event record.']
  gateway_routing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the routing rule applied during the failover.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_account. Business justification: Failover events affect transaction processing for specific merchant accounts. Impact assessment, SLA breach reporting, and merchant notification workflows require knowing which merchant_account was af']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant whose transactions were impacted.']
  network_sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.sla. Business justification: Failover events are triggered by SLA threshold breaches. The failover_event.sla_breach_flag is a denormalization signal — the specific network SLA record being breached must be traceable for penalty c']
  primary_failover_acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary (failed) acquirer endpoint.']
  primary_failover_network_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment network (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) involved.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.sla_profile. Business justification: failover_event has sla_breach_flag (boolean) indicating whether the failover caused an SLA breach. Linking failover_event → sla_profile identifies which specific SLA profile was breached during the fa']
  target_endpoint_acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the secondary (fallback) acquirer endpoint.']
  affected_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of the affected transactions (currency assumed USD).']
  affected_transaction_volume varchar [note: 'Count of individual transactions impacted by the failover.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Error code returned by the gateway when the primary endpoint failed.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the failover was triggered by the gateway.']
  failover_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time from failover start to successful recovery, measured in seconds.']
  failover_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the failover event.']
  network_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or investigator comments.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the failover event record was first inserted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the failover event record.']
  recovery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when traffic was successfully restored to the target endpoint.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the geographic region of the impacted transactions.']
  retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of automatic retry attempts made on the primary endpoint prior to failover.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the failover duration exceeded the SLA threshold.']
  source_endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the failed primary endpoint.']
  target_endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fallback endpoint.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of transactions impacted by the failover.']
  trigger_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the primary endpoint was considered unavailable.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each gateway failover occurrence — when a primary acquirer endpoint becomes unavailable and traffic is rerouted to a secondary. Captures failover trigger timestamp, failed endpoint, failover target endpoint, trigger reason (timeout, connection refused, error threshold breach), affected transaction volume, failover duration, and recovery timestamp. Critical for SLA breach analysis and infrastructure resilience reporting.'
}

Table gateway.webhook_subscription {
  webhook_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the webhook subscription record.']

Business justification: A webhook subscription is registered by a merchant for a specific integration — it defines which events from which integration should trigger webhook notifications. webhook_subscription already has me']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Webhook subscriptions are configured per payment product to notify merchants of product-specific events (authorization, settlement, refund, chargeback). Real-world webhook platforms allow merchants to']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate webhook calls to the target URL.']
  authentication_secret varchar [note: 'Secret key or token used with the selected authentication method (e.g., HMAC secret, OAuth bearer token).']
  backoff_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate delay between successive retries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription becomes active.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription expires or is scheduled to terminate (null for indefinite).']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payload is encrypted at rest or in transit.']
  encryption_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the encryption key used to protect the payload.']
  event_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of event identifiers (e.g., authorization.approved,capture.completed) the subscriber wishes to receive.']
  failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive delivery failures.']
  ip_allowlist varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of IP address ranges permitted to receive webhook calls.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription is for testing purposes only.']
  last_failure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent delivery failure.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subscription.']
  max_payload_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed size of the webhook payload in bytes.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of retry attempts before the subscription is marked failed.']

  oauth_client_identifier varchar [note: 'Client identifier when OAuth Bearer authentication is used.']
  oauth_scopes varchar [note: 'Space‑separated list of OAuth scopes granted to the webhook client.']
  payload_format varchar [note: 'Format of the webhook payload delivered to the target URL.']
  rate_limit_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of webhook calls allowed per minute for this subscription.']

  retry_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how failed webhook deliveries are retried.']
  sla_response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Target maximum time (in seconds) for the webhook endpoint to acknowledge a delivery.']
  subscription_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing code used to reference the subscription in external systems and documentation.']
  subscription_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the webhook subscription.']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the subscription purpose.']
  target_url varchar [note: 'Fully qualified HTTPS endpoint where event notifications are delivered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subscription record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number incremented on each configuration change.']
  webhook_secret_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the secret used for HMAC verification of incoming webhook calls.']
  webhook_subscription_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form description providing context about the subscription purpose.']

  Note: 'Master record for each merchant or partner webhook subscription registered with the gateway. Stores target URL, subscribed event types (authorization.approved, capture.completed, dispute.opened, etc.), authentication method (HMAC-SHA256, OAuth bearer), retry policy, maximum retry attempts, backoff strategy, active status, and subscription creation/expiry dates. SSOT for outbound event notification configuration.'
}

Table gateway.webhook_delivery {

  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'UUID used to correlate this delivery with upstream processing logs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery record was first created in the data lake.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the webhook delivery attempt.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the webhook delivery attempt was initiated.']
  endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Full URL of the merchant\'s webhook endpoint.']

  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the business event that triggered the webhook notification.']
  http_status_code varchar [note: 'HTTP response status code returned by the merchant endpoint.']
  is_final_attempt boolean [note: 'True if this attempt is the last allowed retry.']
  max_attempts varchar [note: 'Configured maximum number of retry attempts allowed for the webhook.']
  next_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the next retry attempt when the previous delivery failed.']
  payload_hash varchar [note: 'SHA-256 hash of the webhook payload to verify integrity.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol used for the webhook delivery.']
  response_body_snippet varchar [note: 'Truncated portion of the HTTP response body for diagnostic purposes.']
  response_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Round‑trip time in milliseconds between sending the webhook and receiving the HTTP response.']
  retry_policy varchar [note: 'Configured retry strategy for failed webhook deliveries.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the webhook event.']
  success_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the webhook delivery was successful (true) or not (false).']
  throttling_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether delivery was delayed due to rate‑limiting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each webhook notification delivery attempt made by the gateway. Captures delivery timestamp, linked webhook_subscription_id, event type, payload hash, HTTP response code, response latency, delivery attempt number, retry sequence, success/failure status, and next retry scheduled time. Enables merchant support teams to diagnose missed notifications and replay failed deliveries.'
}

Table gateway.rate_limit_policy {
  rate_limit_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the rate limit policy.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Rate‑limit policies are negotiated per partner contract; linking supports enforcement reporting and partner‑level usage billing.']
  burst_limit varchar [note: 'Number of requests allowed in a short burst exceeding the steady‑state quota.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance rules that the policy must satisfy (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR).']
  consumer_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consumer subject to the policy (e.g., API key, MID, or IP range).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was first created.']
  daily_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum total requests allowed in a calendar day.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy becomes effective.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy expires; null for indefinite.']
  enforcement_action varchar [note: 'Action taken when limits are breached: throttle (delay) or block (reject).']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rate limit policy.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of consumer scope the policy applies to.']
  rate_limit_policy_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the policy.']
  rate_limit_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy.']
  request_quota_rpm varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed requests per minute for the consumer.']
  request_quota_tps varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed requests per second for the consumer.']
  throttle_response_code varchar [note: 'HTTP status code returned when the consumer exceeds the quota.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the service tier (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold) associated with the policy.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or service that last updated the policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the policy.']

  Note: 'Business-managed rate limiting policy applied to API consumers accessing the gateway. Defines the consumer scope (per API credential, per MID, per IP range), request quota (TPS, RPM, daily cap), burst allowance, throttle response code, policy effective period, and enforcement action (throttle vs. block). Supports tiered service levels and abuse prevention without impacting legitimate high-volume merchants.'
}

Table gateway.sla_profile {
  sla_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA profile.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank or network endpoint covered by the SLA.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partners have SLA agreements with the gateway; the FK enables SLA compliance tracking and breach penalty calculations per partner.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which the SLA applies.']
  merchant_integration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.merchant_integration. Business justification: The sla_profile description explicitly states it defines \'SLA commitments for a merchant integration or acquirer endpoint.\' sla_profile already has acquirer_endpoint_id (existing FK) and merchant_id (']
  network_sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.sla. Business justification: Gateway SLA profiles must reference the governing network-level SLA (scheme uptime/latency obligations) to ensure compliance with scheme requirements. Operations and compliance teams trace gateway SLA']
  penalty_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: SLA breaches trigger penalty obligations (payables to merchants) or penalty income (receivables from acquirers). Direct GL account linkage enables automated accrual of SLA-related financial obligation']
  breach_notification_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum time to notify the merchant after an SLA breach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA profile record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the SLA becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the SLA expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  error_rate_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed error/decline rate expressed as a percentage.']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the contact to notify on SLA breach.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the escalation contact.']
  last_measured_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed availability percentage during the last measurement window.']
  last_measured_error_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed error/decline rate in the last measurement window.']
  last_measured_latency_p95_ms varchar [note: 'Observed 95th percentile latency in the last measurement window.']
  last_measured_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent SLA performance measurement.']
  latency_p50_ms varchar [note: '50th percentile (median) authorization response time in milliseconds.']
  latency_p95_ms varchar [note: '95th percentile authorization response time in milliseconds.']
  latency_p99_ms varchar [note: '99th percentile authorization response time in milliseconds.']
  measurement_metric varchar [note: 'Primary metric used for SLA measurement.']
  measurement_window_minutes varchar [note: 'Time window over which SLA metrics are aggregated.']
  monitoring_endpoint varchar [note: 'URL of the endpoint used to monitor SLA metrics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the SLA.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied for SLA breach.']
  remediation_obligation varchar [note: 'Description of remediation steps or penalties applied when SLA is breached.']
  sla_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the SLA agreement, used in contracts and reporting.']
  sla_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA.']
  sla_revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for tracking changes to the SLA.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA (e.g., merchant integration, acquirer endpoint, API service, partner).']
  sla_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the SLA contract (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  target_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Committed service availability expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.95).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SLA profile.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the gateway SLA commitments for a merchant integration or acquirer endpoint. Specifies target availability percentage, maximum authorization latency (p50/p95/p99 in ms), maximum error rate threshold, measurement window, breach notification SLA, and remediation obligations. Linked to merchant agreements and used as the baseline for SLA performance measurement.'
}

Table gateway.threeds_config {
  threeds_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each 3-D Secure configuration record.']
  merchant_integration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.merchant_integration. Business justification: threeds_config is described as \'3-D Secure authentication settings applied per merchant integration.\' Without this FK, threeds_config is completely siloed within the gateway domain — it has no in-doma']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: 3DS configurations are scheme-specific (Visa 3DS2 vs Mastercard Identity Check have distinct protocol rules). Compliance certification and scheme-specific 3DS reporting require knowing which scheme\'s ']
  scheme_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme_parameter. Business justification: 3DS gateway configurations must comply with scheme-mandated parameters (protocol version, exemption thresholds, challenge rules). Compliance audits trace threeds_config settings to the specific scheme']
  acquirer_bin varchar [note: 'Six‑digit BIN of the acquiring bank used for routing the 3‑DS flow.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry that records changes to this configuration.']
  authentication_flow varchar [note: 'Method used to present the 3‑DS challenge to the cardholder.']
  challenge_preference varchar [note: 'Merchant‑specified preference for challenge flow during authentication.']
  challenge_timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Time limit in seconds for the challenge response.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing this configuration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance validation.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the configuration in external systems.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the 3‑DS configuration.']
  config_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the configuration follows a standard template or is custom‑built for a merchant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the configuration becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the configuration is no longer active (null for open‑ended).']
  exemption_rules varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of exemption criteria applied to the configuration.']
  frictionless_flow_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions can be processed without a challenge under this configuration.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is the default for the merchant.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance review of the configuration.']
  max_challenge_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of challenge attempts allowed before transaction decline.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3‑DS protocol applied (e.g., 1.0 or 2.0).']
  requestor_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity (typically the merchant) that initiates the 3‑DS authentication request.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum risk score allowed for frictionless flow; higher scores trigger challenge.']
  sca_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of PSD2 SCA compliance evaluation for the configuration.']
  threeds_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the configuration.']
  threeds_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the 3‑DS configuration.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of transaction to which the configuration applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version string (e.g., 1.2.3) for the configuration.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record for 3-D Secure (3DS v1 and v2) authentication settings applied per merchant integration. Stores 3DS protocol version, requestor ID, acquirer BIN, merchant category code override, challenge preference (no-preference, no-challenge, challenge-requested), exemption rules (TRA, low-value, trusted-beneficiary), frictionless flow eligibility, and SCA compliance status under PSD2. Governs how the gateway invokes 3DS for each merchant.'
}

Table fx.rate {
  rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each FX rate record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Each fx_rate belongs to a currency pair; linking eliminates the free‑text pair field and enforces referential integrity.']
  rate_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate_feed. Business justification: fx_rate records are produced by a specific rate feed and belong to a currency pair; linking removes duplicated source fields and enables traceability to the feed and pair.']
  ask_price decimal(18,9) [note: 'Price at which the market maker is willing to sell the base currency.']
  base_currency varchar [note: 'Currency being quoted (the numerator in the pair).']
  bid_price decimal(18,9) [note: 'Price at which the market maker is willing to buy the base currency.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Provider‑assigned confidence level (0‑100) indicating reliability of the rate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  cross_border_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the rate is used for a cross‑border transaction.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate becomes effective for transaction conversion.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate ceases to be valid; null for open‑ended rates.']
  external_rate_code varchar [note: 'Reference code used by the external provider to identify this specific rate.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate is currently active for use in transaction processing.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate record.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the market where the rate is sourced or applied.']
  mid_price decimal(18,9) [note: 'Average of bid and ask prices; often used as the reference rate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional remarks or manual adjustments.']
  precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places provided for the rate value.']
  quote_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used to express the rate (the denominator in the pair).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the FX rate indicating its purpose and calculation method.']
  rate_value decimal(18,9) [note: 'Primary exchange rate value expressed as the amount of quote currency per one unit of base currency.']
  spread decimal(18,9) [note: 'Difference between ask and bid prices, expressed in quote currency units.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the FX rate record.']

  Note: 'Master record for foreign exchange rates published and consumed by the payments platform. Captures spot rates, mid-market rates, bid/ask spreads, and indicative rates for every currency pair supported across 200+ countries. Sourced from primary rate feed providers (Reuters, Bloomberg, ECB) and internal treasury desks. Serves as the authoritative rate reference for DCC pricing, cross-border transaction conversion, and settlement FX calculations. Includes rate type classification (spot, forward, indicative, guaranteed), effective timestamp, expiry timestamp, rate source, and confidence band.'
}

Table fx.rate_feed {
  rate_feed_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each FX rate feed configuration.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to authenticate the connection to the external feed source.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulations governing the feed (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR, PSD2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate feed record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Category of credential used (e.g., API key, client certificate). Actual secret values are stored in a vault, not in this table.']
  data_format varchar [note: 'File or message format in which rates are delivered.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the feed configuration starts being used for rate ingestion.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the feed configuration is no longer active; null indicates indefinite validity.']
  encryption_protocol varchar [note: 'Specific encryption protocol negotiated for the feed connection.']
  failover_priority varchar [note: 'Rank used to select an alternate feed when the primary feed is unavailable (lower = higher priority).']
  feed_endpoint varchar [note: 'Network address (URL, host:port, or file path) where the rate feed is accessed.']
  feed_protocol varchar [note: 'Technical protocol through which the FX rates are delivered to the platform.']
  is_encrypted boolean [note: 'True if the feed connection uses encryption (TLS/SSH).']
  last_error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the reason for the most recent feed failure.']
  last_error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed message describing the most recent feed retrieval error.']
  last_failure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent poll that resulted in an error.']
  last_success_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the last successful poll of the external rate feed.']
  polling_frequency_seconds varchar [note: 'How often the platform requests new rates from the provider.']
  provider_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code assigned to the provider for contract and reporting purposes.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the external FX rate feed provider (e.g., Reuters Refinitiv, Bloomberg FX).']
  rate_feed_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing context, notes, or special handling instructions for the feed.']
  rate_feed_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the rate feed configuration.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the FX rate (spot, forward, mid‑market, inter‑bank).']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Target maximum latency for a successful feed retrieval as defined in the provider SLA.']
  sla_uptime_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable availability of the feed over a month, expressed as a percent.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier (e.g., UTC, Europe/London) used for timestamps supplied by the provider.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the rate feed record.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record for each external FX rate feed provider integrated into the platform. Defines the provider identity (Reuters Refinitiv, Bloomberg FX, ECB reference rates, internal treasury), feed protocol (REST, FIX, SFTP), polling frequency, supported currency pairs, rate type delivered, SLA thresholds, failover priority, and connectivity credentials metadata. Governs how the platform ingests, validates, and routes rate data from each upstream source. Critical for rate feed health monitoring and provider SLA management.'
}

Table fx.currency_pair {
  currency_pair_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying a currency pair record.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification of the pair (major, minor, or exotic).']
  cross_border_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the pair may be used for cross‑border payment routing.']
  currency_pair_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the currency pair.']
  currency_pair_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the currency pair.']
  dcc_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the pair is eligible for DCC transactions.']
  decimal_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places supported for pricing the pair.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pair is the default for a given corridor or product.']
  last_rate_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent rate update for the pair.']
  market_convention varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pair is quoted directly (base/quote) or indirectly.']
  min_tradeable_unit decimal(20,8) [note: 'Smallest amount of the base currency that can be traded or converted.']
  pair_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable concatenation of base and quote currency codes.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the currency pair record was created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which settlement amounts are settled.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Settlement speed category for the pair.']
  source_feed varchar [note: 'Origin of the FX rate feed for the pair.']
  source_feed_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the rate feed source.']
  standard_pip_size decimal(10,8) [note: 'Standard pip size used for price quoting of the pair.']
  trading_end_time timestamp [note: 'UTC time when the pair stops being tradable each business day.']
  trading_start_time timestamp [note: 'UTC time when the pair becomes tradable each business day.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all supported currency pairs traded or converted on the platform. Defines the base currency, quote currency, ISO 4217 currency codes, standard pip size, minimum tradeable unit, decimal precision, market convention (direct/indirect quote), trading hours, and active status. Serves as the canonical reference for FX rate lookups, DCC eligibility checks, and cross-border routing decisions. Includes classification of major, minor, and exotic pairs relevant to the payments corridor.'
}

Table fx.dcc_transaction {
  dcc_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the DCC transaction record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: A DCC transaction converts between the cardholder\'s home currency and the transaction currency, defining a specific currency pair. Linking dcc_transaction.currency_pair_id → currency_pair.currency_pai']
  dcc_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.dcc_config. Business justification: A DCC transaction is governed by a DCC configuration; the FK captures the config used for the transaction.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: DCC fraud often involves device spoofing to manipulate currency selection or geo-location. Device fingerprinting enables detection of DCC offer manipulation, VPN-based currency arbitrage, and unauthor']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant where the DCC transaction occurred.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: DCC fee and margin calculations depend on the underlying payment product (card) used, so the product must be recorded.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: A DCC transaction is a specific payment; linking allows settlement, chargeback, and compliance reporting.']
  primary_dcc_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who performed the transaction.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: A DCC transaction applies a specific FX rate at the moment of conversion. Linking dcc_transaction.rate_id → rate.rate_id identifies the exact rate record used, enabling full audit traceability of the ']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: DCC processing requires the card scheme to apply correct margin and compliance rules.']
  acquirer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the acquiring bank for reconciliation.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer confirming authorization.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand of the card used in the transaction.']
  cardholder_home_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the cardholder\'s billing/home currency.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the transaction was initiated.']
  compliance_check_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text details of any compliance issues identified.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of regulatory compliance checks (e.g., AML, sanctions) for the transaction.']
  converted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount after applying the DCC exchange rate, expressed in the cardholder\'s home currency.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the record (e.g., gateway, processing).']
  dcc_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the DCC offer was presented to the cardholder.']
  dcc_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Flat fee charged for the DCC service, if any, expressed in the cardholder\'s currency.']
  dcc_margin_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the fixed DCC margin amount, if applicable.']
  dcc_margin_fixed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed margin amount applied when margin type is fixed.']
  dcc_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage margin applied by the issuer/acquirer on top of the base exchange rate.']
  dcc_margin_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the DCC margin is a percentage or a fixed amount.']
  dcc_offer_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder accepted the DCC offer (true) or declined (false).']
  dcc_offer_decline_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why a DCC offer was declined, if applicable.']
  dcc_transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the DCC transaction.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Technology used to capture the transaction.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the original amount to the cardholder\'s currency.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged for potential fraud.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score generated by the fraud detection platform for this transaction.']
  fx_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exchange rate was retrieved.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address of the transaction request.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for additional remarks or manual adjustments.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount in the original currency before DCC conversion.']
  original_transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction before conversion.']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds from transaction receipt to DCC offer decision.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DCC transaction record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this DCC transaction record.']
  scheme_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance of the DCC transaction with Visa/Mastercard DCC rules.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final settled amount after conversion and fees.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled in the cardholder\'s currency.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'External reference such as Acquirer Reference Number (ARN) that uniquely identifies the transaction across systems.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the transaction (e.g., purchase, refund).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every Dynamic Currency Conversion event executed at the point of sale or online checkout. Records the original transaction currency, cardholder home currency, exchange rate applied, DCC margin percentage, converted amount, cardholder acceptance or decline of DCC offer, merchant MID, terminal TID, acquirer reference number (ARN), and scheme DCC compliance flags. Supports DCC revenue reporting, scheme compliance audits (Visa/Mastercard DCC rules), and cardholder dispute resolution for currency conversion disagreements.'
}

Table fx.correspondent_bank {
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the correspondent bank record.']
  aml_kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current anti‑money‑laundering and know‑your‑customer compliance status of the bank.']
  compliance_documentation varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the stored compliance documents (e.g., AML policies, contracts).']
  contact_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address of the bank\'s primary office for correspondence.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the bank\'s relationship manager.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the bank\'s relationship manager.']
  correspondent_bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the correspondent bank.']
  correspondent_fee_fixed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat fee charged per transaction, in the bank\'s settlement currency.']
  correspondent_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variable fee expressed as a percentage of the transaction amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the correspondent bank record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure the fintech is permitted to have with the correspondent bank, expressed in US dollars.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship ended or is scheduled to end (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the correspondent relationship became effective.']
  iban_format_supported varchar [note: 'Pattern or description of IBAN structures the bank can process (e.g., country-specific length).']
  is_iban_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank can receive or send payments using IBAN format.']
  is_swift_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SWIFT messaging is supported for this bank.']
  is_tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank participates in tokenization services for digital wallets.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or risk review of the relationship.']
  nostro_account_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code of the nostro account (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  nostro_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number held by the fintech in the correspondent bank for settlement purposes.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Country or region whose financial regulations govern the correspondent relationship.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the correspondent banking relationship.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the correspondent bank based on AML, credit, and operational risk assessments.']
  settlement_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily cut‑off time (local bank time) after which transactions are processed in the next business day.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Primary mechanism used for settling transactions with the correspondent bank.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes the bank can settle for.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the correspondent bank.']

  Note: 'Master record for correspondent banking relationships maintained by the platform to facilitate cross-border payment routing. Captures the correspondent bank name, BIC/SWIFT code, IBAN format supported, nostro account details, supported currencies, supported payment corridors, settlement cut-off times, correspondent bank fees, regulatory jurisdiction, AML/KYC status, and relationship status. Serves as the SSOT for correspondent banking counterparty data used in SWIFT routing, cross-border settlement instructions, and treasury liquidity management.'
}

Table fx.nostro_account {

Business justification: Each nostro account operates under a specific fee schedule governing transaction fees, maintenance charges, and service costs. The fee_schedule column is a STRING that denormalizes what should be a FK']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Nostro accounts are balance sheet assets requiring GL account mapping for financial statement preparation, reconciliation, and regulatory capital reporting. Every nostro must map to a specific GL acco']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Nostro accounts are designated for settlement of specific scheme transactions — a Visa settlement nostro differs from a Mastercard one. Scheme-based settlement reconciliation, liquidity management per']
  account_label varchar [note: 'Human‑friendly label or nickname for the account used in treasury dashboards.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Unique account number assigned by the correspondent bank for the nostro account.']
