  regulatory_reporting_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account is subject to specific regulatory reporting obligations.']
  settlement_instructions varchar [note: 'Instructions for processing settlements using this account.']
  treasury_desk varchar [note: 'Internal treasury desk responsible for managing the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the account record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each nostro account held by the platform at correspondent banks in foreign currencies. Captures the account number, correspondent bank reference, currency denomination, account type, opening balance, current balance, minimum balance threshold, overdraft limit, account status, and the treasury desk responsible for management. Nostro accounts are the operational funding pools for cross-border payment execution. Supports real-time liquidity monitoring, pre-funding adequacy checks, and reconciliation against correspondent bank statements.'
}

Table fx.nostro_balance {
  nostro_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each nostro balance snapshot record.']
  nostro_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the nostro account to which this balance belongs.']
  available_balance decimal(20,6) [note: 'Portion of the closing balance that is not on hold and can be used for payments.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the snapshot has been reconciled against correspondent bank statements.']
  closing_balance decimal(20,6) [note: 'Balance amount at the end of the value date, after all posted movements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the balance record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  credit_movements decimal(20,6) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all credit transactions posted to the account on the value date.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the account\'s currency.']
  debit_movements decimal(20,6) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all debit transactions posted to the account on the value date.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Fixed value indicating this row represents a balance snapshot event.']
  hold_balance decimal(20,6) [note: 'Funds that are reserved or blocked and cannot be used for outgoing payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or remarks about the balance snapshot.']
  opening_balance decimal(20,6) [note: 'Balance amount at the beginning of the value date, expressed in the account currency.']
  pending_credits decimal(20,6) [note: 'Sum of credit amounts that are authorized but not yet finalised.']
  pending_debits decimal(20,6) [note: 'Sum of debit amounts that are authorized but not yet finalised.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the balance snapshot was recorded.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that produced the balance snapshot.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the balance record.']
  value_date date [note: 'The business date for which the balance snapshot is taken.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing point-in-time balance snapshots and movements on each nostro account. Records the nostro account reference, value date, opening balance, closing balance, credit movements, debit movements, pending credits, pending debits, and reconciliation status against correspondent bank statements. Enables intraday liquidity monitoring, end-of-day reconciliation, and pre-funding adequacy validation before cross-border payment execution. Distinct from nostro_account (the master) as it captures the time-series balance history.'
}

Table fx.payment_corridor {
  payment_corridor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the payment corridor record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Payment corridors are operational channels tracked by cost center for P&L attribution, transfer pricing between entities, and profitability analysis by geography/product. Enables management reporting ']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule applicable to this corridor.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each payment corridor must comply with particular regulatory obligations; the FK ties corridor definitions to the governing obligation for audit and risk checks.']
  routing_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.routing_table. Business justification: Payment corridors are operationalized through network routing tables that define which rails process corridor transactions. Corridor-to-routing-table mapping is essential for cross-border payment proc']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Payment corridors operate over specific scheme rails (Visa, Mastercard, SWIFT). Corridor compliance validation, interchange fee application, and scheme-mandated transaction limits require knowing whic']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the corridor record was first created.']
  currency_conversion_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether automatic currency conversion is permitted for this corridor.']
  dcc_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if DCC (Dynamic Currency Conversion) is offered for transactions in this corridor.']
  destination_bank_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of BIC codes of banks in the destination country that can receive payments.']
  destination_country_iso varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the destination country for the corridor.']
  destination_currency_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes that can be received in this corridor.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the corridor becomes active and usable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the corridor is retired or no longer available (null if indefinite).']
  estimated_settlement_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical time in minutes from transaction initiation to settlement for this corridor.']
  exchange_rate_source varchar [note: 'Source of FX rates used for conversion in this corridor.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed per transaction in the corridor, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary amount required per transaction in the corridor.']
  ofac_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OFAC or other sanctions screening must be performed for transactions in this corridor.']
  payment_corridor_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the corridor, including any special handling notes.']
  payment_corridor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corridor.']
  payment_corridor_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the corridor based on usage or risk profile.']
  regulatory_restrictions varchar [note: 'Textual description of any regulatory or compliance constraints that apply to the corridor (e.g., AML limits, country sanctions).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the corridor based on AML, sanctions, and fraud exposure.']
  settlement_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for settlement processing (e.g., netting, gross, timing).']
  source_bank_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of BIC codes of banks in the source country that can originate payments.']
  source_currency_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes that can be originated in this corridor.']
  supported_payment_rails varchar [note: 'Payment rail(s) available for routing transactions through this corridor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the corridor record.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record defining a supported cross-border payment corridor between a source country and destination country. Captures the source country ISO code, destination country ISO code, supported currencies, available payment rails (SWIFT, local ACH, RTP, SEPA), regulatory restrictions, OFAC/sanctions screening requirements, estimated settlement time, corridor status, and applicable fee schedule reference. Serves as the routing decision table for cross-border payment execution and corridor availability checks.'
}

Table fx.exposure {
  exposure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each FX exposure snapshot record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: FX exposure is measured per currency pair; linking normalizes the currency reference.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: FX exposures are hedged positions requiring GL classification for balance sheet presentation (derivative assets/liabilities) and hedge accounting documentation under IAS 39/IFRS 9. Links exposure to i']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: FX exposure is tracked per payment scheme to manage concentration risk — Visa-originated cross-border exposure vs Mastercard exposure requires separate risk limits. Scheme-level FX exposure reporting ']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exposure record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the exposure.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the exposure is a long (positive) or short (negative) position.']
  exposure_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the exposure amount, such as pending settlements, nostro balances, forward contracts, etc.']
  exposure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exposure record.']
  gross_notional_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total notional amount of the FX position before any hedging offsets.']
  hedging_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the exposure is fully hedged, partially hedged, or unhedged.']
  limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable exposure amount defined by the risk limit.']
  limit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk limit governing this exposure.']
  limit_utilization_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute amount of the risk limit currently utilized by this exposure.']
  net_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Exposure amount after applying hedging instruments, expressed in the reporting currency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the exposure snapshot.']
  risk_limit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the assigned risk limit that is currently utilized by this exposure.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exposure snapshot was captured.']
  unhedged_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the exposure that remains without hedge coverage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exposure record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the exposure is expected to settle or be realized.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the platform\'s open FX exposure position by currency at a point in time. Records the currency, exposure direction (long/short), gross notional exposure, net exposure after hedges, unhedged exposure, value date, exposure source (pending settlements, nostro balances, forward contracts), and risk limit utilization percentage. Enables treasury to monitor real-time FX risk positions, trigger hedging actions when exposure breaches thresholds, and report FX risk to the board and regulators.'
}

Table fx.cross_border_payment {
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cross-border payment record.']
  beneficiary_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party receiving the funds.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Every cross-border payment involves a specific currency pair (source currency → destination currency). Linking cross_border_payment.currency_pair_id → currency_pair.currency_pair_id connects the payme']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: Cross-border payments are high-risk for device-based fraud (VPN spoofing, geo-location mismatch, device takeover). Device fingerprinting enables detection of suspicious cross-border payment originatio']

Linking cros']

  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: Every cross-border payment applies a specific FX rate for currency conversion. Linking cross_border_payment.rate_id → rate.rate_id provides a precise audit trail to the rate record used, including its']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Enables reconciliation of cross‑border payments with the original gateway request for audit and settlement reporting.']
  routing_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.routing_table. Business justification: Cross-border payments are routed via network routing tables that determine the processing rail. Payment routing audit trails, dispute resolution, and regulatory reporting require tracing which routing']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Regulatory reporting of cross‑border payments must capture the card scheme used for compliance (e.g., PSD2, SWIFT reporting).']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement instruction record governing fund transfer.']

  cross_border_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cross-border payment.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the beneficiary\'s country.']
  destination_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the amount received by the beneficiary.']

  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the FX rate (e.g., spot, forward).']
  exchange_rate_validity_end timestamp [note: 'End of the time window during which the FX rate is valid.']
  exchange_rate_validity_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the time window during which the FX rate is valid.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees charged for processing the cross‑border payment.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the fee amount.']
  fx_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert the original amount to the destination currency.']
  fx_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate was sourced or locked for the transaction.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the payment status.']
  ofac_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions screening.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount in the origin currency before conversion.']
  payment_purpose_code varchar [note: 'ISO 20022 purpose code describing the reason for the payment.']
  payment_rail varchar [note: 'Network or scheme used to route the cross‑border payment.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the originating institution or platform for tracking the payment.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction must be reported to regulators.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the payment record represents a reversal of a prior transaction.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment is settled between banks.']
  swift_uetr varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier assigned by SWIFT for tracking the payment.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of original amount and any applicable fees, expressed in the origin currency.']
  total_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the total amount field.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether funds are being sent (debit) or received (credit).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the converted amount becomes effective for accounting.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for every cross-border payment instruction initiated through the platform. Captures the payment reference, originating country, destination country, sender identity, beneficiary identity, beneficiary bank BIC, beneficiary IBAN/account number, payment amount, origination currency, destination currency, FX rate applied, converted amount, payment rail selected (SWIFT, SEPA, local ACH), SWIFT UETR, payment purpose code, regulatory reporting flags, OFAC screening status, and payment lifecycle status. The SSOT for cross-border payment execution within the FX domain.'
}

  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: DCC configurations are scheme-specific — Visa DCC rules differ from Mastercard\'s. The plain attribute scheme_compliance is a denormalized reference to the governing scheme. Scheme-specific DCC setup, ']
  cardholder_disclosure_text_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the legal disclosure text shown to cardholders for DCC offers.']

  config_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DCC configuration (e.g., standard, premium, custom).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DCC configuration record was first created.']
  dcc_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DCC configuration.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the DCC configuration becomes active.']

  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code that classifies the merchant\'s business type.']
  merchant_mid varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant to which this DCC configuration applies.']

  offer_presentation_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the DCC offer is presented to the cardholder.']
  opt_in_default boolean [note: 'Default opt‑in (true) or opt‑out (false) setting applied when a merchant enrolls in DCC.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the DCC configuration record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version used for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record governing DCC program parameters for each merchant or merchant category enrolled in the DCC service. Captures the merchant MID, enrolled currency pairs, DCC margin percentage, minimum transaction amount for DCC offer, DCC offer presentation method (terminal, online, IVR), scheme compliance settings (Visa DCC, Mastercard Currency Conversion), cardholder disclosure text reference, opt-in/opt-out default, and configuration effective dates. Drives the DCC eligibility and rate calculation logic at transaction time.'
}

Table fx.fx_fee_schedule {
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each FX fee schedule record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: An FX fee schedule is defined for a specific currency pair. The applicable_currency_pair column is a STRING that denormalizes what should be a FK reference to the currency_pair master. Replacing it wi']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Fee schedules are subject to regulatory frameworks; linking to the obligation ensures fee calculations respect compliance rules.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: FX fee schedules for cross-border transactions are scheme-specific — Visa and Mastercard have distinct cross-border fee structures. Scheme-level FX fee reconciliation, interchange reporting, and compl']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the fee – flat amount, percentage of transaction amount, or tiered structure.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that govern the fee schedule, e.g., PSD2, FCA, or local AML rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of customers for which the fee schedule is valid.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule becomes active.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule ceases to be active; null for open‑ended schedules.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Core fee value used when calculation_method is flat; for percentage or tiered methods this stores the base rate or tier reference.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the fee is expressed.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of FX service the fee applies to, such as Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) or SWIFT wire.']
  fee_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number used for change management and audit.']
  fx_fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context, business rules, or notes about the fee schedule.']
  fx_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee schedule.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule is the default for its fee_type and segment.']
  maximum_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Highest fee that can be charged under this schedule, expressed in fee_currency.']
  minimum_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lowest fee that can be charged under this schedule, expressed in fee_currency.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the fee schedule across systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fee schedule used for reporting and UI display.']
  tier_details varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded or delimited representation of tier thresholds and rates when calculation_method is tiered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee schedule record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the fee structures applied to FX and cross-border payment services offered by the platform. Captures the fee schedule name, applicable service type (DCC, cross-border transfer, currency conversion, SWIFT wire), fee calculation method (flat, percentage, tiered), fee currency, minimum fee, maximum fee, applicable currency pairs or corridors, customer segment applicability, effective date, and expiry date. Serves as the pricing reference for FX revenue calculation and merchant/cardholder fee disclosure.'
}

Table fx.rate_margin_config {

Business justification: FX margin configurations are often scoped to specific payment corridors — different corridors carry different risk profiles and competitive dynamics that drive margin decisions. Linking rate_margin_co']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: DCC margin configurations are scheme-specific — Visa and Mastercard impose different DCC margin caps and disclosure requirements. Scheme-specific margin compliance audits and regulatory reporting requ']

  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the margin configuration becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the margin configuration expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']

  margin_value_bps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Margin expressed in basis points when margin_type is fixed_spread.']

  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the margin is settled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|CAD|... — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change management.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record defining the FX margin (spread) applied on top of the interbank mid-market rate for each customer segment, product type, or payment corridor. Captures the margin type (fixed spread, percentage markup, tiered by volume), base rate source, margin basis points, applicable currency pair or corridor, customer tier, product type, effective date, and approval authority. Governs how the platform marks up interbank rates to generate FX revenue while remaining competitive and compliant with PSD2 transparency requirements.'
}

Business justification: Beneficiaries receiving cross-border payments may be group legal entities requiring intercompany elimination, transfer pricing documentation, and consolidation tracking. Links beneficiary to legal ent']

Table fx.corridor_bank_assignment {
  corridor_bank_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this corridor-bank assignment record']
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the correspondent bank authorized for this corridor']
  payment_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the payment corridor being configured with correspondent bank options']
  assigned_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the treasury or operations user who created or last approved this corridor-bank assignment']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this corridor-bank assignment, managed by treasury operations team']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corridor-bank assignment was first created in the system']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when this correspondent bank became authorized and active for routing payments through this corridor']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when this correspondent bank authorization expires or was terminated for this corridor (null if currently active)']
  fee_override decimal(12,2) [note: 'Negotiated fee amount specific to this corridor-bank combination, overriding the correspondent bank\'s standard fee schedule when populated']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this correspondent bank is designated as the primary/preferred routing option for this corridor (typically only one primary per corridor)']
  max_daily_volume_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum daily transaction volume in USD that can be routed through this correspondent bank for this specific corridor, used for liquidity and risk management']
  payment_corridors varchar [note: 'Geographic or network corridors (e.g., US‑EU, APAC‑LATAM) the bank supports for cross‑border payments. [Moved from correspondent_bank: This STRING attribute in correspondent_bank lists corridors the bank supports, but the actual operational assignment with priority, dates, and fees belongs in the association. The attribute should be removed as it creates denormalization and will be replaced by querying the corridor_bank_assignment association.]']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for routing decisions when multiple correspondent banks serve the same corridor (1 = highest priority). Used by payment routing engine to select optimal correspondent bank.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Settlement mechanism used for this specific corridor-bank pairing, which may differ from the correspondent bank\'s default settlement method based on corridor requirements']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this assignment record']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment of correspondent banks to payment corridors for cross-border payment routing. It captures the treasury and operations team\'s active management of which correspondent banks are authorized and prioritized for each corridor. Each record links one payment corridor to one correspondent bank with routing priority, settlement terms, fee overrides, and relationship lifecycle dates that exist only in the context of this specific corridor-bank pairing.. Existence Justification: In cross-border payments operations, payment corridors (e.g., US-to-EU, APAC-to-LATAM) require multiple correspondent banks for redundancy, cost optimization, and regulatory compliance, while each correspondent bank serves multiple corridors based on their geographic reach and currency capabilities. Treasury and operations teams actively manage these assignments with routing priorities, corridor-specific settlement terms, fee negotiations, and lifecycle management (effective dates, suspension, termination). This is not a derived correlation but an operational business process where relationship managers configure and maintain corridor-bank pairings as a core routing decision table.'
}

Table fx.feed_coverage {
  feed_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each feed-pair coverage configuration record']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the currency pair reference']
  rate_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the rate feed provider configuration']
  confidence_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Weighting factor (0.0000 to 1.0000) representing the confidence/quality of rates from this feed for this specific pair. Used in weighted rate aggregation algorithms when multiple feeds provide rates for the same pair.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this feed-pair coverage configuration (active, suspended, deprecated, testing)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date-time when this feed coverage configuration record was first created']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this feed-pair coverage configuration becomes active for rate ingestion and routing decisions']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this feed-pair coverage is no longer active; null indicates indefinite coverage']
  is_primary_source boolean [note: 'True if this feed is designated as the primary/preferred source for this currency pair. Used in rate aggregation and failover logic when multiple feeds cover the same pair.']
  last_rate_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful rate retrieval for this specific feed-pair combination. Used for monitoring feed health at the pair level.']
  polling_frequency_seconds varchar [note: 'Polling frequency in seconds for this specific feed-pair combination, allowing different refresh rates for different pairs from the same feed (e.g., major pairs polled more frequently than exotics)']
  precision_override varchar [note: 'Decimal precision override specific to this feed-pair combination, overriding the currency_pair default when this feed is the source. Allows feed-specific precision handling.']
  supported_currency_pairs varchar [note: 'List of currency pairs (e.g., EUR/USD, GBP/JPY) that the feed supplies. [Moved from rate_feed: This STRING field in rate_feed is a denormalized list representation of the M:N relationship. The proper normalized model captures each feed-pair coverage as a separate record in the association, eliminating the need for this comma-separated list. The association provides granular control over which pairs each feed covers with pair-specific configuration.]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date-time of the most recent modification to this feed coverage configuration']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational coverage configuration between rate_feed and currency_pair. It captures which external FX rate feed providers supply rates for which currency pairs, along with feed-specific precision overrides, polling frequency adjustments, effective date ranges, primary source designation, and confidence weighting used for rate aggregation and failover logic. Each record links one rate_feed to one currency_pair with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific feed-pair subscription.. Existence Justification: In FX payment platforms, rate feed providers each cover different subsets of currency pairs with varying quality and latency characteristics. A single feed (e.g., Reuters) supplies rates for multiple pairs (EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/JPY), and a single currency pair (e.g., EUR/USD) is sourced from multiple feeds for redundancy, rate aggregation, and failover. The platform actively manages this feed-pair coverage configuration with pair-specific polling frequencies, precision overrides, primary source designation, and confidence weights used in rate aggregation algorithms.'
}

Linking gl_account to legal_']

  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'True if the account is included in the formal budgeting process.']
  is_consolidation boolean [note: 'True if the account participates in legal entity consolidation reporting.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for intercompany transactions.']
  normal_balance varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the profit center associated with the account.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for consolidated reporting of this account.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the business segment (e.g., retail, corporate) linked to the account.']
  sox_control_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates the SOX compliance requirement for the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the GL account record.']

  Note: 'Chart of accounts master record defining every general ledger account used across the enterprise. Captures account number, account type (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense), normal balance direction, account hierarchy level, parent account reference, financial statement mapping (P&L vs balance sheet), currency, consolidation flag, intercompany flag, and SOX control classification. This is the SSOT for all GL account definitions in Oracle Financials and the authoritative reference for all journal entry postings.'
}

  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each cost center record.']

  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Every cost center belongs to a specific legal entity for financial reporting, intercompany settlement, and regulatory compliance. In a multi-entity payments fintech, cost centers must be attributed to']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate expenses to the cost center.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total budget allocated to this cost center when part of a shared pool.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center budget was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the cost center budget.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned monetary budget allocated to the cost center for the defined period.']
  budget_period varchar [note: 'Frequency of the budget allocation.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the budget amount applies.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the cost center with internal policies and external regulations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used in accounting and reporting systems.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the cost center.']
  cost_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of cost centers for consolidated reporting.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost center.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center for reporting and allocation purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the cost center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the budget.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level applied to the cost center data for governance.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department responsible for the cost center.']
  division varchar [note: 'Division within the business unit to which the cost center belongs.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective for budgeting and reporting.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center in external ERP or financial systems.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'GL account number associated with the cost center for posting transactions.']

  is_shared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center is shared across multiple business units.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center was last reviewed for budgeting or compliance.']

  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manager responsible for the cost center.']
  owning_business_unit varchar [note: 'Top‑level business unit that owns the cost center.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the cost center based on financial exposure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the cost center record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the cost center record.']

  Note: 'Organizational cost center master record representing a financial responsibility unit within the payments fintech enterprise. Captures cost center code, name, owning business unit, division, department, manager, active status, budget period, currency, and allocation basis. Used for expense allocation, P&L reporting by business unit, and management accounting. Aligns with Oracle Financials cost center segment in the accounting flexfield.'
}

  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date on which the entity was legally formed.']

  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal risk assessment category for the entity.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the entity\'s address.']
  swift_bic varchar [note: 'International bank identifier used for cross‑border payments.']
  tax_id_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax identification number format.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the entity (e.g., EIN, VAT number).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the legal entity record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the legal entity.']

  Note: 'Legal entity master record representing each incorporated company, subsidiary, or branch within the payments fintech corporate group. Captures legal entity name, registration number, jurisdiction of incorporation, tax identification number, functional currency, consolidation group, SWIFT BIC, regulatory license references, and OFAC/sanctions screening status. Serves as the top-level organizational anchor for all financial reporting and intercompany accounting.'
}

Table ledger.accounting_period {
  accounting_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the accounting period record.']
  primary_accounting_config_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ledger to which this accounting period belongs.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the period was closed for posting.']
  closing_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total ledger balance recorded at the moment the period was closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accounting period record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date on which the accounting period ends.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year to which the period belongs, e.g., 2023.']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period is used for post‑close adjustments rather than regular transaction posting.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period has been archived for historical reference and is read‑only.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if this period is the active period for posting at the current point in time.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the period was opened for posting.']
  period_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the period\'s primary purpose, e.g., financial reporting, tax reporting, or internal reporting.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing code that uniquely identifies the period within the fiscal calendar, often used in journal entries and reporting.']
  period_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description providing additional context about the period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the period, e.g., "FY2023 Q1" or "2023‑01".']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the period within its fiscal year (1‑12 for months, 1‑4 for quarters).']
  period_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric order of the period within the fiscal year, useful for sorting (e.g., 1 for Jan, 2 for Feb).']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the period: open (accepting postings), closed (postings stopped), locked (no changes allowed), or pending (not yet active).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period granularity: month, quarter, year, or adjustment.']
  period_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the period record to support re‑open/re‑close scenarios and audit trails.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date on which the accounting period begins.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last modified the accounting period record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the accounting period record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the accounting period record.']

  Note: 'Financial accounting period master defining the fiscal calendar used for GL posting, period-end close, and financial reporting. Captures period name, fiscal year, period number, period type (month, quarter, year), open/closed status, period open date, period close date, adjustment period flag, and the ledger it belongs to. Controls which periods accept journal entry postings and drives the financial period close workflow.'
}

Table ledger.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the journal entry record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Journal entries are posted to a specific accounting period; FK supports period‑level queries.']
  config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.ledger_config. Business justification: journal_entry has ledger_code (STRING) referencing the ledger it was posted to. Normalizing this to ledger_config_id FK enables proper ledger validation, posting rule enforcement, and multi-ledger acc']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Journal entries are posted to a specific legal entity\'s books. In a multi-entity payments fintech spanning 200+ countries, every journal entry must be attributed to the correct legal entity for statut']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Required for FX gain/loss reconciliation report linking each journal entry to the exact FX rate used in the conversion.']
  reversal_of_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original journal entry that this entry reverses, if applicable.']
  accounting_date date [note: 'The fiscal date to which the entry is posted for financial reporting.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for the adjustment.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cost center charged by the entry.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department associated with the entry.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the journal entry, often used in accounting reports.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the journal entry source such as payment processing, settlement, manual adjustment, intercompany, or recurring.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when the entry involves a foreign currency.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Date for which the exchange rate is applicable.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year of the entry.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'True if the entry records an intercompany transaction.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the entry represents an accounting adjustment.']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or nature of the journal entry.']
  journal_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and adjustments, used for reporting.']
  period varchar [note: 'Accounting period identifier (e.g., 2023‑04) to which the entry belongs.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entry was posted to the general ledger.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entry is a reversal of a prior entry.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Analytical segment identifier for reporting (e.g., product line, geography).']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module within the source system that produced the entry.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the journal entry.']
  sox_control_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SOX control that governs this entry.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component captured in the entry, if applicable.']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate credit amount for the journal entry, expressed in the entry currency.']
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate debit amount for the journal entry, expressed in the entry currency.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the underlying business event occurred (e.g., payment authorization).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the journal entry record.']

  Note: 'General ledger journal entry header record representing a balanced accounting transaction posted to the GL. Captures journal entry number, journal source (payment processing, settlement, manual, intercompany, recurring), journal category, accounting date, currency, total debit amount, total credit amount, posting status, reversal flag, reversal period, preparer, approver, SOX control reference, and description. The primary transactional record for all financial activity in Oracle Financials GL module.'
}

Table ledger.journal_line {
  journal_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each line item within a general ledger journal entry.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Journal lines inherit the period of their parent entry; explicit FK improves traceability.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: journal_line has cost_center_code (STRING) for cost allocation. Normalizing to cost_center_id FK enables proper cost center validation, hierarchical rollup, and budget vs. actual reporting. The cost_c']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.gl_account']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: journal_line has legal_entity_code (STRING) for entity attribution at the line level. Normalizing to legal_entity_id FK enables proper entity validation and supports intercompany journal line processi']
  primary_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent journal entry to which this line belongs.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Needed for detailed audit of each journal line recording foreign exchange amounts, supporting regulatory FX reporting.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the adjustment line (e.g., correction, reversal, write‑off).']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business unit owning the transaction for internal reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the journal line was initially created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount credited on this line, expressed in the functional currency of the entity.']
  debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount debited on this line, expressed in the functional currency of the entity.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the functional currency used for accounting.']
  fx_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the transaction amount from transaction currency to functional currency for this line.']
  fx_rate_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the foreign‑exchange rate used for conversion.']
  fx_rate_source varchar [note: 'Name of the provider or system that supplied the foreign‑exchange rate.']
  intercompany_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related inter‑company entity for intercompany journal entries.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the line represents an adjustment (correction, reversal, write‑off) rather than a regular transaction.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Narrative text describing the purpose of the line item.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the line within the journal entry.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status indicating whether the line is posted, pending, reversed, or encountered an error.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the line is posted to the general ledger.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Code of the project or initiative associated with the line for cost allocation.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier (such as ARN, MID, or payment token) used to reconcile this line with source systems.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Code used to group lines for segment‑level financial reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that originated the journal line.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary tax amount associated with the line, expressed in functional currency.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax rule or jurisdiction applicable to this line.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or automated process that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the journal line.']

  Note: 'Individual debit or credit line within a GL journal entry. Captures line number, GL account, cost center, legal entity, project, intercompany entity, debit amount, credit amount, functional currency amount, transaction currency amount, FX rate, line description, tax code, and reconciliation reference. Each journal entry must have balanced lines (total debits = total credits). Supports drill-through from financial statements to source transactions.'
}

  gl_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each GL balance snapshot.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: gl_balance has fiscal_period (INT) and fiscal_year (INT) but no accounting_period_id FK. GL balances are computed per accounting period. Linking to accounting_period enables period-end close validatio']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center associated with the balance for internal reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the GL account for which the balance is recorded.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: GL balances are entity-specific in a multi-entity payments fintech. Each balance record must be attributed to a legal entity for consolidated financial reporting, intercompany elimination, and statuto']
  primary_gl_config_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ledger (e.g., primary ledger, sub‑ledger) to which the balance belongs.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the balance (e.g., posted after close, provisional during interim).']
  balance_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the balance is in functional currency or reporting currency.']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance after applying period debits and credits; used for financial statements.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Rate applied to convert functional currency balance to reporting currency.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the balance aggregates multiple legal entities; otherwise false.']
  last_reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the balance was last validated against source ledgers.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance amount carried forward from the prior period.']
  period_credits decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate credit amount posted to the account in the period.']
  period_debits decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate debit amount posted to the account in the period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent reconciliation process.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the balance snapshot was initially persisted.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the balance record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform) that produced the record.']
  translated_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Closing balance expressed in the reporting currency after applying exchange rate.']

  Note: 'Period-end GL account balance record capturing the opening balance, period activity (debits and credits), and closing balance for each GL account per accounting period per ledger. Supports financial statement generation, variance analysis, and period-over-period comparisons. Captures account, period, ledger, currency type (functional/reporting), beginning balance, period debit, period credit, ending balance, and translated balance for multi-currency reporting.'
}

  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the ledger ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']

  is_consolidation_ledger boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is used for consolidation reporting.']
  is_multi_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates if the ledger supports multiple functional currencies.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the ledger configuration.']

  ledger_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ledger.']
  ledger_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ledger.']
  ledger_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or department responsible for the ledger.']
  ledger_timezone varchar [note: 'Timezone in which ledger processing timestamps are recorded.']

  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of accounting periods used by the ledger.']

  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for external reporting from this ledger.']

  tax_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the ledger is used for tax reporting purposes.']
  translation_method varchar [note: 'Method applied to translate foreign currency balances.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ledger configuration.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the ledger configuration for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the ledger configuration.']

  Note: 'Ledger configuration master defining each accounting ledger (primary, secondary, reporting) within Oracle Financials. Captures ledger name, ledger type, chart of accounts, accounting calendar, functional currency, subledger accounting method, consolidation ledger flag, reporting currency, translation method, and retained earnings account. A payments fintech enterprise typically maintains multiple ledgers for different legal entities and regulatory reporting jurisdictions.'
}

Table ledger.subledger_entry {
  subledger_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the subledger entry record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Subledger entries are posted within specific accounting periods. Linking subledger_entry to accounting_period enables period-end close validation, ensures subledger entries are posted to open periods,']
  capture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.capture. Business justification: Capture events trigger subledger postings for settlement receivables and interchange income. Direct link from subledger_entry to capture enables capture-level subledger reconciliation — a named operat']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_account. Business justification: Subledger entries in card accounting are posted against specific cardholder accounts (not just profiles) for account-level balance reconciliation, statement generation, and credit limit utilization tr']
  counterparty_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.party. Business justification: Subledger entries track transactional counterparties for AML screening, sanctions checks, audit trail requirements, and party-level risk aggregation in payments fintech transaction processing and comp']
  fx_conversion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.fx_conversion. Business justification: FX conversion events generate subledger entries for realized/unrealized FX gains and losses. Payments fintech multi-currency operations require direct traceability from FX subledger postings to conver']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: subledger_entry has gl_account_code (STRING) referencing the GL account for posting. Normalizing to gl_account_id FK enables proper GL account validation, account type enforcement, and subledger-to-GL']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Subledger entries are summarized and transferred to the general ledger via journal entries. Linking subledger_entry to journal_entry enables subledger-to-GL reconciliation, audit trail tracing from op']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Subledger entries are atomic GL postings for individual payment events. Payments fintech subledger reconciliation operates at payment_txn level (MDR income, interchange posting, settlement entries). D']
  primary_subledger_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the transaction.']
  refund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.refund. Business justification: Refunds generate distinct subledger entries (debit revenue, credit refund payable) that must be traceable to the originating refund event. Payments fintech subledger reconciliation teams require this ']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement run associated with this entry.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that includes this entry.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating transaction that generated this subledger entry.']
  accounting_date date [note: 'Date on which the entry is accounted in the ledger, per accounting period.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the adjustment, if applicable.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit events for the entry.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates if the entry triggered any compliance checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subledger entry record was created in the data lake.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Credit monetary amount for the entry.']
  debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Debit monetary amount for the entry.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the subledger entry, often sequential per source system.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the underlying business event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of accounting event represented by this subledger entry.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied if transaction involves currency conversion.']
  gl_derivation_rule varchar [note: 'Rule applied to derive the GL account from transaction attributes.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry is an adjustment (true) or original transaction (false).']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry has been reconciled with the GL.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence order of the subledger entry within its parent transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or remarks.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original transaction amount before any conversion.']
  original_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the original amount before conversion.']
  payment_instrument_token varchar [note: 'Token representing the payment instrument used, per tokenization standards.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of posting this entry to the General Ledger.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric quantity associated with the entry, e.g., number of units or amount of service.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the entry was reconciled.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned to the transaction at time of entry.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module within the source system (e.g., Authorization Engine, Settlement Engine).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the subledger entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subledger entry record.']

  Note: 'Subledger accounting entry record generated by operational subsystems (payment gateway, settlement engine, merchant billing, dispute management) before posting to the GL. Captures subledger application (AP, AR, FA, payment processing), event type, event date, accounting date, transaction reference, GL account derivation rule applied, debit/credit amounts, currency, and transfer-to-GL status. Provides the audit trail linking operational transactions to GL postings per SOX requirements.'
}

Table ledger.revenue_recognition_schedule {
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the revenue recognition schedule record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: revenue_recognition_schedule has accounting_period (STRING) referencing the period for recognition. Normalizing to accounting_period_id FK enables proper period validation, period-end close integratio']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules for merchant acquiring contracts (multi-year agreements, deferred setup fees) must reference the merchant for ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance reporting. Enables merchant-level']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: IFRS 15 revenue recognition requires tracking which payment product generated deferred revenue. Revenue schedules must link to products for contract liability reporting, performance obligation trackin']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of total price allocated to this schedule.']
  business_identifier varchar [note: 'External reference number or code for the revenue schedule as used by finance.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'Category of revenue type for the schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition starts according to the contract.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition ends; null if indefinite.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric interval for the frequency (e.g., every 2 months).']
  is_partial_recognition_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if partial recognition is permitted before full amount.']
  is_prorated boolean [note: 'Indicates if the schedule uses prorated amounts.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revenue schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the schedule.']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date for the final recognition event.']
  recognition_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which revenue is recognized.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue.']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the first recognition event.']
  recognized_to_date_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount recognized to date.']
  total_transaction_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contract transaction price allocated to this performance obligation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule.']

  Note: 'Revenue recognition schedule master defining the rules and timeline for recognizing deferred revenue in compliance with ASC 606 / IFRS 15. Captures contract reference, performance obligation description, total transaction price, allocated amount, recognition method (point-in-time vs over-time), recognition start date, recognition end date, recognition frequency, and recognized-to-date amount. Critical for payments fintech revenue streams including MDR, SaaS platform fees, and BNPL origination fees.'
}

Table ledger.revenue_recognition_event {
  revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue recognition event.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: revenue_recognition_event has accounting_period (STRING) referencing the period in which revenue was recognized. Normalizing to accounting_period_id FK enables period-level revenue reporting, period-e']
  capture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.capture. Business justification: Capture is the primary revenue recognition trigger in card payments under ASC 606 (performance obligation satisfied at capture). Direct link from revenue_recognition_event to capture enables capture-t']
  gl_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GL journal entry created for this revenue recognition.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Revenue recognition events are triggered by payment milestones. Direct link to payment_txn enables payment-level recognition audit trail for ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance reporting, eliminating the join ']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue schedule to which this recognition event belongs.']
  reversal_event_revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [note: 'If this event reverses a prior recognition, reference to that event.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction linked to this revenue event.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the adjustment, if applicable.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the revenue recognition event.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User or system that approved the revenue recognition.']
  audit_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment for audit or review purposes.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates any compliance action required for this event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue recognition event record was created.']
  cumulative_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount recognized to date for the schedule, including this event.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied if the revenue is recorded in a foreign currency.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event is an adjustment to a prior recognition.']
  is_foreign_currency boolean [note: 'True if the recognized amount was originally in a foreign currency.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the event was entered manually rather than automatically.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the underlying transaction before conversion.']
  posted_date date [note: 'Date the revenue recognition entry was posted to the accounting system.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the revenue recognition event.']
  recognition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue was recognized.']
  recognition_trigger varchar [note: 'Mechanism that caused the revenue to be recognized (time‑based, milestone, or usage).']
  recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount recognized in this event.']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for regulatory reporting of the revenue event.']
  remaining_deferred_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Deferred revenue remaining after this recognition event.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for reversing the revenue recognition.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the revenue was settled in the ledger.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating record in the source system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that generated the revenue recognition event (e.g., ERP, Data Warehouse).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the recognized revenue.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Standard tax code applied to the revenue.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue recognition event record.']

  Note: 'Individual revenue recognition event record capturing each instance of revenue being recognized against a schedule. Captures schedule reference, recognition date, accounting period, recognized amount, cumulative recognized amount, remaining deferred amount, GL journal entry reference, recognition trigger (time-based, milestone, usage), and recognition status. Provides the transactional audit trail for ASC 606 compliance and SOX revenue controls.'
}

Table ledger.account_reconciliation {

  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Account reconciliations are entity-specific in a multi-entity payments fintech. Each reconciliation must be attributed to a legal entity for statutory reporting, audit purposes, and SOX compliance. No']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.transaction_batch. Business justification: GL account reconciliation in payments fintech is performed against settlement batches — reconciling GL balances to batch settlement amounts. Direct link enables batch-level reconciliation workflow, a ']
  account_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation record.']

  aging_days varchar [note: 'Number of days unresolved reconciling items have been outstanding.']

  closed_reconciling_items_count varchar [note: 'Count of reconciling items that have been resolved.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing the reconciliation (e.g., SOX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to translate foreign‑currency balances to the functional currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric period within the fiscal year (e.g., month number).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the reconciliation belongs.']
  gl_balance_book decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance of the GL account as recorded in the books for the period.']
  is_auto_reconciled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the reconciliation was performed automatically by the system.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GL account is part of a consolidated reporting hierarchy.']
  last_reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful reconciliation run for this account/period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations entered by the preparer or reviewer.']
  open_reconciling_items_count varchar [note: 'Count of reconciling items that remain unresolved.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reconciliation period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reconciliation period.']

  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier or reference number for the reconciliation run.']
  reconciling_items_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all reconciling items identified for the period.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who reviewed the reconciliation.']

  total_reconciling_items_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of monetary values of all reconciling items for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between book balance and adjusted balance.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the variance (e.g., timing difference, error).']

  Note: 'GL account reconciliation record capturing the formal balance reconciliation performed for each GL account at period end. Captures account, accounting period, GL balance per books, reconciling items, adjusted balance, supporting documentation reference, preparer, reviewer, approval status, and sign-off date. Mandatory SOX control for all balance sheet accounts. Tracks open reconciling items and aging of unresolved differences.'
}

}

Table ledger.financial_statement {
  financial_statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial statement record. _canonical_skip_reason: role inferred as OTHER, no minimum categories required.']
  config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.ledger_config. Business justification: Financial statements are produced from a specific ledger configuration (primary ledger for statutory, reporting ledger for management accounts). Linking financial_statement to ledger_config enables tr']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity for which the statement is prepared.']
  cash_flow_from_financing decimal(20,2) [note: 'Cash flow from financing activities.']
  cash_flow_from_investing decimal(20,2) [note: 'Cash flow from investing activities.']
  cash_flow_from_operations decimal(20,2) [note: 'Cash flow from operating activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the statement record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code of the statement amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|... — promote to reference product]']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the statement record.']
  equity decimal(20,2) [note: 'Equity (shareholder\'s equity) reported.']
  expense decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total expense reported (for income statement).']
  external_audit_signoff boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an external auditor has signed off the statement.']
  filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for regulatory filing (e.g., SEC accession number).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1-4) of the statement.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year associated with the statement.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates if the statement is consolidated across multiple entities.']
  net_income decimal(20,2) [note: 'Net income (profit) reported.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes or comments about the statement.']
  preparation_status varchar [note: 'Current preparation status of the statement.']
  reporting_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the reporting entity.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the reporting period covered by the statement.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period covered by the statement.']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting standard used for the statement.']
  restatement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the statement is a restatement of a prior filing.']
  revenue decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total revenue reported (for income statement).']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Review status after internal audit.']
  statement_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial statement (e.g., income statement, balance sheet, cash flow, statement of equity).']
  total_assets decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total assets reported in the statement.']
  total_liabilities decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total liabilities reported in the statement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the statement record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the statement (incremented on revisions).']

  Note: 'Published financial statement record representing a formal financial report (income statement, balance sheet, cash flow statement, statement of equity) for a legal entity or consolidated group for a reporting period. Captures statement type, reporting entity, reporting period, reporting standard (GAAP/IFRS), currency, preparation status, review status, external audit sign-off, filing reference (SEC, regulatory), and restatement flag. The authoritative record of financial reporting outputs.'
}
